chinese: 他比我高三届 (Tā bǐ wǒ gāo sān jiè) / 他大我三岁 (Tā dà wǒ sān suì)

japanese: 彼（かれ）は３（みっ）つ年上（としうえ）だった。／３（さん）学年（がくねん）先輩（せんぱい）だった。
primary: He was three years above me
example:
A: You know Dave?
B: Yeah, he was three years above me in high school.
A: Did you guys hang out?
B: Not really, since he was three years above me.
A: He seems older.
B: He is, he was three years above me in college.

spanish: Es tres años menor que yo / Iba tres cursos por detrás
vietnamese: Cô ấy kém tôi ba khóa / Cô ấy là đàn em dưới tôi ba năm
japanese: 彼女（かのじょ）は３（みっ）つ年下（としした）だった。／３（さん）学年（がくねん）後輩（こうはい）だった。
chinese: 她比我低三届 (Tā bǐ wǒ dī sān jiè) / 她小我三岁 (Tā xiǎo wǒ sān suì)
primary: She was three years below me
example:

A: So she's younger?
B: Yeah, she was three years below me.

B: Different crowds, makes sense.

A: I guessed she was 25.
B: You are way off, she's 40.
A: Is the answer 100?
B: Way off. Try 1000.
A: His estimate for the project was way off.
B: Yeah, we're over budget now.

chinese: 差远了 (Chà yuǎn le) / 完全不着边 (Wánquán bùzháo biān)

example:

B: Sure, but if Beyonce walks in, she gets it.
A: Did you save my seat?
B: I tried, but some guy just sat down.
spanish: Guárdame el sitio / No dejes que nadie se siente ahí
chinese: 帮我占个座 (Bāng wǒ zhàn gè zuò) / 帮我留个位子 (Bāng wǒ liú gè wèizi)
japanese: 席（せき）取（と）っておいて。／ここキープしておいて。
vietnamese: Giữ chỗ cho tôi nhé / Xí chỗ cho tôi nha

spanish: Huevos de corral / Huevos camperos
vietnamese: Trứng gà thả vườn / Trứng gà ta
japanese: 平飼（ひらガ）い卵（たまご）。／放（はな）し飼（ガ）いの卵（たまご）。
example:

B: They're pasture raised eggs, top tier.
A: I only buy pasture raised eggs now.
B: Boujee! But do they taste better?
A: Pasture raised eggs have orange yolks, it's wild.
B: Nutrient density, fam.
chinese: 散养蛋 (Sànyǎng dàn) / 柴鸡蛋 (Cháijīdàn)
meaning: 방사 유정란 / 풀어 키운 닭이 낳은 알

vietnamese: Tôi có linh cảm / Tôi nghi nghi là vậy
meaning: 딱 촉이 와 / 왠지 그럴 것 같아
example:

B: Yeah, I have a gut feeling.

B: I have a gut feeling this is a shortcut.

B: Same, I have a gut feeling it's a scam.

chinese: 我有预感 (Wǒ yǒu yùgǎn) / 我直觉很准 (Wǒ zhíjué hěn zhǔn)
spanish: Tengo un presentimiento / Me da buena espina (o mala espina)

vietnamese: Đồ iều chuyện / Kẻ hay xía vô chuyện người khác
example:
A: Why is she asking so many questions?
B: She's such a busybody, ignore her.
A: Don't be a busybody, let them live.
B: I'm just curious!
A: Our neighbor is a total busybody.
B: Always watching from the window, right?
japanese: お節介（せっかい）。／出（で）しゃばり。
chinese: 爱管闲事的人 (Ài guǎn xiánshì de rén) / 事儿妈 (Shìrmā)

primary: Don't get me started on her boyfriend
vietnamese: Đừng để tôi nói về gã bạn trai của cô ta / Nhắc đến gã đó làm tôi bực
japanese: 彼（かれ）の話（はなし）はしないで。／彼（かれ）の話（はなし）を始（はじ）めると止（と）まらなくなる。
example:
A: Is she still with that guy?
B: Don't get me started on her boyfriend, he's the worst.

B: Don't get me started on her boyfriend, drama 24/7.
A: Is he nice?
B: Girl, don't get me started on her boyfriend.
meaning: 걔 남친 얘기는 꺼내지도 마 / 말도 마라
chinese: 别跟我提她男朋友 (Bié gēn wǒ tí tā nánpéngyou) / 提她男朋友我就烦 (Tí tā nánpéngyou wǒ jiù fán)
spanish: Ni me hables de su novio / Mejor no toquemos ese tema

example:
A: It's raining on our beach day.
B: It can't be helped, let's go to a museum.
A: The flight is delayed.
B: It can't be helped, grab a beer?
A: I wish I could fix it.
B: It can't be helped, don't stress.
spanish: No se puede hacer nada / Es inevitable
vietnamese: Đành chịu thôi / Hết cách rồi
japanese: 仕方（しかた）がない。／しょうがない。
chinese: 没办法 (Méi bànfǎ) / 这也是没办法的事 (Zhè yěshì méibànfǎ de shì)
出于必要 (Chūyú bìyào) / 迫不得已 (pò bù dé yǐ)
vietnamese: Vì bắt buộc thôi / Do tình thế ép buộc
japanese: 必要（ひつよう）に迫（せま）られて。／背（せ）に腹（はら）は代（か）えられない。
spanish: Por necesidad / No quedó más remedio
example:

B: Out of necessity, bills were piling up.
A: She learned to cook out of necessity.
B: Yeah, takeout gets expensive.
A: We moved here out of necessity.
B: Gotta go where the work is.

chinese: 别无选择 (Bié wú xuǎnzé) / 没得选 (méi de xuǎn)
example:
A: You really sold your car?
B: I had no other choice, needed the cash.
A: Why did you agree?
B: No other choice, they pressured me.
A: We have no other choice but to walk.
B: Ugh, my feet already hurt.
vietnamese: Không còn cách nào khác / Hết lựa chọn rồi
japanese: 他（ほか）に選択肢（せんたくし）がない。／そうするしかなかった。
spanish: No tenía otra opción / Fue lo único que pude hacer
meaning: 다른 방법이 없어 / 선택의 여지가 없어

japanese: そう言（い）われてみれば。／話（はなし）のついでだけど。
meaning: 네가 말을 꺼내서 하는 얘긴데 / 말이 나온 김에
vietnamese: Nhân tiện cậu nhắc tới / Sẵn cậu nói ra thì
spanish: Ya que lo mencionas / Hablando de eso
chinese: 既然你提到了 (Jìrán nǐ tídào le) / 说到这儿 (shuō dào zhèr)
example:

B: Since you bring it up, you never clean either.
A: We need to save money.
B: Since you bring it up, stop buying coffee.
A: He seems distracted.
B: Since you bring it up, I noticed that too.

spanish: Lo hago por inercia / Lo hago sin ganas
japanese: ただ惰性（だせい）でやってるだけ。／心（こころ）こもってないね。
example:
A: You like your job?
B: Nah, I'm just going through the motions.
A: You seem checked out.
B: Yeah, I'm just going through the motions lately.
A: Is he serious about her?
B: Doesn't look like it, he's just going through the motions.
chinese: 我只是走个过场 (Wǒ zhǐshì zǒu gè guòchǎng) / 应付一下 (yìngfù yīxià)
meaning: 그냥 대충 하는 거야 / 영혼 없이 하는 중이야
vietnamese: Tôi chỉ làm cho có lệ thôi / Làm như cái máy ấy mà

chinese: 我不是那种容易后悔的人 (Wǒ bùshì nàzhǒng róngyì hòuhuǐ de rén) / 我很少后悔 (wǒ hěn shǎo hòuhuǐ)
meaning: 나 원래 쉽게 후회 안 해
vietnamese: Tôi không phải kiểu người hay hối hận đâu / Tôi ít khi hối tiếc lắm
japanese: 私（わたし）、あんまり後悔（こうかい）しないタイプなんだ。／済（す）んだことは気（き）にしない。
example:

B: I live for it. I'm not the type to regret things easily.

spanish: No soy de los que se arrepienten fácilmente / No suelo mirar atrás

example:

B: Let's hit that conveyor belt sushi spot.
A: Conveyor belt sushi is fun but dangerous.
B: Yeah, I stack up plates without noticing.
A: Is the fish fresh at conveyor belt sushi?
B: Hit or miss, but it's cheap.

spanish: Sushi giratorio / Sushi de cinta
vietnamese: Sushi băng chuyền / Sushi quay
chinese: 回转寿司 (Huízhuǎn shòusī) / 旋转寿司 (xuánzhuǎn shòusī)

vietnamese: Rau mùi / Ngò rí
chinese: 香菜 (Xiāngcài) / 芫荽 (yán suī)
example:

B: You're lucky, I can't stand it.
A: This salsa has too much cilantro.
B: Perfect for me, pass it here.

example:

B: Nah, I'll get out of your hair, you look tired.

vietnamese: Tôi không làm phiền cậu nữa / Tôi lặn đây cho cậu làm việc
meaning: 이제귀찮게 안 할게 / 방해 안 하고 갈게

primary: Give a big round of applause to ~
example:

japanese: 〜に盛大（せいだい）な拍手（はくしゅ）を。／〜に大（おお）きな拍手（はくしゅ）をお願（ねが）いします。

chinese: 我有事想跟你谈谈 (Wǒ yǒu shì xiǎng gēn nǐ tántán) / 我有事想商量 (wǒ yǒu shì xiǎng shāngliáng)

japanese: 話（はなし）があるんだけど。／相談（そうだん）したいことがある。

chinese: 你凭什么这么说 (Nǐ píng shénme zhème shuō) / 轮得到你说吗 (lúndédào nǐ lái shuō ma)

similar: 2. Since when are you the expert?

spanish: Aclaremos las cosas antes de irnos / Vamos a resolver el malentendido
chinese: 咱们把误会解开 (Zánmen bǎ wùhuì jiěkāi) / 咱们把话说清楚 (zánmen bǎ huà shuō qīngchu)

vietnamese: Tôi chỉ nói thế thôi / Nói vậy thôi mà

example:

vietnamese: Tôi sáng nắng chiều mưa lắm / Tính tôi thất thường lắm
chinese: 我很情绪化 (Wǒ hěn qíngxùhuà) / 我喜怒无常 (xǐnùwúcháng)
japanese: 私（わたし）、気（き）分屋（ぶんや）なんだ。／感情（かんじょう）の起伏（きふく）が激（はげ）しくて。

example:
A: My phone is acting up.
B: Did you try turning it off and on?
A: My knee is acting up again.
B: Getting old, huh?
A: The car is acting up.
B: Mechanic time, prepare your wallet.

japanese: 調子（ちょうし）が悪（わる）い。／暴（あば）れ出（だ）した。
spanish: Está fallando / Me está dando problemas
chinese: 出毛病了 (Chū máobìng le) / 闹脾气了 (nào píqì le)

vietnamese: Bất tiện thật đấy / Phiền phức ghê

example:

spanish: Es muy inoportuno / Qué molestia

spanish: Vivir la vida al máximo / Disfrutar a tope
meaning: 인생 즐기면서 살아 / 후회 없이 살아
chinese: 尽情享受生活 (Jìnqíng xiǎngshòu shēnghuó) / 活得精彩 (huó de jīngcǎi)
japanese: 人生（じんせい）を謳歌（おうか）する。／思（おも）いっきり生（い）きる。
example:

B: She really lives life to the fullest.
A: Why save it for a special occasion?
B: Exactly, live life to the fullest.
A: I want to live life to the fullest, no regrets.
B: Start by booking that ticket.

A: You okay?
B: Don't talk to me, I'm in a food coma.
A: That thanksgiving dinner put me in a food coma.
B: Same, napping immediately.
A: I can't work, I'm in a food coma.
B: The post-lunch slump is real.

chinese: 饭气攻心 (Fànqì gōngxīn) / 吃饱了犯困 (chī bǎo le fànkùn)
vietnamese: Căng da bụng chùng da mắt / Ăn no buồn ngủ quá
spanish: Me ha dado el mal del puerco / Estoy que exploto de lleno
Me parece bien / Trato hecho
japanese: いいね。／それで決（き）まり。
chinese: 听起来不错 (Tīng qǐlái bùcuò) / 就这么办 (jiù zhème bàn)
vietnamese: Chốt kèo nhé / Nghe hợp lý đấy
example:
A: Dinner at 8?
B: Sounds like a plan.
A: We finish this, then we drink.
B: Sounds like a plan to me.
A: Meet at the park.
B: Sounds like a plan, see you there.

primary: This is not a knee-jerk reaction.
meaning: 욱해서 그러는 거 아냐 / 충동적인 거 아냐
spanish: No es una reacción impulsiva / Lo he pensado bien
japanese: 衝動的（しょうどうてき）なことじゃないよ。／よく考（かんガ）えた上（うえ）でのことだよ。
vietnamese: Tôi không phải bốc đồng đâu / Tôi suy nghĩ kỹ rồi mới làm
example:

B: Yes, this is not a knee-jerk reaction. I've been unhappy for months.
A: You're breaking up with him?
B: It's verified. This is not a knee-jerk reaction.
A: Selling the house seems sudden.
B: It's not. This is not a knee-jerk reaction.
chinese: 这不是一时冲动 (Zhè bùshì yīshí chōngdòng) / 这是考虑过的 (zhè shì kǎolǜ guò de)

example:

B: Yeah, it's not going through the motions with them.

B: I want to win, it's not going through the motions.
A: This apology feels real.
B: It is, it's not going through the motions.
vietnamese: Không phải làm cho qua loa đâu / Tôi làm thật tâm đấy
japanese: 形式的（けいしきてき）にやってるわけじゃない。／本気（ほんき）でやってる。
spanish: No es solo por cumplir / Lo hago de verdad
meaning: 그냥 대충 하는 거 아냐 / 진심이야
chinese: 我可不是应付了事 (Wǒ kě bùshì yìngfù liǎoshì) / 我很当回事 (wǒ hěn dāng huí shì)
primary: It's not going through the motions

vietnamese: Tôi nói nghiêm túc đấy / Tôi không đùa đâu
chinese: 我是认真的 (Wǒ shì rènzhēn de) / 我不是在开玩笑 (wǒ bùshì zài kāiwánxiào)

spanish: ¿Te apuntas a cenar? / ¿Te hace una cena?
primary: Are you up for the dinner?

example:

B: Nice.

B: Always down for K-BBQ.
A: Are you up for the dinner later?
B: Depends on where we going.
vietnamese: Đi ăn tối không? / Có hứng đi ăn tối không?

meaning: 나도 그러고 싶은데 (못 해) / 맘은 굴뚝같은데
chinese: 我也想去，但不行 (Wǒ yě xiǎng qù, dàn bùxíng) / 倒是想去，可是有事 (dàoshì xiǎng qù, kěshì yǒushì)
spanish: Ojalá pudiera / Me encantaría, pero no puedo
example:
A: Come to fast!
B: I wish I could, but I have work.
A: Be there in 10?
B: I wish I could, traffic is crazy.
A: Buy the shoes!
B: I wish I could, but my bank account says no.
vietnamese: Tôi cũng muốn lắm chứ / Ước gì đi được
japanese: 行（い）きたいのは山々（やまやま）だけど。／そうできればいいんだけど。

spanish: Parejas / Medias naranjas
japanese: パートナー。／大切（たいせつ）な人（ひと）。
example:
A: Is the party for everyone?
B: Yeah, bring your significant others.
A: No significant others allowed on boys' night.
B: Fair rule.
A: Do you have a significant other?
B: Nope, riding solo.
vietnamese: Nửa kia / Người yêu
chinese: 另一半 (Lìngyī bàn) / 家属 (jiāshǔ)
没问题 (Méi wèntí) / 当然可以 (dāngrán kěyǐ)
japanese: もちろん。／いいよ。
vietnamese: Chắc chắn rồi / Được chứ
example:
A: Can you pass the salt?
B: Sure thing.
A: Thanks for the ride.
B: Sure thing, anytime.
A: Watch my bag?
B: Sure thing, go ahead.
spanish: Dalo por hecho / Claro que sí

japanese: 任（まか）せて。／了解（りょうかい）。
vietnamese: Tôi hiểu rồi / Để đó tôi lo
spanish: Yo me encargo / Déjamelo a mí
chinese: 我帮你 (Wǒ bāng nǐ) / 包在我身上 (bāo zài wǒ shēnshang)
example:
A: I forgot my wallet.
B: Don't stress, I got you.
A: I need a ride home.
B: I got you, hop in.
A: Keep this a secret.
B: I got you, lips sealed.

japanese: はい、どうぞ。／これだね。
spanish: Aquí tienes / Aquí está
vietnamese: Của bạn đây / Đây nè
example:
A: Can I get a napkins?
B: Here you are.
A: Where's my phone?
B: Here you are, you left it on the table.
A: One iced coffee please.
B: Here you are, enjoy.
chinese: 给你 (Gěi nǐ) / 拿着 (názhe)
鼓掌 (Gǔzhǎng) / 喝彩 (hècǎi)
japanese: 拍手（はくしゅ）する。／称賛（しょうさん）する。
vietnamese: Vỗ tay tán thưởng / Hoan nghênh
spanish: Aplaudir / Elogiar
example:
A: We should applaud her effort.
B: Yeah, she tried hard.
A: I applaud your honesty.
B: Truth hurts, but needed to be said.
A: Everyone stood up to applaud.
B: Standing ovation vibes.

vietnamese: Nghe cho rõ đây / Nhìn miệng tôi nói này
spanish: Escúchame bien / Léeme los labios
chinese: 听好了 (Tīng hǎo le) / 仔细听 (zǐxì tīng)
example:
A: Can I borrow money?
B: Read my lips: No.
A: Are you sure?
B: Read my lips, I am not going.
A: Maybe next time?
B: Read my lips: Never.
meaning: 내 말 똑똑히 들어 / 잘 들어
japanese: よーく聞（き）いて。／言（い）っとくけど。

chinese: 心态年轻 (Xīntài niánqīng) / 老顽童 (lǎowántóng)
japanese: 気持（きも）ちは若（わか）い。／心（こころ）は青春（せいしゅん）。
spanish: Tengo el espíritu joven / Soy joven de corazón
vietnamese: Tâm hồn tôi vẫn trẻ trung lắm / Già người nhưng tâm hồn không già
example:
A: You still play video games?
B: Yeah, I'm young at heart.
A: Grandpa is on the dance floor!
B: He's young at heart, gotta love it.
A: Growing old is mandatory, growing up is optional.
B: Facts, young at heart forever.

spanish: Irse a pique / Salir mal
chinese: 走下坡路 (Zǒu xiàpōlù) / 越来越糟 (yuè lái yuè zāo)
japanese: 悪化（あっか）する。／ダメになる。
vietnamese: Đi tong rồi / Hỏng bét cả rồi
example:
A: How did the product launch go yesterday?
B: It started well, but then things went south fast.
A: Oh no, did the servers take a nosedive?
B: Yeah, the whole system just spiraled out of control.
A: That sounds like a total nightmare.
B: It was. Everything went down the drain in an hour.

spanish: ¿Te ha comido la lengua el gato? / ¿Por qué estás tan callado?
example:
A: Why aren't you saying anything about the broken vase?
B: ...
A: Cat got your tongue? I know you were playing ball in here.
B: I didn't mean to break it, deadass.
A: Just own up to it next time.
B: My bad, I was just scared you'd blow up.
vietnamese: Mèo tha mất lưỡi rồi à? / Sao im như thóc thế?
chinese: 哑巴了? (Yǎba le?) / 怎么不说话? (zěnme bù shuōhuà?)
japanese: なんで黙（だま）ってるの？／借（か）りてきた猫（ねこ）みたい。
meaning: 꿀 먹은 벙어리야? / 왜 말이 없어?

chinese: 我总觉得不对劲 (Wǒ zǒng juéde bùduìjìn) / 这种感觉挥之不去 (zhèzhǒng gǎnjué huīzhībùqù)
vietnamese: Tôi cứ thấy lấn cấn sao ấy / Có cảm giác không yên tâm
spanish: No se me quita la sensación / Tengo el presentimiento
example:
A: You checked the lock?
B: Yeah, but I can't shake the feeling I left it open.
A: He seems nice.
B: I can't shake the feeling he's hiding something.
A: Relax, it's fine.
B: I can't shake the feeling something is wrong.
japanese: 嫌（いや）な予感（よかん）が拭（ぬぐ）えない。／なんか引（ひ）っかかる。
meaning: 이 찜찜한 기분을 지울 수가 없어

vietnamese: Tôi kịch liệt đề cử đấy / Cái này đáng thử lắm
spanish: Te lo recomiendo mucho / No te lo puedes perder
japanese: 超（ちょう）おすすめ。／絶対（ぜったい）いいよ。
chinese: 我强烈推荐 (Wǒ qiángliè tuījiàn) / 值得一试 (zhídé yī shì)
example:
A: Is that show good?
B: I highly recommend it, binged it in a day.
A: Should I get the steak?
B: I highly recommend it, best in the city.
A: Thinking about therapy.
B: I highly recommend it, game changer.

primary: Put your cell phone on silent
chinese: 手机调成静音 (Shǒujī tiáo chéng jìngyīn) / 设为无声 (shè wèi wúshēng)
vietnamese: Để điện thoại chế độ im lặng đi / Tắt chuông điện thoại đi
example:
A: Movie is starting. Put your cell phone on silent.
B: Done.
A: Dad is sleeping.
B: Put your cell phone on silent so you don't wake him.
A: Meeting is about to start.
B: Everyone, put your cell phone on silent please.
spanish: Pon el móvil en silencio / Quítale el sonido al móvil
japanese: マナーモードにして。／携帯（けいたい）の音（おと）切（き）って。

vietnamese: Tôi sẽ chịu trách nhiệm hoàn toàn / Chuyện này dừng ở tôi là xong
meaning: 모든 책임은 내가 져 / 내 선에서 끝내
spanish: La responsabilidad es mía / Yo asumo las consecuencias
japanese: 責任（せきにん）は私（わたし）が取（と）る。／ここが最終（さいしゅう）責任（せきにん）だ。
chinese: 我说了算 (Wǒ shuōle suàn) / 我全权负责 (wǒ quánquán fùzé)
example:
A: Who's in charge if this fails?
B: The buck stops here. I'll take the heat.
A: Don't blame the intern.
B: I know, the buck stops here.
A: You sure you want to sign this?
B: Yes, the buck stops here.

A: Can you squeeze me in?
B: Sorry, my book is solid this week.
A: Business is good?
B: Yeah, my book is solid for months.
A: I need an appointment.
B: My book is solid, try next month.
japanese: 予約（よやく）でいっぱいだ。／空（あ）きがない。
vietnamese: Lịch kín mít rồi / Không còn chỗ trống nào đâu
spanish: Tengo la agenda llena / No tengo huecos
chinese: 预订满了 (Yùdìng mǎn le) / 没位子了 (méi wèizi le)

vietnamese: Hôm nay tôi bận tối mắt tối mũi / Lịch trình hôm nay kẹt cứng rồi
japanese: 今日（きょう）は予定（よてい）がびっちりだ。／スケジュールが埋（う）まってる。
chinese: 我的日程排满了 (Wǒ de rìchéng pái mǎn le) / 我今天很忙 (wǒ jīntiān hěn máng)
example:
A: Lunch?
B: Can't, my schedule is solid today.
A: You look stressed.
B: My schedule is solid today, no breaks.
A: Can we meet for 5 mins?
B: Impossible, my schedule is solid today.
spanish: Hoy tengo el día a tope / Estoy completísimo hoy

chinese: 之前约满了 (Zhīqián yuē mǎn le) / 早就订光了 (zǎojiù dìngguāng le)
japanese: 予約（よやく）で満杯（まんぱい）だった。／全然（ぜんぜん）空（あ）いてなかった。
vietnamese: Lúc đó tôi bận không thở nổi / Lịch kín bưng rồi
spanish: Estaba a tope / No tenía ni un minuto libre
example:
A: Why didn't you answer?
B: I was booked solid all day.
A: Did you get a table?
B: No, the restaurant was booked solid.
A: You tired?
B: Exhausted, I was booked solid this week.

A: Who is that?
B: Just a neighborhood friend.
A: You hang out often?
B: Yeah, he's a neighborhood friend, lives two doors down.
A: It's good to have a neighborhood friend.
B: Definitely, good for emergencies.
spanish: Amigo del barrio / Colega de la zona
japanese: 近所（きんじょ）の友達（ともだち）。／地元（じもと）のツレ。
vietnamese: Bạn hàng xóm / Bạn cùng xóm
chinese: 街坊邻居 (Jiēfang línjū) / 发小儿 (Fàxiǎoer)

spanish: Soy joven de espíritu / Llevo un niño dentro
vietnamese: Tâm hồn tôi vẫn còn trẻ chán / Tôi vẫn còn xì tin lắm
japanese: 気持（きも）ちは若（わか）いよ。／心（こころ）はいつまでも青春（せいしゅん）。
example:
A: You're jumping in the bouncy castle?
B: I'm young at heart!
A: Act your age.
B: Boring. I'm young at heart.
A: She dresses so colorful.
B: She's young at heart.
chinese: 我心态很年轻 (Wǒ xīntài hěn niánqīng) / 我人老心不老 (Wǒ rén lǎo xīn bù lǎo)

example:
A: Hey, I have a few questions about the new policy.
B: Sure thing, fire away.
A: Are you sure you have time right now?
B: Yeah, shoot it, I'm not busy at all.
A: Okay, hit me with the truth about the layoffs.
B: I'll give it to you straight, lay it on me.
spanish: Dispara / Soy todo oídos
vietnamese: Nói đi nào / Cứ hỏi tự nhiên
japanese: どうぞ。／何（なん）でも聞（き）いて。
chinese: 尽管问 (Jǐnguǎn wèn) / 有话直说 (Yǒu huà zhíshuō)

vietnamese: Giỏi mấy cũng có lúc sơ suất / Khôn ngoan cũng có khi lầm
example:
A: I failed the test and I studied so hard.
B: Even a monkey can fall from a tree. You'll get it next time.
A: The expert got it wrong?
B: Even a monkey can fall from a tree.
A: I burnt the toast.
B: Even a monkey can fall from a tree, chef.
similar: Happens to the best of us
japanese: 誰（だれ）にでも失敗（しっぱい）はある。／猿（さる）も木（き）から落（お）ちる。
spanish: Hasta el mejor escribano echa un borrón / Cualquiera se equivoca
primary: Even a monkey can fall from a tree
chinese: 人有失手 (Rén yǒu shīshǒu) / 智者千虑必有一失 (Zhìzhě qiānlǜ bì yǒu yī shī)

example:
A: How did you find this apartment?
B: Had to pound the pavement, looked at 20 places.
A: Sales is tough.
B: Yeah, lots of legwork and pounding the pavement.
A: Can't just sit online, gotta pound the pavement.
B: True, go see people in person.
japanese: 足（あし）で稼（かせ）ぐ。／地道（じみち）に回（まわ）る。
spanish: Patearse la calle / Hacer el trabajo de campo
vietnamese: Cày nát mặt đường / Tự thân vận động
chinese: 跑腿儿 (Pǎotuǐr) / 亲自去跑 (Qīnzì qù pǎo)

spanish: ¿Y ahora qué? / ¿Cuál es el plan?
example:
A: We missed the bus. Now what?
B: Uber?
A: I finished the task. Now what?
B: You can go home.
A: The plan failed. Now what?
B: We improvise.
japanese: で、どうする？／これからどうなるの？
vietnamese: Giờ sao đây? / Rồi tính sao?
chinese: 接下来怎么办? (Jiēxiàlái zěnmebàn?) / 然后呢? (Ránhòu ne?)

vietnamese: Tôi quê ở đâu á? Đây là đất của chúng tôi / Đây là khu của chúng mình mà
example:
A: Where are you really from?
B: Where am I from? This is our land, born in Queens.
A: You don't look like you're from here.
B: Bruh, this is our land. My family built this block.
A: Go back to your country.
B: Where am I from? This is our land. Educate yourself.
spanish: ¿Que de dónde soy? Esta es mi tierra / Soy de aquí de toda la vida
primary: Where am i from? This is our land
japanese: 出身（しゅっしん）どこって？ここは俺（おれ）たちの土地（とち）だ。／生（う）まれも育（そだ）ちもここだ。
meaning: 내가 어디서 왔냐고? 여긴 우리 땅이야
chinese: 这是咱们的地盘 (Zhè shì zánmen de dìpán) / 我们是这儿的主人 (Wǒmen shì zhèr de zhǔrén)

example:
A: Did you see the price of rent?
B: Yeah, this is bananas.
A: He jumped off the roof into the pool.
B: That dude is bananas.
A: The line for Supreme is 3 blocks long.
B: This is bananas, hypebeasts are wild.
spanish: Esto es una locura / Es de locos
japanese: 頭（あたま）おかしいよ。／マジで最高（さいこう）。
vietnamese: Điên rồ thật / Chuyện thật như đùa
chinese: 太疯狂了 (Tài fēngkuáng le) / 简直不可理喻 (Jiǎnzhí bùkě lǐyù)

vietnamese: Quá quắt thật / Không thể chấp nhận được
spanish: Indignante / Es una vergüenza
japanese: とんでもない。／法外（ほうがい）だ。
example:
A: They charge $10 for water.
B: That's outrageous.
A: Did you see her dress?
B: Yeah, it was outrageous but she pulled it off.
A: His demands are outrageous.
B: Tell him to kick rocks.
chinese: 太离谱了 (Tài lípǔ le) / 岂有此理 (Qǐyǒucǐlǐ)

example:
A: The movie was long, but the ending was good.
B: I will give you that, the ending saved it.
A: He's annoying, but he's smart.
B: I will give you that, he knows his stuff.
A: At least I tried.
B: True, I will give you that.
japanese: それは認（みと）める。／一理（いちり）あるね。
vietnamese: Cái đó thì tôi công nhận / Điểm này thì cậu đúng
spanish: Te lo concedo / En eso tienes razón
chinese: 这我得承认 (Zhè wǒ děi chéngrèn) / 这一点我服你 (Zhè yīdiǎn wǒ fú nǐ)

primary: I got carried away with works
japanese: 仕事（しごと）に夢中（むちゅう）になりすぎた。／仕事（しごと）の虫（むし）になってた。
spanish: Se me fue el santo al cielo trabajando / Me lié con el trabajo
vietnamese: Tôi mải làm quá / Cuốn theo công việc quá
example:
A: You missed lunch.
B: Sorry, I got carried away with work.
A: Why are you still at the office?
B: I got carried away with work, lost track of time.
A: You need a break.
B: I know, I always get carried away with work.
meaning: 일하느라 정신이 팔렸어 / 일에 너무 몰입했어
chinese: 我工作太投入了 (Wǒ gōngzuò tài tóurù le) / 我忙得忘乎所以了 (Wǒ máng de wànghūsuǒyǐ le)

meaning: 커피 좀 마셨으면 좋겠다 / 커피 땡겨
japanese: コーヒー飲（の）みたい。／カフェイン切（ぎ）れだ。
example:
A: You look tired.
B: I could use some coffee like right now.
A: Starbucks run?
B: Yes! I could use some coffee.
A: Long meeting ahead.
B: Ugh, I could use some coffee first.
vietnamese: Thèm cà phê quá / Phải làm ly cà phê mới được
spanish: Me vendría bien un café / Necesito café urgente
chinese: 我得来杯咖啡 (Wǒ děi lái bēi kāfēi) / 我想喝杯咖啡 (Wǒ xiǎng hē bēi kāfēi)

japanese: 頭（あたま）がパンクしそう。／脳（のう）みそが溶（と）けそう。
vietnamese: Đầu óc tôi quay cuồng rồi / Não tôi đóng băng rồi
example:
A: Do you want to review the budget one more time?
B: Not now, my brain is fried from that meeting.
A: I get it, I'm pretty drained by all these numbers too.
B: Honestly, my mind is mush right now.
A: Let's call it a day then.
B: Good call, I'm running on fumes anyway.
spanish: Tengo el cerebro frito / No me da la cabeza para más
chinese: 我脑子都要炸了 (Wǒ nǎozi dōu yào zhà le) / 脑子不够用了 (Nǎozi bùgòu yòng le)

vietnamese: Hơi đâu mà làm? / Làm chi cho mệt?
japanese: なんでわざわざ？／やるだけ無駄（むだ）でしょ？
example:
A: Should I text him back?
B: Why bother? He ghosted you.
A: I'm gonna clean before the party.
B: Why bother? It's gonna get messy again.
A: Trying to explain it to him.
B: Why bother? He never listens.
meaning: 굳이 왜 그래? / 뭐 하러 그래?
spanish: ¿Para qué molestarse? / No merece la pena
chinese: 何必呢? (Hébì ne?) / 费那个劲干嘛? (Fèi nàge jìn gànmá?)

vietnamese: Xả hơi / Thư giãn chút
example:
A: Stressed?
B: Yeah, need to go home and unwind.
A: How do you unwind?
B: Wine and Netflix, standard procedure.
A: Let's go for a drink to unwind.
B: Say less.
spanish: Desconectar / Relajarse
japanese: くつろぐ。／羽（は）を伸（の）ばす。
chinese: 放松一下 (Fàngsōng yīxià) / 歇会儿 (Xiē huìr)

spanish: ¿A que no sabes qué? / ¿Adivina qué?
example:
A: Guess what?
B: What?
A: I got the job!
B: Yoooo! Congrats!
A: Guess what?
B: You saw your ex.
vietnamese: Biết gì không? / Đoán xem nào?
japanese: ねえ聞（き）いて。／あのさ。
chinese: 你猜怎么着? (Nǐ cāi zěnmezháo?) / 跟你说个事儿 (Gēn nǐ shuō gè shìr)

japanese: 前（まえ）もって。／事前（じぜん）に。
vietnamese: Làm trước đi / Chuẩn bị trước đi
example:
A: Did you book the tickets?
B: Yeah, bought them beforehand.
A: Let me know beforehand if you're bringing friends.
B: Will do.
A: We should have checked the weather beforehand.
B: Now we're soaked. Great.
spanish: De antemano / Con antelación
chinese: 预先 (Yùxiān) / 事先 (Shìxiān)

example:
A: Did I scare him off?
B: You might have come across too strong.
A: I want to be confident, not come across too strong.
B: Just be chill, don't force it.
A: The sales guy came across too strong.
B: Hate that, makes me leave immediately.
spanish: Entrar demasiado fuerte / Ser demasiado intenso
vietnamese: Tỏ ra quá đà / Gây áp lực quá
japanese: グイグイ来（く）りすぎ。／強引（ごういん）すぎて引（ひ）く。
chinese: 表现得太强势了 (Biǎoxiàn de tài qiángshì le) / 给人感觉太冲了 (Gěi rén gǎnjué tài chōng le)

spanish: Me dejó alucinado / Me quedé de piedra
example:
A: Did you see her performance?
B: I was blown away. Incredible.
A: The food here blew me away.
B: Told you it was fire.
A: He blew me away with that speech.
B: Very inspiring.
vietnamese: Sốc tận óc / Choáng ngợp luôn
japanese: 感動（かんどう）した。／圧倒（あっとう）された。
chinese: 被惊艳到了 (Bèi jīngyàn dào le) / 佩服得五体投地 (Pèifú de wǔtǐtóudì)

vietnamese: Dạo này tôi hơi phiền phức đúng không / Gần đây tôi cứ lải nhải suốt
japanese: 最近（さいきん）迷惑（めいわく）かけてごめん。／手（て）を焼（や）かせてごめん。
example:
A: Sorry, I know I have been a pain lately.
B: It's okay, you're going through stuff.
A: She admits she's been a pain lately.
B: At least she knows.
A: Don't be a pain.
B: I'm trying not to be!
meaning: 내가 요즘 좀 진상이었지 / 골치 아프게 굴었지
spanish: He sido un pesado últimamente / He dado mucho la lata
primary: I have been a pain lately
chinese: 我最近挺招人烦的 (Wǒ zuìjìn tǐng zhāorénfán de) / 我最近很难搞 (Wǒ zuìjìn hěn nángǎo)

vietnamese: Trải qua / Kiểm tra kỹ
japanese: 経験（けいけん）する。／切（き）り抜（ぬ）ける。
example:
A: Did the payment go through?
B: Yeah, got the receipt.
A: He's going through a lot right now.
B: Poor guy.
A: Let's go through the details.
B: boring but necessary.
spanish: Pasar por / Atravesar
chinese: 经历 (Jīnglì) / 遭受 (Zāoshòu)

vietnamese: Khỏi phải bàn / Đương nhiên rồi
japanese: 言（い）うまでもない。／当然（とうぜん）でしょ。
example:
A: You're invited, obviously.
B: It goes without saying.
A: It goes without saying, don't tell Mom.
B: Lips sealed.
A: We need to win.
B: That goes without saying.
spanish: No hace falta ni decirlo / Es obvio
chinese: 不言而喻 (Bùyán'éryù) / 那还用说 (Nà hái yòng shuō)

spanish: Me gusta que / Valoro que
example:
A: I like the fact that he's honest.
B: Rare quality these days.
A: I like the fact that it's open 24/7.
B: Late night eats!
A: She doesn't care what people think. I like the fact that.
B: Confidence is key.
vietnamese: Tôi thích cái chỗ là / Ưng nhất là điểm này
japanese: そういうとこ好（す）き。／そこがいいんだよね。
chinese: 我就喜欢这一点 (Wǒ jiù xǐhuan zhè yīdiǎn) / 我很欣赏... (Wǒ hěn xīnshǎng...)

example:
A: Will you be at the protest tomorrow?
B: I'll be there no matter what.
A: Even if it pours? The forecast looks bad.
B: Rain or shine, I'm showing up.
A: Glad to hear you're with us through thick and thin.
B: Come hell or high water, count me in.
japanese: 何（なに）ガ何（なん）でも。／雨（あめ）が降（ふ）ろうが槍（やり）が降（ふ）ろうが。
vietnamese: Bằng mọi giá / Dù thế nào đi nữa
spanish: Pase lo que pase / Sí o sí
similar: 1. Rain or shine, through thick and thin
chinese: 不管怎样 (Bùguǎn zěnyàng) / 无论如何 (Wúlùn rúhé)

vietnamese: Theo kinh nghiệm của tôi / Dựa trên những gì tôi trải qua
example:
A: You think it will work?
B: Given my experience, probably not.
A: Why are you worried?
B: Given my experience with him, he's gonna flake.
A: Given my experience, cheap is expensive.
B: Buy nice or buy twice.
japanese: 経験（けいけん）上（じょう）。／私（わたし）の経験（けいけん）から言（い）うと。
spanish: Por mi experiencia / Según lo que he vivido
chinese: 根据我的经验 (Gēnjù wǒ de jīngyàn) / 照我看来 (Zhào wǒ kànlái)

vietnamese: Nuốt lời / Rút lại lời đã nói
example:
A: You said he'd lose.
B: I know, I have to eat my words. He won.
A: Never say never.
B: Or you'll have to eat your words.
A: She proved me wrong.
B: Time to eat your words.
japanese: 前言（ぜんげん）撤回（てっかい）するよ。／間違（まちが）ってた、認（みと）める。
spanish: Tragarme mis palabras / Admitir mi error
chinese: 收回我说的话 (Shōuhuí wǒ shuō de huà) / 打脸了 (Dǎliǎn le)

japanese: 期待（きたい）に応（こた）える。／期待（きたい）通（どお）りだ。
vietnamese: Đúng như tôi kỳ vọng / Không làm tôi thất vọng
spanish: Estar a la altura de mis expectativas / Cumplir con lo que esperaba
example:
A: Was the movie good?
B: Didn't live up to my expectations.
A: Hope the food lives up to my expectations.
B: It's Michelin star, chill.
A: Use the data.
B: Failed to live up to expectations.
chinese: 没辜负我的期望 (Méi gūfù wǒ de qīwàng) / 正如我所料 (Zhèngrú wǒ suǒ liào)

spanish: Es adictivo / No puedes comer solo uno
vietnamese: Ăn hoài không chán / Càng ăn càng ghiền
japanese: クセになる。／止（と）まらないおいしさ。
example:
A: These chips are so moreish.
B: I ate the whole bag.
A: That sauce is dangerous.
B: Very moreish.
A: Want one?
B: No, they're too moreish and I'm on a diet.
chinese: 好听到上瘾 (Hǎotīng dào shàngyǐn) / 吃不够 (Chī bùgòu) (for food)

example:
A: Did you win?
B: Against him? I never stood a chance.
A: She's out of your league.
B: keep reminding me I never stood a chance.
A: The test was impossible.
B: Yeah, we never stood a chance.
meaning: 애초에 가망이 없었어 / 상대가 안 됐어
spanish: Nunca tuve oportunidad / No tenía nada que hacer
japanese: 勝（か）ち目（め）はなかった。／最初（さいしょ）から無理（むり）ゲーだった。
vietnamese: Tôi chẳng có cửa nào đâu / Ngay từ đầu đã thua rồi
chinese: 我根本没机会 (Wǒ gēnběn méi jīhuì) / 我毫无胜算 (Wǒ háowú shèngsuàn)

japanese: 予想（よそう）できたはずなのに。／やっぱりこうなったか。
spanish: Debí haberlo visto venir / Era de esperar
vietnamese: Đáng lẽ tôi phải đoán ra sớm hơn / Biết trước thì đỡ rồi
example:
A: He cheated.
B: I guess I should have seen it coming.
A: It rained.
B: Should have seen it coming, sky was grey.
A: They cancelled.
B: Classic them. Should have seen it coming.
primary: I guess i should have seen it coming
meaning: 이럴 줄 알았어야 했는데 / 예상했어야 했는데
chinese: 我早该想到的 (Wǒ zǎo gāi xiǎngdào de) / 我早该料到会这样 (Wǒ zǎo gāi liàodào huì zhèyàng)

japanese: 今日（きょう）の気分（きぶん）はどう？／調子（ちょうし）いい？
vietnamese: Hôm nay thấy trong người thế nào? / Nay ổn không?
spanish: ¿Cómo te encuentras hoy? / ¿Qué tal estás hoy?
example:
A: How are you feeling today?
B: Better than yesterday.
A: You look pale. How are you feeling today?
B: Little sick.
A: Big day! How are you feeling today?
B: Nervous but ready.
chinese: 你今天感觉怎么样? (Nǐ jīntiān gǎnjué zěnmeyàng?)

spanish: Estar cabreado con / Estar molesto con
example:
A: Why is he ticked off at you?
B: I ate his lunch.
A: Don't get ticked off at the messenger.
B: I know, sorry.
A: She's ticked off at the service.
B: Leave a bad review then.
japanese: 〜にムカつく。／〜に腹（はら）が立（た）つ。
vietnamese: Nổi cáu với / Bực mình với
chinese: 惹恼 (Rěnǎo) / 让...生气 (Ràng... shēngqì)

spanish: Investigar a fondo / Examinar
example:
A: Did you vet the new guy?
B: Yeah, he's clean.
A: We need to vet these sources.
B: Fake news is everywhere.
A: Vet the venue before booking.
B: Good call.
A: Took the dog to the vet.
B: Hope he's okay.
vietnamese: Bác sĩ thú y / Cựu binh
japanese: 厳（きび）しくチェックする。／身元（みもと）を洗（あら）う。
chinese: 兽医 (Shòuyī) / 审查 (Shěnchá) (verb)

vietnamese: Có bẫy gì không? / Điều kiện là gì?
japanese: 裏（うら）があるんでしょ？／話（はなし）がうますぎる。
example:
A: They're offering a free cruise to everyone in the office.
B: Wait, what's the catch?
A: Apparently, there are no strings attached.
B: That sounds too good to be true, honestly.
A: You think there's a hidden hook somewhere?
B: Always. Let's find out what the real story is.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el truco? / ¿Dónde está la trampa?
chinese: 有什么条件? (Yǒu shénme tiáojiàn?) / 没那么简单吧? (Méi nàme jiǎndān ba?)

vietnamese: Không cần quà cáp gì đâu / Cậu đến là vui rồi
example:
A: What do you want for your birthday?
B: You don't need to get me anything.
A: I'm bringing wine.
B: You don't need to get me anything, but thanks.
A: Seriously, no gifts.
B: Okay, if you insist.
meaning: 선물 안 사와도 돼 / 빈손으로 와
spanish: No hace falta que me regales nada / No tenías que molestarte
primary: You don't need to get me anything
japanese: 何（なに）もいらないよ。／気（き）を使（つか）わないで。
chinese: 不用给我买什么 (Bùyòng gěi wǒ mǎi shénme) / 别破费了 (Bié pòfèi le)

japanese: 今（いま）、肩（かた）の治療（ちりょう）中（ちゅう）なんだ。／肩（かた）を痛（いた）めてて。
vietnamese: Dạo này tôi đang trị liệu vai / Đang chữa cái vai đau
spanish: Me estoy tratando el hombro / Estoy yendo a rehabilitación del hombro
example:
A: Want to play tennis?
B: Can't, I'm getting treatment for my shoulder.
A: What happened?
B: Gym injury. Currently getting treatment.
A: Hope it heals active.
B: Thanks, PT is helping.
primary: I'm currently getting treatment for my shoulder
chinese: 我肩膀正在做治疗 (Wǒ jiānbǎng zhèngzài zuò zhìliáo)

example:
A: Massage the crown of the head.
B: Feels so good.
A: The bird landed on the crown of his head.
B: Like a Disney princess.
A: Balding at the crown of the head.
B: It happens.
japanese: つむじ。／頭（あたま）のてっぺん。
vietnamese: Đỉnh đầu / Chóp đầu
spanish: La coronilla / La parte superior de la cabeza
chinese: 头顶 (Tóudǐng)

japanese: 選（えら）べない。／めっちゃ迷（まよ）ってる。
vietnamese: Tôi phân vân quá / Không biết chọn cái nào
meaning: 나 완전 결정장애 왔어 / 너무 고민돼
spanish: Estoy hecho un lío / No sé qué decidir
example:
A: New York or LA?
B: I'm so torn. I love both.
A: Pizza or Tacos?
B: Don't make me choose, I'm so torn.
A: She's torn between two guys.
B: Drama.
chinese: 我很纠结 (Wǒ hěn jiūjié) / 我左右为难 (Wǒ zuǒyòu wéinán)

spanish: En el limbo / En punto muerto
vietnamese: Lơ lửng giữa chừng / Tiến thoái lưỡng nan
example:
A: Did you get the visa?
B: No, application is in limbo.
A: The project is in limbo.
B: Waiting for budget approval?
A: I hate being in limbo.
B: Not knowing is the worst.
japanese: どっちつかずの状態（じょうたい）。／宙（ちゅう）ぶらりん。
chinese: 悬而未决 (Xuán'érwèijué) / 没着落 (Méi zhuóluò)

japanese: 彼（かれ）は本物（ほんもの）だ。／ガチですごい。
vietnamese: Cậu ấy đỉnh thật đấy / Hàng thật giá thật đấy
example:
A: Is he a good lawyer?
B: Yeah, he is legit.
A: That sneaker store is legit.
B: No fakes?
A: He's legit crazy.
B: In a good way?
spanish: Es legal / Es de fiar
chinese: 他很正规 (Tā hěn zhèngguī) / 他很靠谱 (Tā hěn kàopǔ)

vietnamese: Cậu ấy là món hời đấy / Người yêu lý tưởng đấy
japanese: 彼（かれ）はいい男（おとこ）だ。／逃（のが）しちゃダメな人（ひと）だよ。
spanish: Es un partidazo / Es un chollo de tío
example:
A: You dating Mike?
B: Yeah, he is a total catch.
A: Don't let him go, he's a catch.
B: I know, he cooks too.
A: She's a catch.
B: Smart, funny, and rich.
chinese: 他是个好对象 (Tā shì gè hǎo duìxiàng) / 他是个抢手货 (Tā shì gè qiǎngshǒuhuò)

spanish: No te lo tomes a la tremenda / No te enfades por eso
japanese: ヘソ曲（ま）げないで。／怒（おこ）らないでよ。
vietnamese: Đừng có cáu kỉnh thế / Đừng làm quá lên
example:
A: He dented my car!
B: Don't get bent out of shape, insurance covers it.
A: She canceled.
B: Don't get bent out of shape, we can go without her.
A: It's just a joke, don't get bent out of shape.
B: Wasn't funny.
primary: Don't get bent out of shape
chinese: 别不高兴 (Bié bù gāoxìng) / 别急眼 (Bié jíyǎn)

vietnamese: Tôi cũng không hối hận lắm đâu / Chẳng tiếc nuối gì mấy
spanish: No me arrepiento de mucho / No tengo grandes remordimientos
example:
A: Do you regret moving?
B: Nah, I don't regret that much.
A: Mistakes were made.
B: True, but I don't regret that much.
A: Life is good.
B: Exactly, no regrets.
japanese: そんなに後悔（こうかい）してない。／まあいい経験（けいけん）だった。
chinese: 我没什么好后悔的 (Wǒ méi shénme hǎo hòuhuǐ de)

japanese: 中年（ちゅうねん）の危機（きき）。／ミッドライフ・クライシス。
vietnamese: Khủng hoảng tuổi trung niên / Khủng hoảng giữa đời
example:
A: I think I'm having a midlife crisis.
B: You're like thirty, calm down.
A: Seriously, I just want to quit everything.
B: That's just NYC burnout, not a crisis.
A: Maybe I should buy a convertible.
B: In this traffic? Get a bike instead.
spanish: Crisis de los cuarenta / Crisis de la mediana edad
meaning: 40~50대에 겪는 인생에 대한 혼란과 권태, 삶을 다시 돌아보는 시기
chinese: 中年危机 (Zhōngnián wēijī)

japanese: 大（おお）げさだよ。／悲劇（ひげき）のヒロインぶらないで。
spanish: No seas tan dramático / No montes un numerito
vietnamese: Đừng có diễn sâu quá / Đừng làm quá lên thế
example:
A: My life is over!
B: You broke a nail. Don't be so dramatic.
A: I'm dying of hunger.
B: Don't be so dramatic, we're eating in 5 mins.
A: He hates me.
B: He literally didn't see you. Don't be so dramatic.
chinese: 别太夸张 (Bié tài kuāzhāng) / 别戏精了 (Bié xìjīng le)

japanese: 泣（な）き言（ごと）言（い）わないで。／グチグチ言（い）うな。
example:
A: It's so hard being rich.
B: Don't cry me a river.
A: I have to work on Saturday.
B: Welcome to adulthood, don't cry me a river.
A: He broke up with me.
B: After you cheated? Don't cry me a river.
spanish: No me vengas con lloriqueos / A llorar a la llorería
vietnamese: Thôi đừng có khóc lóc ỉ ôi nữa / Đừng than vãn nữa
chinese: 别跟我哭诉 (Bié gēn wǒ kūsù) / 少来这套 (Shǎo lái zhè tào)

primary: I am at loss for words
japanese: 言葉（ことば）が出（で）ない。／呆（あき）れてものが言（い）えない。
example:
A: She wore white to the wedding.
B: I am at a loss for words. The disrespect.
A: We won the lottery!
B: I am at a loss for words!
A: Look at this mess.
B: I am at a loss for words. Who did this?
vietnamese: Cạn lời luôn / Không nói nên lời
spanish: Me he quedado sin palabras / No sé qué decir
chinese: 我无语了 (Wǒ wúyǔ le) / 我都不知道说什么好了 (Wǒ dōu bù zhīdào shuō shénme hǎo le)

japanese: 今（いま）の気（き）づいた？／ピンときた？
example:
A: He winked at her.
B: Did you catch on that?
A: She's avoiding the question.
B: Yeah, I caught on that.
A: The mood shifted.
B: Glad I'm not the only one who caught on that.
spanish: ¿Te has dado cuenta de eso? / ¿Pillaste eso?
vietnamese: Cậu có nắm được ý đó không? / Hiểu ra chưa?
chinese: 你懂了吗? (Nǐ dǒng le ma?) / 你明白我的意思了吗? (Nǐ míngbai wǒ de yìsi le ma?)

japanese: 空気（くうき）を読（よ）むこと。／暗黙（あんもく）の了解（りょうかい）。
spanish: Señales sociales / Indirectas sociales
example:
A: He missed all the social cues to leave.
B: So awkward.
A: Pick up on social cues, man.
B: I'm trying!
A: She's good at reading social cues.
B: High EQ.
vietnamese: Tín hiệu ngầm / Ý tứ
chinese: 社交暗示 (Shèjiāo ànshì) / 眼力见儿 (Yǎnlìjiànr)

spanish: ¿Te percataste de eso? / ¿Lo captaste?
vietnamese: Cậu có nhận ra không? / Có phát hiện ra không?
example:
A: There's tension between them.
B: Did you pick up on that too?
A: He was lying.
B: Yeah, I picked up on that immediately.
A: Subtle shade intended.
B: I picked up on that.
japanese: 今（いま）のわかった？／気（き）づいた？
chinese: 你注意到了吗? (Nǐ zhùyì dào le ma?) / 你听出来了吗? (Nǐ tīng chūlái le ma?)

meaning: 마크 이직 제안 받은 거 들었어?
vietnamese: Nghe tin Mark có việc mới chưa? / Biết vụ Mark được mời làm chỗ mới chưa?
primary: Did you hear Mark's new job offer?
spanish: ¿Te enteraste de la oferta de trabajo de Mark? / ¿Supiste lo del nuevo curro de Mark?
example:
A: Did you hear Mark's new job offer?
B: Yeah, he's making bank now.
A: Mark is leaving.
B: Did you hear about his offer? It's intense.
A: Gossip time.
B: Did you hear Mark's new job offer? Crazy.
japanese: マークの新（あたら）しい仕事（しごと）の話（はなし）聞（き）いた？／マークの転職（てんしょく）の件（けん）、知（し）ってる？
chinese: 你听说Mark的新工作了吗? (Nǐ tīngshuō Mark de xīn gōngzuò le ma?)

japanese: 福利（ふくり）厚生（こうせい）。／待遇（たいぐう）パッケージ。
example:
A: The salary is okay, but the benefits package is fire.
B: Unlimited PTO?
A: How's the benefits package?
B: Mid. No dental.
A: I need a job with a good benefits package.
B: Same, health insurance is expensive.
vietnamese: Gói phúc lợi / Chế độ đãi ngộ
spanish: Paquete de beneficios / Prestaciones
chinese: 福利待遇 (Fúlì dàiyù)

vietnamese: Khó nói lắm / Chưa chắc đâu
example:
A: Will it rain?
B: Hard to say, the sky is weird.
A: Is he guilty?
B: Hard to say without proof.
A: Who's gonna win?
B: Hard to say, they're both good.
spanish: Difícil de decir / Está por ver
japanese: 何（なん）とも言（い）えない。／難（むずか）しいところだね。
chinese: 很难说 (Hěn nánshuō) / 说不准 (Shuōbuzhǔn)

primary: If you are in my shoes
example:
A: Just forgive him.
B: If you were in my shoes, you wouldn't say that.
A: What would you do if you were in my shoes?
B: I'd quit immediately.
A: Try to see it if you are in my shoes.
B: I'm trying, but it's hard.
vietnamese: Nếu cậu ở trong hoàn cảnh của tôi / Đặt mình vào vị trí tôi xem
spanish: Si estuvieras en mi lugar / Ponte en mi piel
japanese: あなたが私（わたし）の立場（たちば）だったら。／私（わたし）だったら。
chinese: 如果你是我 (Rúguǒ nǐ shì wǒ) / 换做是你 (Huànzuò shì nǐ)

spanish: Esto es prioritario / Esto va antes que nada
vietnamese: Cái này là ưu tiên số một / Việc này phải làm trước đã
japanese: これが最優先（さいゆうせん）だ。／何（なに）よりもこれが先（さき）。
example:
A: Should we start working on the presentation slides?
B: No, the client feedback must come first.
A: Right, this is the top priority for today.
B: Exactly, everything else takes a backseat for now.
A: Let's tackle this first then move on.
B: Good, it's at the top of my list too.
chinese: 这个得优先 (Zhège děi yōuxiān) / 这是当务之急 (Zhè shì dāngwùzhījí)

vietnamese: Nghe quen không? / Có nhớ ra gì không?
japanese: ピンとこない？／聞（き）き覚（おぼ）えない？
spanish: ¿Te suena? / ¿Te dice algo?
example:
A: The name Smith. Does it ring a bell?
B: Nope, drawing a blank.
A: That song rings a bell.
B: It's from TikTok.
A: Does this place ring a bell?
B: Yeah, we came here years ago.
chinese: 听着耳熟吗? (Tīngzhe ěrshú ma?) / 有印象吗? (Yǒu yìnxiàng ma?)

japanese: とっくにそうなるべきだった。／やっとか、って感（かん）じ。
vietnamese: Đáng lẽ phải làm từ lâu rồi / Trễ quá rồi
spanish: Ya era hora / Llevaba tiempo pendiente
example:
A: She finally dumped him.
B: That was long overdue.
A: We need a vacation.
B: Seriously, it's long overdue.
A: The promotion is long overdue.
B: You earned it.
chinese: 早就该这样了 (Zǎojiù gāi zhèyàng le)

vietnamese: Thời hoàng kim / Thời đỉnh cao
spanish: Época dorada / En sus mejores tiempos
japanese: 全盛期（ぜんせいき）。／黄金（おうごん）時代（じだい）。
example:
A: This place was poppin' in its heyday.
B: Now it's a ghost town.
A: In his heyday, he was unstoppable.
B: Age catches up to everyone.
A: I miss the heyday of hip hop.
B: 90s vibes forever.
chinese: 全盛时期 (Quánshèng shíqī) / 黄金时代 (Huángjīn shídài)

vietnamese: Không cần xin lỗi đâu / Có gì đâu mà xin lỗi
japanese: 謝（あやま）らなくていいよ。／気（き）にしないで。
spanish: No hace falta disculparse / No te preocupes, no pasa nada
example:
A: Sorry I'm late!
B: No apologies necessary, I just got here.
A: My bad for the mess.
B: No apologies necessary, let's just clean it.
A: I feel bad.
B: Don't. No apologies necessary.
chinese: 不用道歉 (Bùyòng dàoqiàn) / 没事儿 (Méishìr)

japanese: 大人（おとな）になれよ。／いい加減（かげん）にしろよ。
vietnamese: Lớn rồi, khôn lên đi / Đừng trẻ con nữa
example:
A: You're still playing pranks?
B: Grow up.
A: He needs to grow up.
B: He's 30 acting like 13.
A: Stop whining and grow up.
B: Harsh but true.
spanish: Madura un poco / Deja de comportarte como un crío
chinese: 成熟点 (Chéngshú diǎn) / 长大点吧 (Zhǎngdà diǎn ba)

spanish: Suele contestar rápido / No suele tardar en responder
primary: He is usually on top of responses
example:
A: Why hasn't he emailed back?
B: Weird, he is usually on top of responses.
A: I love that he texts back fast.
B: Yeah, he is usually on top of responses.
A: No reply in 3 days?
B: Unlike him, he is usually on top of responses.
vietnamese: Cậu ấy trả lời tin nhắn nhanh lắm / Cậu ấy check tin nhắn thường xuyên mà
meaning: 걔 답장 칼이야 / 연락 잘 돼
japanese: 彼（かれ）はいつも返事（へんじ）が早（はや）い。／彼（かれ）はマメだ。
chinese: 他回复通常很及时 (Tā huífù tōngcháng hěn jíshí)

spanish: Beneficio de la duda / Darle un voto de confianza
vietnamese: Tin tưởng lần này xem sao / Tạm tin đi
japanese: 善意（ぜんい）に解釈（かいしゃく）する。／信（しん）じてあげる。
meaning: 속는 셈 치고 믿어주기 / 일단 믿어줌
example:
A: I think he forgot.
B: Give him the benefit of the doubt.
A: She looks suspicious.
B: Let's give her the benefit of the doubt for now.
A: I stopped giving people the benefit of the doubt.
B: Cynical much?
chinese: 姑且信之 (Gūqiě xìn zhī) / 把人往好处想 (Bǎ rén wǎng hǎochù xiǎng)

spanish: Despejar la mente / Olvidarse de los problemas
japanese: 忘（わす）れる。／気晴（きば）らしをする。
vietnamese: Đừng bận tâm nữa / Quên đi cho nhẹ đầu
example:
A: Let's go out to take your mind off things.
B: Please, I need a distraction.
A: A movie will take your mind off work.
B: Good idea.
A: Exercise helps take my mind off stress.
B: Gym therapy.
chinese: 转移注意力 (Zhuǎnyí zhùyìlì) / 别想那事儿了 (Bié xiǎng nà shìr le)

spanish: Ponte en mi lugar / Ponte en mis zapatos
vietnamese: Thử đặt mình vào hoàn cảnh tôi đi / Nghĩ cho tôi chút đi
example:
A: You're overreacting.
B: Put yourself in my shoes! I lost my wallet.
A: Try to put yourself in his shoes.
B: I am, and he's still wrong.
A: If you put yourself in my shoes, you'd understand.
B: Maybe.
japanese: 私（わたし）の身（み）にもなってよ。／逆（ぎゃく）の立場（たちば）で考（かんガ）えて。
chinese: 你换位思考一下 (Nǐ huànwèisīkǎo yīxià) / 你站在我的立场想想 (Nǐ zhàn zài wǒ de lìchǎng xiǎngxiang)

example:
A: The cops are outside.
B: Keep a low profile.
A: He's famous but keeps a low profile.
B: Respect that.
A: I'm trying to keep a low profile at work.
B: Trying to avoid more work?
vietnamese: Kín tiếng chút đi / Đừng làm màu nữa
spanish: Pasar desapercibido / No llamar la atención
meaning: 존버해 / 눈에 띄지 말고 조용히 있어
japanese: 目立（めだ）たないようにする。／おとなしくしてる。
chinese: 保持低调 (Bǎochí dīdiào) / 别太张扬 (Bié tài zhāngyáng)

primary: You should focus on what you should do
spanish: Céntrate en lo tuyo / A lo tuyo
example:
A: I think Sarah is making a mistake with that client.
B: Honestly, you should focus on what you should do.
A: I’m just worried about the team's reputation.
B: Just stay in your lane and crush your own tasks.
A: You’re right, I should just do me for now.
B: Exactly, focus on your own grind and let her be.
vietnamese: Tập trung vào việc của mình đi / Lo chuyện của cậu trước đã
japanese: 自分（じぶん）のやるべきことに集中（しゅうちゅう）して。／余計（よけい）なお世話（せわ）だ。
similar: 2. Keep your eyes on your own paper
chinese: 你应该专注于你该做的事 (Nǐ yīnggāi zhuānzhù yú nǐ gāi zuò de shì)

vietnamese: Tôi trải qua rồi / Chuyện thường thôi, tôi lạ gì
example:
A: Want to go to Times Square?
B: Been there, done that. Too crowded.
A: Dating a musician?
B: Been there, done that. Never again.
A: Skydiving?
B: Been there, done that. It's okay.
spanish: Ya he pasado por eso / Me conozco esa historia
japanese: 経験（けいけん）済（ず）みだよ。／私（わたし）も通（とお）った道（みち）だ。
chinese: 过来人 (Guòláirén) / 早经历过了 (Zǎo jīnglì guò le)

vietnamese: Vui thôi mà / Làm cho vui thôi
japanese: 面白（おもしろ）半分（はんぶん）で。／ただのノリで。
spanish: Por las risas / Por diversión
example:
A: Why did you do it?
B: Just for the craic!
A: Let's go out for the craic.
B: I'm down for a good time.
A: He's great craic.
B: Life of the party.
chinese: 为了好玩 (Wèile hǎowán) / 图个乐呵 (Tú gè lèhē)

japanese: あれ、なんだっけ。／例（れい）のあれ。
example:
A: Pass me the... thingamajig.
B: The remote?
A: I need a thingamajig to fix this.
B: A screwdriver?
A: What's that thingamajig called?
B: No idea.
vietnamese: Cái rắc-co / Cái thứ gì gì đó (quên tên)
spanish: El coso ese / El chisme
chinese: 那个叫什么来着 (Nàge jiào shénme láizhe) / 那个东西 (Nàge dōngxi)

example:
A: I don't get it.
B: Walk me through your process.
A: Can you walk me through the app?
B: Sure, it's easy.
A: Walk me through what happened.
B: Okay, start from the beginning.
vietnamese: Giải thích từ đầu đuôi đi / Chỉ tớ từng bước một
spanish: Explícamelo paso a paso / Guíame
japanese: 具体的（ぐたいてき）に説明（せつめい）して。／手順（てじゅん）を教（おし）えて。
chinese: 给我讲讲 (Gěi wǒ jiǎngjiang) / 带我过一遍 (Dài wǒ guò yībiàn)

spanish: Dar la vuelta / Rotación
vietnamese: Doanh số / Sự luân chuyển
japanese: 売上（うりあげ）。／ひっくり返（かえ）す。
example:
A: Turn over the paper.
B: Done.
A: Turn over the keys.
B: Fine, take the car.
A: High staff turnover.
B: Bad management.
chinese: 移交 (Yíjiāo) / 翻过来 (Fān guòlái)

spanish: Sigue así / No te rindas
example:
A: It's so hard.
B: Keep at it, you'll get better.
A: I want to quit.
B: Nope, keep at it. Don't be weak.
A: Keep at it and you'll succeed.
B: Thanks for the motivation.
japanese: その調子（ちょうし）。／諦（あきら）めずに続（つづ）けて。
vietnamese: Cố lên, tiếp tục đi / Đừng bỏ cuộc
chinese: 坚持下去 (Jiānchí xiàqù) / 别松劲 (Bié sōngjìn)

japanese: 髪（かみ）やったんだ。／美容院（びよういん）行（い）ってきた。
spanish: Me he peinado / He ido a la peluquería
vietnamese: Tôi mới làm tóc đấy / Tóc tai mới tút tát lại
example:
A: You look different.
B: I got my hair done.
A: Love the color.
B: Thanks, just got my hair done.
A: Where do you get your hair done?
B: Place on 5th Ave.
chinese: 我做了头发 (Wǒ zuò le tóufa)

example:
A: Hey! It's been a minute.
B: Yeah, how have you been?
A: Haven't seen you in a minute.
B: I've been busy.
A: Let's catch up, it's been a minute.
B: For sure.
japanese: 久（ひさ）しぶりだね。／ちょっと間（あいだ）が空（あ）いたね。
vietnamese: Lâu lắm rồi mới gặp / Đã một thời gian rồi nhỉ
spanish: Hace un siglo / Hace tiempo que no nos vemos
chinese: 有阵子了 (Yǒu zhènzi le) / 过了好一会儿了 (Guò le hǎo yīhuìr le)

japanese: 超（ちょう）久（ひさ）しぶり。／何年（なんねん）ぶりだろう。
spanish: Hace una eternidad / Cuánto tiempo
meaning: 진짜 백만 년 만이다 / 너무 오랜만이야
vietnamese: Cả tỷ năm rồi mới gặp / Lâu lắm rồi không gặp
example:
A: It's been ages since we hung out.
B: Too long.
A: How is she? It's been ages.
B: She's good, married now.
A: This place hasn't changed in ages.
B: Still smells the same.
chinese: 好久了 (Hǎojiǔ le) / 八百年了 (Bābǎinián le)

japanese: 期待（きたい）を裏切（うらぎ）る。／ガッカリさせる。
example:
A: I’m so nervous about this presentation tomorrow.
B: Why? You’ve been working on it for weeks.
A: I just don't want to let the team down.
B: You won't, don't feel like you'll fail us.
A: I'm just scared I'll drop the ball at the last minute.
B: Relax, you’ve never left us hanging before.
vietnamese: Làm thất vọng / Phụ lòng
spanish: Defraudar a alguien / Dejar tirado a alguien
chinese: 让某人失望 (Ràng mǒurén shīwàng) / 放鸽子 (Fànggēzi)

example:
A: I skipped the intro.
B: Don't skip, pay attention.
A: Are you threatening me?
B: Just warning you, don't ignore it.
A: I'm listening now.
B: Good.
spanish: Te has saltado mucho / Te has pasado tres pueblos
japanese: 飛（と）ばしすぎだよ、聞（き）いてないと怒（おこ）るよ。／ちゃんと聞（き）かないと痛（いた）い目（め）見（み）るよ。
meaning: 너 너무 많이 건너뛰었어, 내 말 무시하면 혼날 줄 알아
primary: you skipped too much, don't forget I am going to punish you if you ignore what I said
vietnamese: Cậu lướt nhanh quá đấy, liệu hồn mà nghe lời tôi / Bỏ qua nhiều quá rồi, coi chừng tôi xử đấy
chinese: 你跳过太多了，别忘了你要是无视我说的话我可要惩罚你 (Nǐ tiàoguò tài duō le, bié wàngle nǐ yàoshi wúshì wǒ shuō de huà wǒ kě yào chéngfá nǐ)

japanese: 今（いま）の場所（ばしょ）に設定（せってい）する。／現在地（げんざいち）をマーク。
spanish: Fijar esta posición en '&&&ahora&&&&' / Marcar este punto como actual
example:
A: When did it happen?
B: Set position to now.
A: Marking this spot.
B: Copy that.
A: &&&now&&&&
B: Code for 'right now'.
vietnamese: Cài chỗ này thành 'ngay bây giờ' / Đặt mốc này là hiện tại
chinese: 把位置设为"&&&now&&&&" (Bǎ wèizhì shè wéi "&&&now&&&&")

spanish: Solucionar / Arreglar
japanese: 解決（かいけつ）する。／整理（せいり）する。
example:
A: Need to sort out my life.
B: Start with your room.
A: Sort out the files.
B: On it.
A: Did you sort it out with him?
B: Yeah, we're good.
vietnamese: Giải quyết xong xuôi / Sắp xếp ổn thỏa
chinese: 解决 (Jiějué) / 理清 (Lǐqīng)

japanese: 気晴（きば）らしをする。／嫌（いや）なことを忘（わす）れる。
spanish: Desconectar / Dejar de pensar en ello
example:
A: Gaming helps take my mind off things.
B: Same.
A: I need a vacation to take my mind off things.
B: Book it.
A: Just breathe.
B: Trying to take my mind off the panic.
meaning: 잡생각 좀 없애다 / 머리 좀 식히다
vietnamese: Giải tỏa đầu óc / Tìm niềm vui để quên sầu
chinese: 散散心 (Sànsanxīn) /不想那些烦心事 (Bù xiǎng nàxiē fánxīnshì)

example:
A: Did you get the joke?
B: Nope, went over my head.
A: The lecture went over my head.
B: Too technical.
A: Explain it simply, don't go over my head.
B: Okay, 5 year old version.
vietnamese: Cậu nói cao siêu quá / Tôi chẳng hiểu gì sất
japanese: 難（むずか）しすぎてわからなかった。／チンプンカンプンだった。
meaning: 내 머리 꼭대기에 있네 / 이해가 안 돼
spanish: Se me escapa / No me entero de nada
chinese: 你说得太深奥了 (Nǐ shuō de tài shēn'ào le) / 我没听懂 (Wǒ méi tīngdǒng)

vietnamese: Quá tầm với tôi / Ngoài khả năng của tôi
example:
A: Can you fix the car?
B: This is way over my head.
A: The math is over my head.
B: Use a calculator.
A: I'm in over my head with this lie.
B: Come clean.
spanish: Me supera / Es demasiado para mí
meaning: 내 능력 밖이야 / 무슨 말인지 모르겠어
japanese: 私（わたし）の手（て）には負（お）えない。／理解（りかい）不能（ふのう）だ。
chinese: 太难懂了 (Tài nándǒng le) / 超出我的理解范围 (Chāochū wǒ de lǐjiě fànwéi)

japanese: 五分（ごぶ）五分（ごぶ）。／引（ひ）き分（わ）け。
spanish: Empatados / A la par
vietnamese: Huề nhau / Ngang tài ngang sức
example:
A: What's the score?
B: Tied or even.
A: I paid for lunch, you paid for dinner.
B: We're even.
A: It's a tie.
B: Sudden death round?
chinese: 打平了 (Dǎpíng le) / 不分胜负 (Bùfēnshèngfù)

vietnamese: Huề cả làng / Sòng phẳng nhé
example:
A: Here's your $20.
B: Cool, we're Even Steven.
A: I hit you, you hit me.
B: Even Steven.
A: Split the bill?
B: Yeah, Even Steven.
spanish: Estamos en paz / Cuentas saldadas
japanese: チャラにする。／これで貸（か）し借（か）りなし。
chinese: 互不相欠 (Hùbùxiāngqiàn) / 扯平了 (Chěpíng le)

japanese: 言（い）ってみて。／聞（き）かせて。
example:
A: I have news.
B: Lay it on me.
A: It's bad.
B: Just lay it on me, I can take it.
A: Want to hear my idea?
B: Lay it on me.
spanish: Suéltalo / Cuéntamelo todo
vietnamese: Nói nghe xem nào / Kể đi tớ nghe
chinese: 直说吧 (Zhíshuō ba) / 告诉我吧 (Gàosu wǒ ba)

vietnamese: Tính tiền cho tôi / Thanh toán giúp tôi
spanish: Cóbrame / Hazme la cuenta
example:
A: Find everything okay?
B: Yes, ring me up.
A: Taking these.
B: I'll ring you up over here.
A: Can you ring me up quickly?
B: Sure thing.
japanese: お会計（かいけい）お願（ねが）い。／レジ打（う）って。
chinese: 结账 (Jiézhàng) / 给...打电话 (Gěi... dǎ diànhuà)

vietnamese: Không cùng đẳng cấp với mình đâu / Quá tầm với chúng ta
japanese: 高嶺（たかね）の花（はな）だ。／私（わたし）たちにはレベルが高（たか）すぎる。
spanish: Fuera de nuestro alcance / Nos queda grande
example:
A: Should we try to bid on that massive Manhattan project?
B: Honestly, I think that’s out of our league.
A: But the potential profit is huge if we win.
B: It’s definitely out of our pay grade right now.
A: You think it's way beyond us at this stage?
B: Yeah, let's stick to what we actually know.
chinese: 高攀不起 (Gāopānbùqǐ) / 配不上 (Pèibùshàng)

example:
A: You okay?
B: Having a mental breakdown, hbu?
A: This week gave me a mental breakdown.
B: Need a drink?
A: Don't have a mental breakdown over a quiz.
B: I'm trying not to!
vietnamese: Tôi suy sụp mất thôi / Tôi sắp điên rồi
spanish: Estoy al borde del colapso / Me va a dar algo
japanese: メンタル崩壊（ほうかい）しそう。／頭（あたま）がおかしくなりそう。
chinese: 我崩溃了 (Wǒ bēngkuì le) / 我心态崩了 (Wǒ xīntài bēng le)

japanese: 先伸（さきの）ばしにする。／グズグズする。
example:
A: Start the essay?
B: No, I love to procrastinate.
A: Stop procrastinating.
B: I work better under pressure.
A: Procrastination is the thief of time.
B: Deep.
vietnamese: Nước đến chân mới nhảy / Trì hoãn
spanish: Dejarlo para luego / Procrastinar
chinese: 拖延 (Tuōyán) / 磨蹭 (Móceng)

japanese: ずっと気（き）になってた。／頭（あたま）から離（はな）れなかった。
example:
A: You called?
B: Yeah, you've been on my mind.
A: That song has been on my mind all day.
B: Earworm.
A: It's been on my mind to visit.
B: Come through.
vietnamese: Tôi cứ suy nghĩ mãi / Cứ lấn cấn trong lòng
spanish: No me lo quito de la cabeza / Le he estado dando vueltas
chinese: 一直在我心头 (Yīzhí zài wǒ xīntóu) / 一直挂念着 (Yīzhí guàniànzhe)

japanese: 損得（そんとく）を考（かんガ）える。／メリットとデメリットを秤（はかり）にかける。
vietnamese: Cân đo đong đếm / Tính toán thiệt hơn
example:
A: Should I move?
B: Weigh the pros and cons first.
A: Making a list of pros and cons.
B: Smart.
A: We weighed the pros and cons and said no.
B: Fair enough.
spanish: Sopesar los pros y los contras / Valorar lo bueno y lo malo
chinese: 权衡利弊 (Quánhéng lìbì)

japanese: 立（た）て直（なお）す。／軌道（きどう）修正（しゅうせい）する。
spanish: Volver a la normalidad / Reconducir la situación
example:
A: We're behind schedule.
B: Let's get things back on track.
A: My life is a mess.
B: Time to get back on track.
A: Focus, get back on track.
B: Roger that.
vietnamese: Đưa mọi thứ về quỹ đạo / Chỉnh đốn lại mọi việc
chinese: 让一切重回正轨 (Ràng yīqiè chóng huí zhèngguǐ)

spanish: ¿Qué te carcome? / ¿Qué te ronda por la cabeza?
meaning: 무슨 일 있어? / 왜 그렇게 꽁해 있어?
example:
A: You look sad. What's eating you?
B: Just tired.
A: Tell me what's eating you.
B: Work stress.

chinese: 怎么样? (Zěnmeyàng?) / 还好吗? (Hái hǎo ma?)

example:

B: Perfect. See you there around 9.

example:

B: Definitely. Drink one for me.

meaning: 아쉽지만 나 못 가. 일이 생겼어.
chinese: 我去不了 (Wǒ qù bù liǎo) / 我赶不上了 (Wǒ gǎnbùshàng le)

vietnamese: Trong cái rủi có cái may / Tái ông thất mã
spanish: No hay mal que por bien no venga / Una bendición disfrazada
japanese: 不幸（ふこう）中（ちゅう）の幸（さい）い。／結果（けっか）オーライ。

example:

B: Maybe it's a blessing in disguise .

B: See? Everything happens for a reason.
chinese: 塞翁失马焉知非福 (Sàiwēngshīmǎ yānzhīfēifú) / 因祸得福 (Yīnhuòdéfú)

example:

B: Thanks for understanding. Next week is on me.

B: Your call. I trust your taste.

chinese: 由你决定 (Yóu nǐ juédìng) / 听你的 (Tīng nǐ de)

similar: When do they start letting people on?

similar: Expected departure time still the same?

example:

B: Looks like it. No red text yet.

B: You should be fine. The weather looks clear.
A: Fingers crossed. Hate missing connections.
B: Yean, sleeping in the airport is not the vibe.

chinese: 航班准点吗? (Hángbān zhǔndiǎn ma?)

similar: Any chance for a window spot?

I'd prefer a window if possible.
Put me by the window please.
primary: Can I get a window seat?
example:
A: Checking in for JFK. Can I get a window seat?
B: Let me check... Yeah, 14A is open.
A: Perfect. I need to lean against the wall to sleep.
B: I feel that. Aisle seats are too busy.
A: Exactly. Getting bumped by the cart is the worst.
B: Here's your boarding pass. Enjoy the view.
vietnamese: Cho tôi ngồi cạnh cửa sổ được không? / Có ghế sát cửa sổ không?

chinese: 能给我个靠窗的座位吗? (Néng gěi wǒ gè kàochuāng de zuòwèi ma?)

How long are we in the air?
spanish: ¿Cuánto dura el vuelo? / ¿Cuánto tiempo de vuelo es?

example:

example:

B: Go for it. It's pretty clean too.

spanish: ¿Dónde está el baño más cercano? / ¿El servicio, por favor?
vietnamese: Nhà vệ sinh gần nhất ở đâu? / Toilet đâu nhỉ?
chinese: 最近的洗手间在哪儿? (Zuìjìn de xǐshǒujiān zài nǎr?)

example:

example:

B: Yikes. You really dodged a bullet breaking up with him.

chinese: 临时抱佛脚 (Línshí bàofójiǎo) / 突击复习 (Tūjī fùxí)

example:

B: Probably wise, I woke up feeling trashed.

example:

B: I hate the back seat. Makes me carsick.

A: Fine, but I pick the music.
B: Deal. Just drive safely.

example:

B: I hit the jackpot. Great pay, chill boss.
A: That's the dream. You deserve it.
B: Thanks. I finally feel appreciated.
A: First round is on you then?
B: Absolutely.

vietnamese: No bụng đói con mắt / Tham ăn quá đà

example:

B: I know. My eyes were bigger than my stomach.

B: Everything looked so good on the menu.
A: Leftovers for days I guess.
B: I'm not complaining.

example:

B: I wish I could, but I have too much on my plate right now.

A: Catch you when you come up for air.
B: Thanks for understanding.

vietnamese: Tôi bận tối mắt tối mũi / Việc ngập đầu rồi
chinese: 我实在是太忙了 (Wǒ shízài shì tài máng le) / 我忙不过来了 (Wǒ máng bù guòlái le)

example:

B: Don't leave out any details.

chinese: 背刺 (Bèicì) / 拿人挡枪 (Ná rén dǎngqiāng)

example:
