chinese: 拜金女 (Bàijīnnǚ)

vietnamese: Cậu chỉ được cái mồm / Nói thì hay lắm
spanish: Eres puro bla bla bla / Mucho ruido y pocas nueces
japanese: 口（くち）だけだね。／言（い）うだけ番長（ばんちょう）。
example:
A: I'm going to start a business next month.
B: You said that last year. You're all talk.
A: This time is different.
B: Show me, don't tell me.
A: Watch me succeed.
B: I'm waiting.
chinese: 光说不练 (Guāngshuōbùliàn) / 嘴炮 (Zuǐpào)

vietnamese: Tôi đồng ý hai tay / Duyệt luôn
japanese: 賛成（さんせい）。／それに一票（いっぴょう）。
spanish: Me parece bien / Estoy de acuerdo
example:
A: Let's order pizza for lunch.
B: I go along with that.
A: Pepperoni?
B: Always.
A: Done and done.
B: Best decision today.
chinese: 我同意 (Wǒ tóngyì) / 我赞成 (Wǒ zànchéng)

example:
A: Can you help me move this couch?
B: Sorry, my hands are full with the baby.
A: Ah, right.
B: Give me ten minutes.
A: No rush.
B: I'll be there soon.
spanish: Estoy a tope / No doy abasto
similar: Have a lot on my plate.
japanese: 手（て）が離（はな）せない。／手（て）一杯（いっぱい）だ。
vietnamese: Tôi đang tối mắt tối mũi đây / Bận túi bụi rồi
chinese: 我忙得不可开交 (Wǒ máng de bùkěkāijiāo)

spanish: Pequeño choque / Golpe sin importancia
japanese: 軽（かる）い接触（せっしょく）事故（じこ）。／コツンとぶつかった。
example:
A: You're late.
B: I got into a fender bender.
A: Oh no, are you okay?
B: Yeah, just a scratch on the bumper.
A: That's annoying.
B: Tell me about it. Insurance is gonna go up.
vietnamese: Va quẹt nhẹ / Đụng xe nhẹ
chinese: 小剐蹭 (Xiǎo guǎcèng)

vietnamese: Không thể lười biếng được / Giờ không phải lúc chơi bời
primary: I can’t afford to slack off
japanese: サボってる余裕（よゆう）はない。／気（き）を抜（ぬ）けない。
spanish: No puedo permitirme el lujo de vaguear / Tengo que ponerme las pilas
similar: 1. I've gotta stay on the grind
4. I need to stay locked in
example:
A: Want to go to that new club tonight?
B: Man, I can’t afford to slack off right now.
A: It’s just one night, you need a break.
B: My finals are next week, I gotta stay on the grind.
A: I get it, you really need to stay locked in.
B: Exactly, I can't fall behind at this stage.
chinese: 我可不敢偷懒 (Wǒ kě bùgǎn tōulǎn)

vietnamese: Tùy cơ ứng biến / Tới đâu hay tới đó
example:
A: What's the plan for dinner?
B: Let's just play it by ear.
A: I hate uncertainty.
B: Be spontaneous.
A: I need a reservation.
B: Live a little.
japanese: 出（で）たとこ勝負（しょうぶ）でいこう。／流（なが）れに任（まか）せよう。
spanish: Improvisar / Ver sobre la marcha
chinese: 见机行事 (Jiànjīxíngshì) / 看着办 (Kànzhe bàn)

spanish: Mira quién fue a hablar / Le dijo la sartén al cazo
example:
A: You are such a messy eater.
B: Takes one to know one. Look at your shirt.
A: Oops.
B: Exactly.
A: We can't take us anywhere.
B: Nope.
japanese: お互（たが）い様（さま）でしょ。／自分（じぶん）はどうなの。
vietnamese: Ngưu tầm ngưu, mã tầm mã / Nhìn lại mình đi
chinese: 彼此彼此 (Bǐcǐ bǐcǐ) / 你也一样 (Nǐ yě yīyàng)

vietnamese: Truyền miệng / Lời đồn
example:
A: How did you find this restaurant?
B: By word of mouth. It's famous among locals.
A: The food is incredible.
B: Best kept secret in town.
A: Not anymore.
B: Shh, don't tweet it.
japanese: 口（くち）コミで。／人（ひと）づてに。
spanish: Por el boca a boca / De oídas
chinese: 口碑 (Kǒubēi) / 口口相传 (Kǒukǒuxiāngchuán)

japanese: 吐（は）き気（け）がする。／気持（きも）ち悪（わる）い。
example:
A: Are you okay? You look pale.
B: I feel nauseous. I shouldn't have eaten that sushi.
A: Go to the bathroom.
B: Way ahead of you.
A: Do you need water?
B: I need a bucket.
spanish: Me siento mareado / Tengo náuseas
vietnamese: Buồn nôn quá / Muốn ói
chinese: 我想吐 (Wǒ xiǎng tù) / 我有点恶心 (Wǒ yǒudiǎn ěxin)

vietnamese: Thở cái đã / Lấy hơi đã
japanese: 息（いき）を整（ととの）える。／一休（ひとやす）みさせて。
example:
A: Hurry up! We're almost there.
B: Wait, let me catch my breath.
A: You're so out of shape.
B: I just ran up ten flights of stairs!
A: Excuses.
B: Shut up.
spanish: Recuperar el aliento / Tomar aire
chinese: 喘口气 (Chuǎn kǒu qì)

example:
A: Why are you limping?
B: Breaking in new shoes. Blisters everywhere.
A: Use bandaids.
B: I ran out.
A: Beauty is pain.
B: Not worth it.
spanish: Estrenando zapatos / Domando los zapatos
vietnamese: Đi cho mềm giày / Đang tập đi giày mới
japanese: 靴（くつ）慣（な）らし中（ちゅう）。／新（あたら）しい靴（くつ）だから。
chinese: 磨合新鞋 (Móhé xīn xié)

example:
A: I can't stop thinking about the exam.
B: You need to take your mind off it.
A: How?
B: Let's go watch a movie.
A: Maybe a horror movie?
B: Perfect distraction.
vietnamese: Quên hết đi / Đừng nghĩ ngợi gì nữa
japanese: 気（き）を紛（まぎ）らわせる。／忘（わす）れる。
spanish: Distraerse / Olvidar las penas
chinese: 转移注意力 (Zhuǎnyí zhùyìlì)

japanese: 冷（つめ）たくあしらう。／無視（むし）する。
spanish: Hacer el vacío / Ignorar olímpicamente
vietnamese: Bơ đẹp / Lạnh nhạt
example:
A: Is she mad at you?
B: Yeah, she's giving me the cold shoulder.
A: What did you do?
B: I ate her yogurt.
A: You deserve it.
B: It was expired!
chinese: 冷落 (Lěngluò) / 不理睬 (Bù lǐcǎi)

japanese: 気（き）にしてないよ。／悪（わる）く思（おも）ってないよ。
vietnamese: Không sao đâu / Tôi không để bụng đâu
spanish: No te preocupes / No hay problema
example:
A: I didn't mean to sound rude.
B: None taken. I know you're stressed.
A: Thanks for understanding.
B: We've all been there.
A: You're a saint.
B: I try.
chinese: 没事 (Méishì) / 我没在意 (Wǒ méi zàiyì)

vietnamese: Lẽ ra phải tính kỹ hơn / Hối hận thì muộn rồi
example:
A: I bought a drum set.
B: You live in a studio apartment.
A: I should have thought that through.
B: Your neighbors are going to kill you.
A: I'll play softly?
B: Impossible.
spanish: Debí haberlo pensado mejor / Me precipité
japanese: よく考（かんガ）えればよかった。／軽率（けいそつ）だった。
chinese: 早该想清楚的 (Zǎo gāi xiǎng qīngchu de)

example:
A: I had a huge fight with my sister today.
B: I'm sorry to hear that. I don’t want to pry, but...
A: It's okay, I just need to vent a little.
B: Not trying to get in your business, but is she okay?
A: She’s just being difficult as usual.
B: Understood, I'll stay out of it then.
vietnamese: Không phải tôi tọc mạch đâu / Tôi không muốn soi mói
similar: 1. Not trying to get in your business
2. I'm not trying to poke around
japanese: 詮索（せんさく）したくないけど。／根掘（ねほ）り葉掘（はほ）り聞（き）きたくないけど。
spanish: No quiero ser cotilla / No quiero meterme donde no me llaman
chinese: 我不想打听隐私 (Wǒ bù xiǎng dǎting yǐnsī)

similar: Trick someone into doing your work.
vietnamese: Đùn đẩy việc cho người khác / Khôn lỏi bắt người khác làm
japanese: うまく乗（の）せられた。／人（ひと）に使（つか）い道（みち）を教（おし）えられた。
example:
A: Did you just make me clean your entire room?
B: You said you liked organizing!
A: You totally pulled a Tom Sawyer on me.
B: Hey, it looks great though.
A: You owe me dinner.
B: Deal.
spanish: Engañar a alguien para que haga tu trabajo / Liar a otro
chinese: 忽悠别人替自己干活 (Hūyou biérén tì zìjǐ gànhuó) (Literally: Pull a Tom Sawyer)

spanish: Nada que perder / Por probar que no quede
example:
A: Should I apply for that job?
B: Do it. Nothing to lose.
A: But I'm underqualified.
B: Let them decide that.
A: Okay, applying.
B: Good attitude.
vietnamese: Chẳng mất gì cả / Cứ thử đi có mất gì đâu
japanese: 失（うし）なうものは何（なに）もない。／ダメ元（もと）で。
chinese: 没什么可失去的 (Méi shénme kě shīqù de) / 大不了就这样 (Dàbùliǎo jiù zhèyàng)

japanese: 目（め）で食（た）べてる。／取（と）りすぎだよ。
vietnamese: No bụng đói con mắt / Tham ăn
spanish: Comiste con los ojos / Se te fueron los ojos
primary: Eyes are bigger than your stomach
similar: Bit off more than you can chew.
example:
A: I can't finish this steak.
B: Your eyes were bigger than your stomach.
A: It looked smaller on the menu.
B: Doggy bag?
A: Please.
B: I warned you.
chinese: 眼大肚子小 (Yǎn dà dùzi xiǎo)

spanish: Las palabras se las lleva el viento / Del dicho al hecho hay un trecho
similar: Put your money where your mouth is.
japanese: 言（い）うのは簡単（かんたん）。／口（くち）ではなんとでも言（い）える。
example:
A: I'm going to run a marathon next year.
B: Talk is cheap. Start training.
A: I bought the shoes.
B: That's the easy part.
A: Watch me.
B: I'm watching.
vietnamese: Nói thì dễ / Làm mới khó
chinese: 说得轻巧 (Shuō de qīngqiǎo)

vietnamese: Hoàn hảo mọi mặt / Đẹp người đẹp nết
japanese: パーフェクトだ。／全（すべ）てを持（も）ってる。
example:
A: He's smart, funny, and rich.
B: He's the whole package.
A: And he cooks.
B: Marry him.
A: We just met.
B: Did I stutter?
spanish: Lo tiene todo / Es el pack completo
chinese: 完美的对象 (Wánměi de duìxiàng) / 啥都有 (Shá dōu yǒu)

vietnamese: Làm theo linh tính đi / Nghe con tim mách bảo
example:
A: I don't know which one to pick.
B: Follow your gut.
A: My gut says 'both'.
B: Your gut is greedy.
A: It's wise.
B: It's expensive.
japanese: 直感（ちょっかん）に従（したが）って。／ピンときた方（ほう）で。
spanish: Sigue tu instinto / Haz caso a tu corazonada
chinese: 跟着感觉走 (Gēnzhe gǎnjué zǒu) / 听从直觉 (Tīngcóng zhíjué)

japanese: ドキドキしてる。／胸（むね）が高鳴（たかな）ってる。
spanish: Me palpita el corazón / Tengo mariposas en el estómago
vietnamese: Tim đập thình thịch / Xao xuyến quá
example:
A: He just smiled at me.
B: And?
A: My heart is fluttering!
B: You are obsessed.
A: He's so cute.
B: Go talk to him.
chinese: 我心怦怦跳 (Wǒ xīn pēngpēng tiào) / 我小鹿乱撞 (Wǒ xiǎolùluànzhuàng)

spanish: Los sentimientos se apagan / La chispa se pierde
vietnamese: Tình cảm phai nhạt / Hết yêu rồi
japanese: 気持（きも）ちが冷（さ）める。／熱（ねつ）が冷（さ）める。
example:
A: Do you still love him?
B: My feelings have faded.
A: That happens.
B: We're just friends now.
A: Better than enemies.
B: True.
chinese: 感情淡了 (Gǎnqíng dàn le)

spanish: Deja de restregármelo / Ya vale de regodearse
japanese: しつこいよ。／もう言（い）わなくていいよ。
example:
A: Remember when you tripped on stage?
B: Stop rubbing it in!
A: It was so funny.
B: It was traumatic.
A: Viral moment.
B: I hate you.
vietnamese: Đừng có xát muối nữa / Biết rồi đừng nói mãi
chinese: 别显摆了 (Bié xiǎnbai le) / 别往伤口上撒盐了 (Bié wǎng shāngkǒu shàng sǎyán le)

japanese: それが証拠（しょうこ）だよ。／よくわかるでしょ。
example:
A: He failed school but is now a billionaire.
B: It just goes to show that grades aren't everything.
A: Or he got lucky.
B: Hard work counts too.
A: Sure.
B: Don't drop out though.
spanish: Eso demuestra que / Para que veas
vietnamese: Thế mới thấy là / Điều đó chứng tỏ
chinese: 这就证明了 (Zhè jiù zhèngmíng le) / 可见 (Kějiàn)

example:
A: I don't think I can finish this marathon.
B: Come on, you're almost there! Mind over matter.
A: My legs feel like lead right now.
B: Just focus on the mindset and keep moving.
A: I guess I just have to power through it.
B: That’s the spirit! Willpower is everything.
vietnamese: Ý chí quyết định tất cả / Có chí thì nên
spanish: El poder de la mente / Todo está en la cabeza
japanese: 気（き）の持（も）ちよう。／精神（せいしん）力（りょく）だよ。
chinese: 精神战胜物质 (Jīngshén zhànshèng wùzhì) / 意志力 (Yìzhìlì)

japanese: アイデアを聞（き）いてほしい。／壁打（かべう）ちさせて。
meaning: 아이디어 좀 공유하고 싶어. 의견 좀 줘.
vietnamese: Tham khảo ý kiến chút / Trao đổi ý tưởng với cậu
spanish: Intercambiar ideas contigo / Comentar unas ideas contigo
example:
A: Hey, can I bounce some ideas off you?
B: Sure, what are you working on?
A: Just a new marketing pitch.
B: Lay it on me.
A: So, imagine this...
B: I'm listening.
chinese: 跟你碰碰想法 (Gēn nǐ pèngpeng xiǎngfǎ) / 跟你商量一下 (Gēn nǐ shāngliang yīxià)

example:
A: Why do you like this club so much?
B: It gives me a sense of belonging.
A: Like a second family?
B: Exactly. Everyone gets me here.
A: That's really special.
B: You should join too.
spanish: Sentido de pertenencia / Sentirse parte de algo
vietnamese: Cảm giác thuộc về / Cảm giác thân thuộc
japanese: 居場所（いばしょ）。／所属（しょぞく）感（かん）。
chinese: 归属感 (Guīshǔgǎn)

example:
A: You look relaxed.
B: I decided to pamper myself today.
A: Spa day?
B: Massage, facial, the works.
A: I'm jealous.
B: You should come next time.
vietnamese: Nuông chiều bản thân chút / Tự thưởng cho mình
meaning: 나를 위한 사치. 호강 좀 하다.
spanish: Mimarme / Darme un capricho
japanese: 自分（じぶん）へのご褒美（ほうび）。／贅沢（ぜいたく）する。
chinese: 犒劳自己 (Kàoláo zìjǐ)

example:
A: Are we getting dessert?
B: I'm going to indulge myself tonight.
A: Chocolate cake?
B: And ice cream. Don't judge.
A: I support this decision.
B: Diet starts tomorrow.
vietnamese: Xả láng một bữa / Tự thưởng
spanish: Mimarme / Darme un gusto
japanese: 思（おも）う存分（ぞんぶん）楽（たの）しむ。／好（す）きなことする。
chinese: 放纵自己 (Fàngzòng zìjǐ) / 对自己好点 (Duì zìjǐ hǎo diǎn)

japanese: ボロすぎ。／みすぼらしい。
vietnamese: Tồi tàn quá / Xập xệ quá
example:
A: What do you think of this apartment?
B: Honestly? It's too shabby.
A: The rent is cheap though.
B: There's a reason for that.
A: I can fix it up.
B: You're brave.
spanish: Está hecho polvo / Es muy cutre
chinese: 太寒酸了 (Tài hánsuān le) / 太破旧了 (Tài pòjiù le)

vietnamese: Cũng ra gì đấy / Không tệ chút nào
example:
A: I finished the marathon in 4 hours.
B: Not too shabby for your first time.
A: My legs are killing me though.
B: You should be proud.
A: Beer me.
B: Coming right up.
japanese: 悪（わる）くないね。／なかなかイケる。
spanish: Nada mal / No está nada mal
chinese: 还不赖 (Hái bùlài) / 挺好的 (Tǐng hǎo de)

spanish: Suena cursi / Suena ñoño
example:
A: Can I say something?
B: Go ahead.
A: It sounds corny, but you complete me.
B: Aww, that is corny. But I love it.
A: I mean it.
B: I know you do.
vietnamese: Nghe sến sẩm quá / Nghe hơi cải lương
japanese: キザに聞（き）こえるかもだけど。／ベタだけど。
chinese: 听起来很老土 (Tīngqǐlái hěn lǎotǔ) / 听起来很肉麻 (Tīngqǐlái hěn ròumá)

vietnamese: Nghe thì có vẻ hiển nhiên / Biết rồi khổ lắm nói mãi
japanese: 当（あ）たり前（まえ）に聞（き）こえるかもだけど。／言（い）わずもがなだけど。
example:
A: Any advice for the test?
B: It might sound obvious, but get good sleep.
A: I was planning to pull an all-nighter.
B: Don't. Your brain needs rest.
A: You're probably right.
B: Trust me on this.
spanish: Puede parecer obvio / Suena de cajón
chinese: 这可能听起来很明显 (Zhè kěnéng tīngqǐlái hěn míngxiǎn) / 这不是废话吗 (Zhè bùshì fèihuà ma)

spanish: Mira quién habla / Mira quién fue a hablar
example:
A: You need to stop eating junk food.
B: Look who's talking! You're eating a burger.
A: This is a protein burger.
B: Whatever helps you sleep at night.
A: Shut up and pass the fries.
B: Hypocrite.
japanese: どの口（くち）が言（い）う。／お前（まえ）が言（い）うな。
vietnamese: Nhìn lại mình đi / Lại còn nói người khác
chinese: 瞧瞧谁在说话 (Qiáoqiao shéi zài shuōhuà) / 也不看看你自己 (Yě bù kànkan nǐ zìjǐ)

vietnamese: Đang gấp lắm / Thời gian gấp gáp
example:
A: Do you have a minute to chat?
B: I'm a little pressed for time right now.
A: It's urgent.
B: Okay, walk with me.
A: I'll be quick.
B: Shoot.
japanese: 時間（じかん）がないんだ。／急（いそ）いでる。
spanish: Voy con la hora pegada / Ando mal de tiempo
chinese: 时间紧迫 (Shíjiān jǐnpò) / 赶时间 (Gǎnshíjiān)

example:
A: Do you think they'll find out about the mistake?
B: Honestly, it's just a matter of time.
A: Maybe we can fix it before they notice.
B: It’s bound to happen, the audit starts tomorrow.
A: I guess it’s inevitable then.
B: Yeah, it’s just a waiting game now.
spanish: Cuestión de tiempo / Es solo esperar
japanese: 時間（じかん）の問題（もんだい）だ。／そのうちそうなる。
vietnamese: Sớm muộn gì cũng xảy ra / Vấn đề thời gian thôi
chinese: 时间问题 (Shíjiān wèntí) / 迟早的事 (Chízǎo de shì)

example:
A: I haven't started the essay yet.
B: Dude, the clock is ticking.
A: I work better under pressure.
B: You're gonna regret this.
A: I'll be fine.
B: Don't call me when you're crying.
japanese: 時間（じかん）が迫（せま）ってる。／チクタクいってるよ。
spanish: El tiempo corre / Se acaba el tiempo
vietnamese: Thời gian không chờ ai đâu / Nhanh lên kẻo muộn
chinese: 时间不多了 (Shíjiān bùduō le) / 没时间了 (Méi shíjiān le)

example:
A: How do I get to the airport fast?
B: Your best bet is the express train.
A: Is a taxi faster?
B: Not with this traffic.
A: Train it is then.
B: Safe travels.
japanese: それが一番（いちばん）確実（かくじつ）だ。／ベストな選択（せんたく）だ。
vietnamese: Cách tốt nhất là / Chỉ còn nước này thôi
spanish: Tu mejor opción / Lo mejor que puedes hacer
chinese: 你最好的选择 (Nǐ zuìhǎo de xuǎnzé) / 最好...... (Zuìhǎo......)

spanish: No soy persona hoy / Hoy no estoy católico
meaning: 오늘 컨디션이 별로야. 내가 아닌 것 같아.
japanese: いつもの自分（じぶん）じゃない。／調子（ちょうし）狂（くる）う。
vietnamese: Hôm nay tôi cứ sao sao ấy / Người ngợm chán quá
example:
A: You seem quiet today.
B: Yeah, I'm not myself today.
A: Is everything okay?
B: Just tired and headachey.
A: Go home and rest.
B: I wish I could.
chinese: 我今天不在状态 (Wǒ jīntiān bù zài zhuàngtài)

spanish: Espabila / Céntrate
example:
A: I can't stop crying about him.
B: Girl, get it together. He's trash.
A: But I miss him.
B: You're better than this.
A: I know, I know.
B: Let's go dance it off.
vietnamese: Tỉnh táo lại đi / Chấn chỉnh lại đi
japanese: しっかりして。／シャンとしろ。
chinese: 振作起来 (Zhènzuò qǐlái) / 搞搞清楚 (Gǎogǎo qīngchu)

japanese: 現実（げんじつ）を見（み）て。／夢（ゆめ）から覚（さ）めて。
spanish: Baja de las nubes / Pon los pies en la tierra
example:
A: I'm going to become a billionaire by next year.
B: You need to come back down to earth.
A: You just don't believe in me.
B: I believe in math, and yours doesn't add up.
A: Watch me.
B: I'll be watching.
vietnamese: Trở về thực tại đi / Đừng mơ mộng nữa
chinese: 别做白日梦了 (Bié zuò báirìmèng le) / 脚踏实地点 (Jiǎotàshídì diǎn)

spanish: Se acercan las fechas límite / Los plazos se echan encima
japanese: 締（し）め切（き）りが迫（せま）ってる。／ヤバい。
example:
A: Want to grab a drink?
B: Can't. Deadlines are looming.
A: Just one drink.
B: I wish. I have to work late.
A: Good luck.
B: Pray for me.
vietnamese: Sắp đến hạn chót rồi / Deadline dí sát đít rồi
chinese: 截止日期快到了 (Jiézhǐ rìqī kuài dào le) / 马上到期了 (Mǎshàng dàoqī le)

example:
A: How's the new role?
B: Intense. I'm juggling a lot right now.
A: Don't burn out.
B: Trying not to. Coffee helps.
A: Take breaks.
B: I'll try.
vietnamese: Đang bù đầu rối tóc / Làm trăm công nghìn việc
spanish: Haciendo malabares con todo / Tengo mil cosas a la vez
japanese: いろいろ抱（かか）え込（こ）んでる。／てんてこ舞（まい）だ。
meaning: 정신없이 바빠. 여러 가지 일을 동시에 하고 있어.
chinese: 忙得团团转 (Máng de tuántuánzhuàn) / 分身乏术 (Fēnshēnfáshù)

japanese: アウトドア派（は）。／外（そと）が好（す）き。
spanish: De aire libre / Amante de la naturaleza
example:
A: Are you outdoorsy?
B: Not really. I prefer AC.
A: Oh, I was going to invite you camping.
B: Hard pass. Bugs and I don't mix.
A: Hotel it is then.
B: Now you're speaking my language.
vietnamese: Thích ra ngoài trời / Mê thiên nhiên
chinese: 户外型 (Hùwàixíng) / 喜欢户外活动的 (Xǐhuan hùwài huódòng de)

spanish: ¿Me pillas? / ¿Me entiendes?
meaning: 내 말 무슨 뜻인지 알지? (눈치 줌)
similar: Pick up what I'm putting down?
example:
A: He's nice, but... you know.
B: I catch your drift.
A: Yeah, not explicitly saying it.
B: Loud and clear.
A: Good.
B: Say no more.
vietnamese: Hiểu ý tôi chứ? / Nắm được tình hình chưa?
japanese: 言（い）いたいことわかる？／察（さっ）して。
chinese: 你懂我意思吗? (Nǐ dǒng wǒ yìsi ma?) / 明白了吗? (Míngbai le ma?)

vietnamese: Họp mặt gia đình / Cỗ bàn gia đình
spanish: Reunión familiar / Evento familiar
japanese: 親戚（しんせき）の集（あつまり）。／家族（かぞく）行事（ぎょうじ）。
example:
A: Can you come out Saturday?
B: Can't, I have a family function.
A: Boring?
B: It's my grandma's 90th, so mandatory.
A: Have fun.
B: I'll try to sneak out early.
chinese: 家庭聚会 (Jiātíng jùhuì)

japanese: 詰（つ）めて。／席（せき）空（あ）けて。
example:
A: The subway is so packed today.
B: Hey, can you scoot over a little?
A: Sorry, let me make some space for you.
B: Thanks, if you could just shift a bit to the left.
A: Is this enough? I’m trying to slide over.
B: Perfect, thanks for giving me some room.
vietnamese: Xích qua kia chút / Nhích qua bên kia đi
spanish: Córrete un poco / Hazme sitio
meaning: 옆으로 좀 비켜봐 / 자리 좀 만들어줘
chinese: 挪一挪 (Nuóyīnuó) / 挤一挤 (Jǐyījǐ)

spanish: Mantén la compostura / No pierdas los papeles
example:
A: I'm freaking out about the wedding.
B: You need to keep it together.
A: So much to do.
B: One thing at a time. Breathe.
A: You're right.
B: You got this, bridezilla.
japanese: 落（お）ち着（つ）いて。／取（と）り乱（みだ）さないで。
vietnamese: Bình tĩnh lại đi / Giữ cái đầu lạnh
chinese: 稳住 (Wěnzhù) / 别崩溃 (Bié bēngkuì)

example:
A: He was screaming in my face.
B: Did you yell back?
A: No, I managed to keep my cool.
B: Proud of you. That takes patience.
A: I was shaking though.
B: But you handled it like a pro.
japanese: 冷静（れいせい）さを保（たも）つ。／キレない。
vietnamese: Đừng nóng / Giữ bình tĩnh
spanish: Mantén la calma / No te alteres
chinese: 保持冷静 (Bǎochí lěngjìng) / 别冲动 (Bié chōngdòng)

vietnamese: Trời đẹp quá / Thời tiết hôm nay tuyệt vời
spanish: Hace un día precioso / Hace muy bueno
example:
A: Step outside yet?
B: No, why?
A: It's beautiful out. You need fresh air.
B: Maybe I'll take a walk.
A: Do it. It's wasted inside.
B: grabbing my shoes now.
japanese: 外（そと）は天気（てんき）いいよ。／最高（さいこう）の天気（てんき）だ。
chinese: 外面天气真好 (Wàimian tiānqì zhēn hǎo)

japanese: 自惚（うぬぼ）れないで。／勘違（かんちが）いしないで。
example:
A: She was totally checking me out.
B: Don't flatter yourself. She was looking at the menu.
A: No, we made eye contact.
B: In your dreams.
A: I have aura.
B: You have delusions.
vietnamese: Đừng tưởng bở / Bớt ảo tưởng đi
spanish: No te lo creas tanto / No te vengas arriba
chinese: 别自作多情了 (Bié zìzuòduōqíng le) / 别往自己脸上贴金了 (Bié wǎng zìjǐ liǎnshàng tiējīn le)

japanese: せかさないで。／まとわりつかないで。
meaning: 그만 좀 괴롭혀. 귀찮게 하지 마.
example:
A: Are we there yet? Are we there yet?
B: Stop pestering me! We'll be there soon.
A: I'm bored.
B: Read a book or sleep.
A: Ugh, fine.
B: Silence is golden.
vietnamese: Đừng quấy rầy tôi nữa / Để tôi yên
spanish: Deja de dar la lata / Deja de incordiar
chinese: 别缠着我 (Bié chánzhe wǒ) / 别烦我 (Bié fán wǒ)

vietnamese: Cứ tự nhiên / Làm tới bến đi
japanese: 好（す）きにやって。／遠慮（えんりょ）なくどうぞ。
spanish: Adelante / Sírvete tú mismo
example:
A: Can I finish these fries?
B: Knock yourself out. I'm full.
A: Yes! I hate wasting food.
B: You are a human vacuum.
A: It's a talent.
B: Sure it is.
chinese: 请便 (Qǐngbiàn) / 随便你 (Suíbiàn nǐ)

spanish: Consultártelo / Comentártelo antes
example:
A: I promised them a discount.
B: What? Without asking me?
A: I meant to run it by you first.
B: Next time, ask before you promise.
A: My bad.
B: It's fine, just communicate.
vietnamese: Hỏi ý kiến cậu trước / Thông qua cậu chút
japanese: 確認（かくにん）しておきたい。／一応（いちおう）耳（みみ）に入（い）れておく。
chinese: 跟你商量一下 (Gēn nǐ shāngliang yīxià) / 问问你的意见 (Wènwen nǐ de yìjiàn)

japanese: 下書（したが）き中（ちゅう）。／構成（こうせい）中（ちゅう）。
spanish: Redactando / Escribiendo el borrador
example:
A: What are you doing?
B: Just drafting an email to the client.
A: Make sure to sound professional.
B: I'm trying. It's tricky.
A: Let me proofread it.
B: Thanks, I need fresh eyes.
vietnamese: Đang lên khung / Đang viết nháp
chinese: 起草 (Qǐcǎo) / 打草稿 (Dǎ cǎogǎo)

similar: Six of one, half dozen of the other.
example:
A: It's technically cyan, not blue.
B: Same difference. It's blue.
A: Artists care about that stuff.
B: I'm colorblind to nuance.
A: Clearly.
B: Don't roast me.
vietnamese: Cũng rứa thôi / Khác gì nhau đâu
japanese: 似（に）たり寄（よ）ったり。／五十歩（ごじゅっぽ）百歩（ひゃっぽ）。
spanish: Es lo mismo / Tanto monta, monta tanto
chinese: 半斤八两 (Bànjīnbāliǎng) / 没啥区别 (Méishá qūbié)

japanese: 騒（さわ）ぎ立（た）てないで。／人前（ひとまえ）で恥（は）ずかしいことしないで。
vietnamese: Đừng làm loạn lên / Đừng gây chú ý
spanish: No montes un numerito / No des el espectáculo
example:
A: I'm going to scream.
B: Don't make a scene. We're in a library.
A: I'm so mad though.
B: Scream inside your heart.
A: Ughhhh.
B: Good job.
chinese: 别闹了 (Bié nào le) / 别丢人现眼了 (Bié diūrénxiànyǎn le)

japanese: 八（や）つ当（あ）たりしないで。／私（わたし）に当（あ）たらないで。
primary: Don’t take it out on me
vietnamese: Đừng giận cá chém thớt / Sao lại trút giận lên tôi
example:
A: Why is this coffee taking so long?!
B: Hey, don’t take it out on me, I just got here.
A: I'm just so stressed about this meeting.
B: I get it, but I'm not your punching bag.
A: You're right, I'm sorry for venting at you.
B: Thanks, just try to keep your cool with me.
similar: 3. Not my fault, don't blame me
4. Don't catch an attitude with me
spanish: No la pagues conmigo / Yo no tengo la culpa
chinese: 别拿我撒气 (Bié ná wǒ sāqì) / 别把气撒我身上 (Bié bǎ qì sā wǒ shēnshàng)

japanese: 心（こころ）が狭（せま）い。／ネチネチしてる。
example:
A: Is he still mad about the sandwich?
B: Yeah, he's being so petty.
A: It was three years ago.
B: He never forgets.
A: That's exhausting.
B: Tell me about it.
spanish: Qué rencoroso / Qué picado
vietnamese: Nhỏ mọn thế / Tính toán chi li thế
chinese: 太小心眼了 (Tài xiǎoxīnyǎn le) / 斤斤计较 (Jīnjīnjìjiào)

spanish: Vienen cosas buenas / Lo mejor está por llegar
japanese: いいことあるよ。／これからだよ。
similar: Light at the end of the tunnel.
example:
A: I've had such a bad month.
B: Hang in there. Good things are coming.
A: I hope you're right.
B: I promise. The universe balances out.
A: Thanks for the positivity.
B: Always.
vietnamese: Khổ tận cam lai / Sắp có tin vui rồi
chinese: 好事将近 (Hǎoshì jiāngjìn) / 好日子在后头呢 (Hǎo rìzi zài hòutou ne)

vietnamese: Làm tốt lắm / Đỉnh của chóp
spanish: Te estás saliendo / Lo estás petando
example:
A: I just closed another deal.
B: Wow, you're killing it lately!
A: I'm trying to get that promotion.
B: Ideally, it's already yours.
A: Fingers crossed.
B: You don't need luck.
japanese: 絶好調（ぜっこうちょう）だね。／神（かみ）がかってる。
chinese: 你太棒了 (Nǐ tài bàng le) / 你做得太好了 (Nǐ zuò de tài hǎo le)

example:
A: How was my presentation?
B: You crushed it! Everyone was impressed.
A: I was so nervous.
B: Couldn't tell at all.
A: Phew.
B: Drinks on you tonight.
japanese: 最高（さいこう）だったよ。／圧倒（あっとう）したね。
spanish: La has clavado / Lo bordaste
vietnamese: Làm xuất sắc luôn / Quá đỉnh
chinese: 你干得漂亮 (Nǐ gàn de piàoliang) / 完胜 (Wánshèng)

vietnamese: Không thể tin nổi / Đỉnh cao trí tuệ
spanish: Alucinante / Te vuela la cabeza
example:
A: Have you seen the new sci-fi movie?
B: Yes, the visual effects were mind-blowing.
A: Better than the first one?
B: Way better. It blew my mind.
A: I need to buy tickets.
B: Go IMAX or go home.
japanese: 度肝（どぎも）を抜（ぬ）く。／衝撃（しょうげき）的（てき）。
chinese: 太震撼了 (Tài zhènhàn le) / 脑洞大开 (Nǎodòngdàkāi)

example:
A: He borrowed money from you again?
B: Yeah, he said he'd pay me back.
A: Stop being such a doormat.
B: I just want to help.
A: He's using you.
B: I know...
vietnamese: Kẻ nhu nhược / Người dễ bị bắt nạt
spanish: Felpudo / Se deja pisotear
japanese: 言（い）いなり。／敷物（しきもの）扱（あつか）い。
chinese: 受气包 (Shòuqìbāo) / 软柿子 (Ruǎnshìzi)

spanish: Me pillaste desprevenido / Me agarraste en frío
japanese: 不意（ふい）を突（つ）かれた。／びっくりした。
vietnamese: Làm tôi bất ngờ / Không đỡ kịp
example:
A: I'm quitting tomorrow.
B: Whoa, you caught me off guard.
A: I've been planning it for months.
B: You hid it well.
A: Didn't want drama.
B: Well, mission accomplished.
chinese: 让我措手不及 (Ràng wǒ cuòshǒubùjí) / 吓我一跳 (Xià wǒ yī tiào)

japanese: 元（もと）奥（おく）さん。／別（わか）れた妻（つま）。
example:
A: Who was that?
B: My ex-wife.
A: Oh, awkward?
B: We're cool now.
A: That's mature.
B: It took ten years.
vietnamese: Vợ cũ
spanish: Exmujer / Exesposa
chinese: 前妻 (Qiánqī)

example:
A: Your Korean is amazing.
B: Thanks, but I still have a long way to go.
A: You're too modest.
B: Seriously, verbs are hard.
A: You'll get there.
B: Fighting!
primary: I still have a long way to go
spanish: Aún me falta mucho / Me queda un largo camino
vietnamese: Tôi còn non lắm / Vẫn phải học hỏi nhiều
japanese: まだまだだよ。／修行（しゅぎょう）が足（た）りない。
chinese: 我还差得远呢 (Wǒ hái chà de yuǎn ne)

example:
A: Are we almost finished?
B: Nope, long way to go.
A: I'm tired.
B: Pace yourself.
A: Can we take a break?
B: Five minutes only.
spanish: Queda mucho trecho / Falta mucho
vietnamese: Còn xa lắm / Đường còn dài
japanese: 先（さき）は長（なが）い。／まだまだこれから。
chinese: 路还长着呢 (Lù hái chángzhe ne) / 任重道远 (Rènzhòngdàoyuǎn)

vietnamese: Nghe xem có lọt tai không / Nói mà không biết ngượng à
spanish: Escúchate un poco / ¿Te oyes lo que dices?
japanese: 何（なに）言（い）ってるの？／自分（じぶん）の言（い）ってることわかってる？
example:
A: I think I should quit my job and move to Mars.
B: Wow, listen to yourself right now.
A: Why? I’m serious about the space program.
B: This is outrageous, you don't even like flying!
A: I can learn to love it for the mission.
B: Seriously, you're tripping if you think that'll work.
meaning: 네가 하는 말 좀 들어봐, 정말 너무하네
chinese: 听听你自己说的话 (Tīngting nǐ zìjǐ shuō de huà)

spanish: Afrontar las consecuencias / Apechugar
japanese: 潔（いさぎよ）く認（みと）める。／年貢（ねんぐ）の納（おさ）め時（どき）だ。
example:
A: I crashed my dad's car.
B: You have to face the music.
A: He's going to kill me.
B: Better to tell him now than later.
A: Come with me?
B: No chance.
vietnamese: Chấp nhận sự thật đi / Dám làm dám chịu
chinese: 承担后果 (Chéngdān hòuguǒ) / 面对现实 (Miànduì xiànshí)

japanese: もう無理（むり）。／言葉（ことば）も出（で）ない（呆（あき）れ）。
example:
A: Did you see what she wore?
B: I can't even.
A: It was a choice.
B: I literally have no words.
A: Same.
B: Moving on.
vietnamese: Hết nói nổi / Cạn lời
spanish: No puedo con la vida / Me da algo
chinese: 我无语了 (Wǒ wúyǔ le) / 我受不了了 (Wǒ shòubùliǎo le)

example:
A: Why are you so sad lately?
B: Just autumn blues, I guess.
A: The weather is getting colder.
B: Yeah, I miss summer.
A: Let's get pumpkin spice lattes.
B: That actually helps.
spanish: Depresión otoñal / Bajón de otoño
vietnamese: Buồn man mác khi thu về / Bệnh mùa thu
japanese: 秋（あき）の憂鬱（ゆううつ）。／センチメンタルな気分（きぶん）。
chinese: 悲秋 (Bēiqiū) / 秋季抑郁 (Qiūjì yìyù)

japanese: 勘違（かんちが）いしたもん勝（が）ち。／思（おも）い込（こ）みが大事（だいじ）。
vietnamese: Ảo tưởng là sức mạnh / Mơ mộng tí cho đời vui
spanish: La ilusión es la solución / Vivir en los mundos de Yupi
similar: Fake it till you make it.
example:
A: He's definitely staring at me.
B: He's looking at the clock.
A: Delulu is the solulu.
B: Whatever makes you happy.
A: We're practically married.
B: Okay, crazy.
chinese: 甚至不清是唯一的解药 (Shènzhì bùqīng shì wéiyī de jiěyào) / 敢想才会有 (Gǎnxiǎng cái huì yǒu)

spanish: ¡Venga! / ¡Dale!
japanese: かかってこい！／やってやるよ！
vietnamese: Nhào vô / Chơi tới bến luôn
example:
A: The final exam is going to be brutal.
B: Bring it on. I studied all week.
A: Confident much?
B: Prepared much.
A: Good luck.
B: You too.
chinese: 放马过来 (Fàngmǎguòlái) / 尽管来吧 (Jǐnguǎn lái ba)

meaning: 말도 안 돼. 절대 안 돼.
vietnamese: Không đời nào / Miễn bàn
spanish: Ni de broma / Ni hablar
example:
A: Can I borrow the car for a road trip?
B: Out of the question.
A: Why?
B: You don't have a license.
A: Details.
B: Important details.
japanese: 論外（ろんがい）だ。／あり得（え）ない。
chinese: 绝不可能 (Jué bù kěnéng) / 没门儿 (Méiménr)

spanish: Sal a que te dé el aire / Pisa la calle un poco
example:
A: I argued with a stranger online for 4 hours.
B: You need to touch grass.
A: But he was wrong!
B: It doesn't matter. Go for a walk.
A: Fine.
B: Leave the phone.
japanese: 現実（げんじつ）を見（み）ろ。／外（そと）の空気（くうき）吸（す）ってこい。
vietnamese: Ra ngoài chạm cỏ đi / Bớt sống ảo đi
chinese: 出去走走 (Chūqù zǒuzou) / 别老宅着 (Bié lǎo zhāizhe) (Literal: touch grass)

vietnamese: Cứ giả vờ giỏi cho đến khi giỏi thật / Cứ tỏ ra tự tin rồi sẽ thành công
japanese: ハッタリかませ。／できるフリしてればなんとかなる。
spanish: Finge hasta que lo consigas / Créetelo hasta que sea verdad
example:
A: I have no idea what I'm doing.
B: Just fake it 'til you make it.
A: What if they find out?
B: They won't. Everyone is faking it.
A: Comforting?
B: Revealing.
chinese: 装久了就成真的了 (Zhuāng jiǔ le jiù chéng zhēn de le) / 弄假成真 (Nòngjiǎchéngzhēn)

spanish: Tomando un café / Disfrutando del café
example:
A: What are you doing?
B: Just sipping on some coffee. You?
A: Working hard.
B: Join me.
A: I wish.
B: I'll save you a cup.

similar: 3. Takes a thief to catch a thief

example:

similar: Like two peas in a pod.

chinese: 你自己也不咋地 (Nǐ zìjǐ yě bù zǎ dì) / 亏你好意思说 (Kuī nǐ hǎoyìsi shuō)

B: Oh, let me be clear, safety definitely comes first then.

spanish: Icónico / Mítico

A: We need a heater.

spanish: Hay corriente / Entra aire

example:

A: Expensive?

chinese: 按摩师 (Ànmóshī)

example:

B: It's so frustrating!

B: Let's go to a cafe.
A: Fine.
B: Their wifi is fast.

chinese: 太让人抓狂了 (Tài ràng rén zhuākuáng le) / 很挫败 (Hěn cuòbài)

A: Sounds perfect.
B: No drama.

japanese: 目立（めだ）たないようにして。／溶（と）け込（こ）んで。
vietnamese: Đừng chơi trội / Đừng làm mình nổi bật
example:

vietnamese: Cục vàng của tôi / Người thương nhất đời
chinese: 掌上明珠 (Zhǎngshàngmíngzhū) / 最珍爱的人 (Zuì zhēn'ài de rén)

japanese: 足（あし）が痺（しび）れた。／感覚（かんかく）がない。
example:
A: Stand up.
B: I can't, my leg fell asleep.
A: Shake it off.
B: It feels like ants are biting me.
A: Dance it off.
B: Ouch.
vietnamese: Chân tôi bị tê / Tê chân quá
spanish: Se me ha dormido la pierna / Tengo la pierna entumecida
chinese: 我腿麻了 (Wǒ tuǐ má le)

japanese: 私（わたし）が選（えら）んだんじゃない。／私（わたし）のせいじゃない。
spanish: Yo no lo elegí / No fue elección mía
example:
A: This movie is terrible.
B: I didn't pick it!
A: You suggested it.
B: I said 'maybe'.
A: Excuses.
B: Watch or leave.
vietnamese: Tôi có chọn đâu / Không phải tôi chọn
chinese: 不是我选的 (Bùshì wǒ xuǎn de)

spanish: Tengo mucho lío / Estoy hasta arriba de trabajo
example:
A: Can you help me move?
B: Sorry, I have a lot on my plate right now.
A: Just one box?
B: I have deadlines.
A: Fine.
B: Good luck.
vietnamese: Việc ngập đầu rồi / Bận tối mắt tối mũi
primary: I have a lot on my plate
japanese: 手（て）一杯（いっぱい）だ。／余裕（よゆう）がない。
chinese: 我有一大堆事儿 (Wǒ yǒu yī dà duī shìr) / 我忙死了 (Wǒ máng sǐ le)

spanish: Empezamos desde ahí / Seguimos a partir de ahí
example:
A: I'm stuck on chapter 3.
B: Okay, we'll start from there.
A: I don't understand the plot.
B: Neither does the author.
A: Really?
B: Just kidding.
vietnamese: Cứ bắt đầu từ đó đi / Tính từ đó nhé
japanese: そこから始（はじ）めよう。／そこんところから。
similar: Let's pick it up from there.
chinese: 我们从那儿开始 (Wǒmen cóng nàr kāishǐ)

japanese: チャック開（あ）いてるよ。／社会（しゃかい）の窓（まど）全開（ぜんかい）。
example:
A: Why is everyone staring at me?
B: Your fly is open.
A: Oh my god.
B: XYZ, buddy.
A: Did you see anything?
B: Too much.
vietnamese: Khóa quần chưa kéo kìa / Cửa sổ mở toang hoác kìa
spanish: Tienes la bragueta abierta / Se te ha bajado la cremallera
chinese: 你拉链开了 (Nǐ lāliàn kāi le)

vietnamese: Pizza luôn thấu hiểu / Chỉ có pizza là không phán xét
example:
A: I'm lonely.
B: Order a pizza. Pizza doesn't judge.
A: It just makes me fat.
B: That's just more of you to love.
A: You're an enabler.
B: I'm a good friend.
similar: Pizza is always there for you.
spanish: La pizza no juzga / La pizza te quiere igual
meaning: 피자는 너를 비난하지 않아. (음식은 배신하지 않아)
japanese: ピザは裏切（うらぎ）らない。／ピザは正義（せいぎ）。
chinese: 披萨不会评判你 (Pīsà bùhuì píngpàn nǐ) / 吃就完了 (Chī jiù wán le)

japanese: もう行（い）かなくちゃ。／お暇（いとま）するね。
similar: 2. Time for me to hit the road
spanish: Será mejor que me vaya / Me tengo que ir yendo
vietnamese: Thôi tôi lượn đây / Tôi phải đi rồi
example:
A: This coffee was great, but look at the time! 
B: Yeah, it's getting pretty late. 
A: I better be off if I want to catch the last train. 
B: No problem, I should get going too. 
A: Let's do this again next week. 
B: For sure, I've gotta run now, see ya!
chinese: 我得走了 (Wǒ děi zǒu le)

japanese: 帰（かえ）っちゃダメ。／抜（ぬ）けられないよ。
vietnamese: Sao bỏ đi khơi khơi vậy được / Cậu không thể cứ thế mà đi
example:
A: I'm bored. Bye.
B: You can't just leave! We're in the middle of a game.
A: I'm losing.
B: Be a good sport.
A: No.
B: Come back!
spanish: No puedes irte así como así / No puedes largarte sin más
chinese: 你不能就这么走了 (Nǐ bùnéng jiù zhème zǒu le)

japanese: よく考（かんが）えて。／熟考（じゅっこう）して。
vietnamese: Nghĩ cho kỹ vào / Tính toán cho cẩn thận
spanish: Piénsalo bien / Medítalo
example:
A: I'm going to quit my job and become a mime.
B: Think it through.
A: I have.
B: Mimes don't talk. You love talking.
A: Good point.
B: Stick to your day job.
chinese: 想清楚 (Xiǎng qīngchu) / 深思熟虑 (Shēnsīshúlǜ)

japanese: 夢（ゆめ）にも思（おも）わなかった。／知（し）る由（よし）もなかった。
example:
A: I bought Bitcoin in 2010.
B: Nice.
A: Little did I know I'd lose the password.
B: Tragedy.
A: Billions gone.
B: Don't cry.
vietnamese: Có nằm mơ tôi cũng không biết / Ai mà dè
spanish: Poco me imaginaba / Ni me imaginaba
chinese: 我一点都不知道 (Wǒ yīdiǎn dōu bù zhīdào) / 谁能想到 (Shéi néng xiǎngdào)

japanese: おまけ映像（えいぞう）。／エンドロール後（ご）。
vietnamese: Cảnh sau credit / Đoạn phim tặng kèm cuối phim
spanish: Escena post-créditos
example:
A: Let's go.
B: Wait! There's a post-credit scene.
A: Are you sure?
B: It's a Marvel movie. There's always one.
A: Five more minutes?
B: Sit down.
chinese: 片尾彩蛋 (Piànwěi cǎidàn)

vietnamese: Vẫn nguyên vẹn / Không sứt mẻ gì
japanese: 無傷（むきず）。／そのまま。
spanish: Intacto / Entero
example:
A: Did the package arrive?
B: Yes, it's completely intact.
A: Phew. It was glass.
B: Good packing.
A: Bubble wrap implies love.
B: Sure.
chinese: 完好无损 (Wánhǎowúsǔn)

spanish: Qué aleatorio / Qué ocurrencia más rara
vietnamese: Lạc quẻ quá / Tự nhiên ất ơ đâu ra
japanese: いきなりだね。／脈絡（みゃくらく）ないね。
example:
A: I want to buy a llama.
B: That's so random.
A: They spark joy.
B: They spit.
A: Only when angry.
B: Good luck.
chinese: 太莫名其妙了 (Tài mòmíngqímiào le) / 太突然了 (Tài tūrán le)

japanese: 新婚（しんこん）さん。／ラブラブ。
spanish: Recién casados
example:
A: Look at them holding hands.
B: They're newlyweds.
A: Give it a year.
B: Cynic.
A: Realist.
B: Let them carry the torch.
vietnamese: Vợ chồng son / Cặp đôi mới cưới
chinese: 新婚夫妇 (Xīnhūn fūfù)

example:
A: He's very left-wing.
B: And she's conservative.
A: Opposites attract?
B: Or argue constantly.
A: Probably the latter.
B: Dinner must be fun.
vietnamese: Cánh tả / Phe tư tưởng cấp tiến
japanese: 左派（さは）。／リベラル。
spanish: De izquierdas / Progresista
chinese: 左翼 (Zuǒyì)

vietnamese: Nói một cách chính xác thì / Về mặt kỹ thuật mà nói
example:
A: Is a tomato a vegetable?
B: Technically speaking, it's a fruit.
A: Don't put it in fruit salad.
B: Knowledge is knowing it's a fruit; wisdom is not putting it in a fruit salad.
A: Deep.
B: Old quote.
spanish: Técnicamente hablando / En rigor
japanese: 厳密（げんみつ）に言（い）えば。／定義（ていぎ）上（じょう）は。
chinese: 严格来说 (Yángé láishuō) / 技术上讲 (Jìshù shàng jiǎng)

japanese: 認（みと）めたくないけど。／癪（しゃく）だけど。
example:
A: I was right, wasn't I?
B: I hate to admit it, but yes.
A: Say it louder.
B: Don't push your luck.
A: I'll record it.
B: No.
vietnamese: Không muốn nhận đâu nhưng mà... / Ghét phải thừa nhận là...
spanish: Me duele admitirlo / Odio reconocerlo
chinese: 虽然我不想承认 (Suīrán wǒ bù xiǎng chéngrèn)

vietnamese: Đi đi nào / Lên đường may mắn
spanish: Anda, vete ya / Venga, tira
example:
A: Is there anything else you need help with? 
B: Nope, that’s everything. Off you go! 
A: Are you sure? I can stay a bit longer. 
B: Seriously, you're free to go, enjoy your night. 
A: Alright, I'm heading out then. 
B: Run along, I'll see you tomorrow morning!
japanese: 行（い）っていいよ。／さあ行（い）け。
chinese: 去吧 (Qù ba) / 走你 (Zǒu nǐ)

primary: Why couldn't I bring myself to say it?
spanish: ¿Por qué no me atreví a decirlo? / ¿Por qué no fui capaz de decirlo?
example:
A: Did you tell him you love him?
B: No. Why couldn't I bring myself to say it?
A: Fear of rejection?
B: Probably.
A: Try again tomorrow.
B: If I'm brave.
japanese: なんで言（い）えなかったんだろう。／言（い）えばよかった。
similar: I couldn't get the words out.
vietnamese: Sao tôi lại không thốt nên lời vậy chứ? / Sao tôi lại không dám nói ra nhỉ?
chinese: 我怎么就说不出口呢? (Wǒ zěnme jiù shuōbu chūkǒu ne?)

vietnamese: Ăn no vào / Cứ ăn thoải mái
japanese: 腹（はら）いっぱい食（た）べな。／食（く）い倒（だお）れろ。
example:
A: Is it free?
B: Yes, eat your fill.
A: I will regret this later.
B: That's future you's problem.
A: Present me is happy.
B: Bon appetit.
spanish: Come hasta hartarte / Ponte las botas
chinese: 吃个够 (Chī gè gòu) / 尽管吃 (Jǐnguǎn chī)

spanish: Situación crítica / Incierto
example:
A: Will he survive?
B: It was touch and go for a while.
A: But now?
B: He's stable.
A: Thank god.
B: Miracle.
vietnamese: Ngàn cân treo sợi tóc / Hên xui may rủi
japanese: 際（きわ）どい状況（じょうきょう）。／一進一退（いっしんいったい）。
chinese: 一触即发 (Yīchùjífā) / 这种感觉很微妙 (Zhèzhǒng gǎnjué hěn wēimiào)

spanish: Me sentía muy angustiado / Estaba muy preocupado
japanese: 心（こころ）が痛（いた）んだ。／気（き）が重（おも）かった。
vietnamese: Lòng tôi rối bời / Tôi thấy phiền lòng quá
example:
A: You look pale.
B: I felt so troubled about the news.
A: Don't let it eat you up.
B: Hard not to.
A: Breathe.
B: Trying.
chinese: 我很纠结 (Wǒ hěn jiūjié) / 我很苦恼 (Wǒ hěn kǔnǎo)

vietnamese: Gì cơ? Tôi bị lú rồi / Nói gì thế? Chẳng hiểu gì cả
japanese: え、何（なに）だって？／話（はなし）についていけない。
spanish: ¿Qué dices? Me he perdido / ¿Cómo? No te sigo
example:
A: The quantum fluctuation implies...
B: Say what? I'm lost.
A: It's simple physics.
B: To you maybe.
A: I'll draw a picture.
B: Please.
chinese: 说什么? 我没听懂 (Shuō shénme? Wǒ méi tīngdǒng) / 啥? 我懵了 (Shá? Wǒ měng le)

vietnamese: Tôi cũng mong là thế / Được thế thì còn gì bằng
japanese: そうならいいのに。／だったらなぁ。
spanish: Ojalá / Ya me gustaría
example:
A: Are you rich?
B: I wish.
A: You have a Gucci bag.
B: It's fake.
A: Looks real.
B: That's the point.
chinese: 想得美 (Xiǎng de měi) / 但愿如此 (Dànyuàn rúcǐ)

japanese: 絶対（ぜったい）ない。／あり得（え）ない。
example:
A: Can we get a dog?
B: Not happening.
A: A small one?
B: No poop machines.
A: I'll clean it.
B: Lies.
vietnamese: Mơ đi cưng / Không có chuyện đó đâu
spanish: Ni de coña / Ni hablar
chinese: 没门儿 (Méiménr) / 这事儿没戏 (Zhè shìr méixì)

vietnamese: Nhậu sương sương trước khi đi quẩy / Làm vài ly lấy đà
meaning: 술 마시기 전에 먼저 한잔하는 것. (1차 전)
spanish: Hacer la previa / Beber antes de salir
japanese: 景気（けいき）付（づ）けに飲（の）む。／（パーティー前（まえ）の）宅飲（たくの）み。
example:
A: Coming to the club?
B: We're pregaming at Mike's place first.
A: Save me a shot.
B: Hurry up.
A: On my way.
B: Don't be late.
chinese: 预热 (Yùrè) / 喝两杯再走 (Hē liǎngbēi zài zǒu)

vietnamese: Dân gốc ở đây / Sinh ra và lớn lên ở đây
example:
A: Do you know this city well?
B: I was born and raised here.
A: Best pizza place?
B: Tony's.
A: Take me.
B: Let's go.
japanese: 生粋（きっすい）の。／地元（じもと）民（みん）。
spanish: Nacido y criado / De aquí de toda la vida
chinese: 土生土长 (Tǔshēngtǔzhǎng)

similar: Just what I wanted to hear.
japanese: 嬉（うれ）しい知（し）らせだ。／心地（ここち）よい響（ひび）きだ。
vietnamese: Nghe mát lòng mát dạ / Tin vui đây rồi
spanish: Música para mis oídos / Qué gusto oír eso
example:
A: The boss said we get a bonus.
B: That is music to my ears.
A: And a day off.
B: Even better.
A: But not until next month.
B: I can wait.
chinese: 中听 (Zhōngtīng) / 这话我爱听 (Zhèhuà wǒ ài tīng)

spanish: Ve al grano / Déjate de rodeos
primary: Cut to the chase / get to the point
example:
A: So, regarding the project, there are many factors... 
B: Look, just cut to the chase. 
A: Well, we might need a bigger budget. 
B: Get to the point, how much more do you need? 
A: About five thousand dollars. 
B: Finally, give it to me straight next time!
vietnamese: Vào thẳng vấn đề đi / Đừng vòng vo tam quốc nữa
japanese: 単刀直入（たんとうちょくにゅう）に言（い）って。／要点（ようてん）だけ言（い）って。
chinese: 直奔主题 (Zhíbènzhǔtí) / 说重点 (Shuō zhòngdiǎn)

japanese: 20分（ぷん）くらい遅（おく）れる。／遅刻（ちこく）確定（かくてい）。
spanish: Llego 20 minutos tarde / Se me ha hecho tarde 20 minutos
vietnamese: Tôi bị trễ khoảng 20 phút / Chắc tôi đến muộn 20 phút đấy
example:
A: Where are you?
B: I'm running 20 minutes late.
A: Movie starts in 10.
B: Buy popcorn.
A: I'll eat it all.
B: Save some.
chinese: 我晚了20分钟 (Wǒ wǎn le èrshí fēnzhōng)

japanese: バレた。／見（み）つかった。
vietnamese: Bị tóm / Bị bắt quả tang
example:
A: Did you sneak out?
B: Yeah, but I got busted.
A: By who?
B: Mom was waiting in the kitchen.
A: Scary.
B: Terrifying.
spanish: Que te pillen / Ser descubierto
chinese: 被抓现行 (Bèi zhuā xiànxíng) / 露馅了 (Lòuxiàn le)

meaning: 그날 있었던 일 좀 자세히 말해봐.
spanish: Cuéntame qué pasó ese día paso a paso / Explícame ese día
japanese: その日（ひ）のこと詳（くわ）しく話（はなし）て。／再現（さいげん）して。
vietnamese: Kể lại chi tiết ngày hôm đó xem / Tường thuật lại ngày đó đi
example:
A: I lost the contract.
B: Walk me through that day.
A: I overslept...
B: Bad start.
A: Then I spilled coffee.
B: Classic.
chinese: 跟我讲讲那天的事 (Gēn wǒ jiǎngjiang nàtiān de shì)

vietnamese: Mục đích là gì? / Có ý đồ gì đây?
example:
A: I'll clean your room for free.
B: What's the angle?
A: No angle.
B: You want to borrow my car.
A: Maybe.
B: I knew it.
japanese: 狙（ねら）いは何（なに）？／魂胆（こんたん）は？
spanish: ¿Cúal es el truco? / ¿Qué tramas?
chinese: 什么意图? (Shénme yìtú?) / 什么角度? (Shénme jiǎodù?)

spanish: Perdió los papeles / Explotó
example:
A: Why is he crying?
B: I snapped at him.
A: Why?
B: He chewed with his mouth open.
A: Reasonable.
B: I feel bad though.
japanese: キレた。／プッツンした。
vietnamese: Tức nước vỡ bờ / Nổi điên lên
chinese: 炸毛了 (Zhàmáo le) / 崩溃了 (Bēngkuì le)

vietnamese: Đồng bọn / Kẻ tòng phạm
japanese: 共犯（きょうはん）者（しゃ）。／グル。
example:
A: Who ate the cake?
B: The dog was my accomplice.
A: Looking at his face, he's guilty.
B: We split it 50/50.
A: You're both grounded.
B: Worth it.
spanish: Cómplice / Compinche
chinese: 同伙 (Tónghuǒ) / 共犯 (Gòngfàn)

japanese: お見舞（みま）いに行（い）く。／顔（かお）を見（み）に行（い）く。
spanish: Visitar a alguien / Ir a ver a alguien
vietnamese: Thăm nuôi / Đi thăm bệnh
example:
A: Where are you going?
B: To visit someone in the hospital.
A: Nothing serious?
B: Just a broken leg.
A: Sign the cast for me.
B: Will do.
chinese: 拜访某人 (Bàifǎng mǒurén) / 串门 (Chuànmén)

example:
A: Can I borrow $10?
B: I don't have it on me.
A: You have a wallet.
B: Digital wallet only.
A: Transfer me.
B: No signal.
primary: I don't have it on me
spanish: No lo llevo encima / No lo tengo aquí
japanese: 持（も）ち合（あ）わせがない。／手持（ても）ちがない。
vietnamese: Tôi không mang theo người / Trong túi không có
chinese: 我没带在身上 (Wǒ méi dài zài shēnshàng)

spanish: La cola empieza allí atrás / A la cola
vietnamese: Xếp hàng tít đằng kia kìa / Đuôi hàng ở kia cơ
example:
A: Excuse me.
B: The line starts back there.
A: I just have one question.
B: So do they.
A: Tough crowd.
B: Fair crowd.
japanese: 最後尾（さいこうび）はあっちだよ。／並（なら）んで。
meaning: 줄은 저 뒤가 끝이에요. (새치기 하지 마)
chinese: 队尾在那边 (Duìwěi zài nàbian)

example:
A: Did you talk to him?
B: No, I decided to sever ties.
A: Drastic.
B: Necessary.
A: Peace of mind?
B: Exactly.
japanese: 断（た）つ。／縁（えん）を切（き）る。
vietnamese: Cắt đứt / Đoạn tuyệt
spanish: Cortar / Romper
chinese: 切断 (Qiēduàn) / 断绝 (Duànjué)

vietnamese: Được ăn cả ngã về không / Chơi lớn không thì nghỉ
japanese: 派手（はで）にやろう。／中途半端（ちゅうとはんぱ）はなしだ。
example:
A: Should we just throw a small party for the launch? 
B: No way, go big or go home. 
A: But a huge event will be so expensive. 
B: It's all or nothing if we want to make an impact. 
A: I guess we should go for broke then. 
B: Exactly, let's give it our all!
spanish: O todo o nada / A lo grande o nada
meaning: 통 크게 하거나 아니면 관두거나 / 최선을 다해
chinese: 要么做大要么回家 (Yàome zuò dà yàome huíjiā) / 豁出去了 (Huōchūqu le)

vietnamese: Tôi hấp tấp quá / Tôi dại dột quá
spanish: Fui impulsivo / Me precipité
japanese: 早（はや）まったことした。／浅（あさ）はかだった。
example:
A: You quit your job?
B: I was rash.
A: Can you go back?
B: I burned the bridge.
A: Well then.
B: Startup life begins.
chinese: 我太鲁莽了 (Wǒ tài lǔmǎng le) / 我太冲动了 (Wǒ tài chōngdòng le)

example:
A: Are you okay?
B: Just tired.
A: You seem gaunt.
B: I haven't slept in days.
A: Eat a burger.
B: I need sleep, not food.
japanese: やつれてるよ。／ゲッソリしてる。
vietnamese: Trông cậu hốc hác thế / Sao gầy trơ xương thế kia
spanish: Te ves demacrado / Estás en los huesos
chinese: 你看起来很憔悴 (Nǐ kànqǐlái hěn qiáocuì)

vietnamese: Khai ra tuốt tuồn tuột / Phun ra hết mọi chuyện
spanish: Cantar como un canario / Irse de la lengua
japanese: 洗（あら）いざらい話（はな）す。／ペラペラしゃべる。
example:
A: Did he talk to the cops?
B: Sang like a canary.
A: We're doomed.
B: Pack your bags.
A: Mexico?
B: Mars.
chinese: 全招了 (Quán zhāo le) / 供认不讳 (Gòngrènbùhuì)

spanish: Comérselo todo / Arruinar a alguien comiendo
primary: Eat someone out of house and home
meaning: 집안 기둥뿌리 뽑히게 먹다. (엄청 많이 먹다)
vietnamese: Ăn thủng nồi trôi rế / Ăn sạt nghiệp luôn
example:
A: Your teenagers are here.
B: They're going to eat me out of house and home.
A: Order pizza.
B: Ten pizzas.
A: Good luck.
B: Send help.
japanese: 食（く）いつぶす。／エンゲル係数（けいすう）爆上（ばくあ）がり。
chinese: 把某人吃穷了 (Bǎ mǒurén chī qióng le)

japanese: 嫉妬（しっと）に狂（くる）ってる。／彼（かれ）を蝕（むしば）んでる。
example:
A: He seems moody.
B: Your success is eating him up inside.
A: He should be happy for me.
B: Ego is a fragile thing.
A: That's sad.
B: It is.
vietnamese: Gậm nhấm tâm can / Dằn vặt trong lòng
spanish: Le está carcomiendo por dentro / Le consume por dentro
chinese: 让他内心备受煎熬 (Ràng tā nèixīn bèishòu jiān'áo)

vietnamese: Biết ngay mà / Chả lạ gì
spanish: Me lo imaginaba / Típico
example:
A: It started raining as soon as I washed the car.
B: It figures.
A: Every time.
B: The universe hates clean cars.
A: Apparently.
B: Wash it again.
japanese: やっぱりね。／案（あん）の定（じょう）。
chinese: 虽迟但到 (Suī chí dàn dào) / 怪不得 (Guàibude)

vietnamese: Chửi té tát / Chửi như tát nước vào mặt
japanese: 罵倒（ばとう）する。／こき下（お）ろす。
example:
A: Why is he crying?
B: The boss cursed him out.
A: For what?
B: Spilling coffee.
A: Harsh.
B: Very.
spanish: Poner a parir a alguien / Echar una bronca monumental
chinese: 痛骂某人 (Tòngmà mǒurén) / 爆粗口 (Bào cūkǒu)

spanish: De una forma u otra / Sea como sea
japanese: なんとかして。／どのみち。
vietnamese: Không cách này thì cách khác / Kiểu gì cũng phải xong
example:
A: Will you finish on time?
B: I'll get it done, one way or the other.
A: That sounds ominous.
B: Determination.
A: Good luck.
B: Thanks.
chinese: 不管怎样 (Bùguǎn zěnyàng) / 迟早 (Chízǎo)

vietnamese: Ly kỳ khúc khuỷu / Tình tiết lắt léo
japanese: 波乱（はらん）万丈（ばんじょう）。／山（やま）あり谷（たに）あり。
example:
A: How was your trip?
B: It had its twists and turns.
A: Sounds like an adventure.
B: Or a nightmare.
A: Tell me more.
B: Buy me a drink first.
spanish: Giros inesperados / Altibajos
chinese: 曲折 (Qūzhé) / 跌宕起伏 (Diēdàngqǐfú)

spanish: Recompensar generosamente / Pagar muy bien
example:
A: Find my lost cat.
B: Any reward?
A: I will reward you handsomely.
B: Cash?
A: And cookies.
B: I'm on it.
japanese: たんまり礼（れい）をする。／弾（はず）むよ。
vietnamese: Hậu tạ / Thưởng hậu hĩnh
chinese: 重赏某人 (Zhòngshǎng mǒurén)

meaning: (어렵거나 하기 싫은 일을) 감수하다 / 매도 먼저 맞는 게 낫다
vietnamese: Nhắm mắt làm liều / Cắn răng chịu đựng
japanese: 覚悟（かくご）を決（き）める。／腹（はら）をくくる。
spanish: Hacer de tripas corazón / Tragar con ello
example:
A: I really don't want to tell the boss I lost the file. 
B: You just have to bite the bullet and tell him. 
A: He’s going to be so angry. 
B: Just face the music now so it's over with. 
A: You're right, I'll go to his office now. 
B: Good, suck it up and get it over with.
chinese: 咬紧牙关 (Yǎojǐnyáguān) / 硬着头皮 (Yìngzhe tóupí)

japanese: どんな犠牲（ぎせい）を払（はら）っても。／何（なに）としても。
example:
A: We must win this game.
B: At all costs.
A: Even cheating?
B: Let's stick to skill first.
A: Clean game.
B: Right.
vietnamese: Bằng bất cứ giá nào / Kiểu gì cũng phải làm
spanish: A toda costa / Cueste lo que cueste
chinese: 不惜一切代价 (Bùxī yīqiè dàijià)

japanese: 寝耳（ねみみ）に水（みず）。／青天（せいてん）の霹靂（へきれき）。
vietnamese: Sét đánh ngang tai / Chuyện động trời
spanish: Tal cual, de la nada / Una sorpresa total
example:
A: He resigned?
B: It was a bolt from the blue.
A: I thought he loved it here.
B: Guess not.
A: Who will replace him?
B: You?
chinese: 晴天霹雳 (Qíngtiānpīlì)

vietnamese: Tan nát hết rồi / Sắp toang rồi
japanese: ガタガタだ。／崩壊（ほうかい）寸前（すんぜん）。
example:
A: How is the project?
B: Falling apart.
A: Can we save it?
B: We need duct tape and a miracle.
A: I have duct tape.
B: Pray for the miracle.
spanish: Desmoronándose / Cayéndose a pedazos
chinese: 分崩离析 (Fēnbēnglíxī) / 崩溃 (Bēngkuì)

vietnamese: Tỉnh táo lại đi / Nghĩ cho thông suốt vào
example:
A: Are you okay to drive?
B: I can't think straight.
A: Give me the keys.
B: Take them.
A: Safety first.
B: Thank you.
spanish: Pensar con claridad / Pensar bien
japanese: まともに考（かんが）える。／頭（あたま）を冷（ひ）やす。
chinese: 思路清晰 (Sīlù qīngxī) / 不犯糊涂 (Bù fàn hútu)

vietnamese: Đau nửa đầu / Nhức đầu kinh khủng
japanese: 偏頭痛（へんずつう）。／ガンガンする。
example:
A: Lights off, please.
B: Migraine?
A: Yes, my head is exploding.
B: I'll get the pills.
A: Quietly.
B: Like a ninja.
spanish: Migraña / Jaqueca
chinese: 偏头痛 (Piāntóutòng)

vietnamese: Cho ai ra rìa / Bỏ rơi ai đó
example:
A: Did you invite Tom?
B: No, I left him out.
A: Why?
B: He eats all the pizza.
A: Fair point.
B: Exactly.
spanish: Dejar a alguien fuera / Excluir
japanese: 仲間外（なかまはず）れにする。／ハブる。
chinese: 冷落某人 (Lěngluò mǒurén) / 不带某人玩 (Bù dài mǒurén wán)

japanese: 知（し）らないなぁ。／未経験（みけいけん）だし。
spanish: No sabría decirte / Ni idea
example:
A: What's it like flying first class?
B: I wouldn't know.
A: Me neither.
B: Economy squalor for life.
A: Maybe one day.
B: Keep dreaming.
vietnamese: Sao tôi biết được / Tôi đã trải qua đâu mà biết
chinese: 我哪知道 (Wǒ nǎ zhīdào)

spanish: No te alteres / No te sofoques
japanese: カッカしないで。／興奮（こうふん）しないで。
vietnamese: Đừng có sồn sồn lên thế / Bình tĩnh đi
example:
A: I can't believe he said that!
B: Don't get worked up.
A: I'm furious.
B: High blood pressure kills.
A: Thanks for the reminder.
B: Just breathe.
chinese: 别激动 (Bié jīdòng) / 别上火 (Bié shànghuǒ)

primary: Will you take it to my room?
japanese: 部屋（へや）に運（はこ）んでくれる？／持（も）ってきて。
example:
A: Your package arrived.
B: Will you take it to my room?
A: I'm not your butler.
B: Please?
A: Fine, this once.
B: Thanks.
vietnamese: Mang lên phòng giúp tôi nhé / Chuyển cái này lên phòng tôi được không?
spanish: ¿Lo llevas a mi habitación? / ¿Me lo subes al cuarto?
similar: Drop it off in my room.
chinese: 能帮我拿回房间吗? (Néng bāng wǒ ná huí fángjiān ma?)

vietnamese: Tôi cũng lấy món y hệt / Cho tôi giống bạn này
spanish: Tomaré lo mismo / Lo mismo para mí
japanese: 同（おな）じもので。／右（みぎ）に同（おな）じ。
example:
A: I'll have the lobster thermidor.
B: I'll have the same, please.
A: It's market price.
B: I'm rich today.
A: Payday?
B: Lottery.
chinese: 我也要一样的 (Wǒ yě yào yīyàng de)

vietnamese: Định nói với cậu mãi mà chưa có dịp / Tôi muốn nói chuyện này lâu rồi
japanese: 言（い）おうと思（おも）ってたんだけど。／実（じつ）はね。
example:
A: I've been meaning to tell you something.
B: Oh god, what did you do now?
A: Nothing bad! I'm moving to Brooklyn.
B: Finally! I've been saying that for years.
A: Yeah, yeah. You were right.
B: I'm always right, glad you caught up.
similar: I meant to drop this on you
Been trying to find the right time
This has been on my mind
I gotta get this off my chest
spanish: Llevaba tiempo queriendo decirte / Quería comentarte algo
primary: I've been meaning to tell you
chinese: 我一直想告诉你 (Wǒ yīzhí xiǎng gàosu nǐ)

example:
A: This cookie is divine.
B: Care for another?
A: Don't twist my arm.
B: Take two.
A: You're an angel.
B: I know.
japanese: もう一（ひと）つどう？／お代（か）わりは？
spanish: ¿Quieres otro? / ¿Te apetece otro?
vietnamese: Làm thêm cái nữa không? / Uống thêm ly nữa nhé?
chinese: 再来一个? (Zài lái yīgè?)

vietnamese: Thương cảm / Thấy tội nghiệp
spanish: Compadecerse de / Entender a
japanese: 気持（きも）ちはわかるよ。／同情（どうじょう）する。
example:
A: He lost his wallet again.
B: I feel for him, but he's careless.
A: He needs a chain.
B: Or a babysitter.
A: Mean.
B: Honest.
chinese: 同情 (Tóngqíng) / 替...感到难过 (Tì... gǎndào nánguò)

vietnamese: Bao lâu rồi chưa gặp nhỉ? / Lâu lắm rồi đấy nhỉ
japanese: どれくらいぶり？／何（なん）年（ねん）ぶり？
example:
A: Hey! How long has it been?
B: Since college graduation.
A: Five years?
B: Ten.
A: We're old.
B: Experienced.
spanish: ¿Cuánto tiempo ha pasado? / ¿Cuánto hace?
chinese: 多久了? (Duōjiǔ le?)

spanish: ¿Cómo has estado? / ¿Qué tal te ha ido?
example:
A: Hey! How have you been?
B: Busy but good.
A: Still working at Google?
B: Nope, started my own thing.
A: Fancy.
B: Stressful.
vietnamese: Dạo này sống sao rồi? / Khỏe không?
japanese: 元気（げんき）だった？／どうしてた？
similar: What have you been up to?
chinese: 你最近怎么样? (Nǐ zuìjìn zěnmeyàng?)

japanese: 変（か）わり種（だね）。／逸脱（いつだつ）してる。
spanish: Caso atípico / Excepción
example:
A: Everyone hates cilantro.
B: I love it.
A: You're an outlier.
B: More for me.
A: True.
B: Enjoy your soap weed.
vietnamese: Kẻ ngoại đạo / Trường hợp cá biệt
chinese: 局外人 (Júwàirén) / 异类 (Yìlèi)

spanish: Me vendría bien / Necesito un poco de
japanese: 〜がほしいなぁ。／〜があればなぁ。
similar: It would be nice to have.
example:
A: You look tired.
B: I could use some coffee.
A: Black?
B: Inject it into my veins.
A: Coming right up.
B: Savior.
vietnamese: Tôi đang cần chút... / Có chút... thì tốt quá
chinese: 我正好需要 (Wǒ zhènghǎo xūyào) / 我想来点 (Wǒ xiǎng lái diǎn)

spanish: Tener mano para / Dársele bien
example:
A: The baby stopped crying instantly.
B: She has a way with children.
A: She's a whisperer.
B: She just has candy.
A: Ah, bribery.
B: Works every time.
japanese: 扱（あつか）いがうまい。／手（て）懐（なず）けてる。
vietnamese: Có khiếu về... / Mát tay với...
chinese: 擅长 (Shàncháng) / 有一套 (Yǒu yī tào)

japanese: 天才（てんさい）だね。／素質（そしつ）あるよ。
spanish: Tienes un don natural / Has nacido para esto
vietnamese: Cậu có năng khiếu bẩm sinh đấy / Sinh ra để làm việc này rồi
example:
A: I've never skied before.
B: Wow, you're a natural.
A: Or I'm just falling gracefully.
B: It looks good.
A: My knees hurt.
B: Drink hot cocoa.
chinese: 你真是个天才 (Nǐ zhēnshì gè tiāncái) / 你天生就是干这个的 (Nǐ tiānshēng jiùshì gàn zhège de)

primary: You have a way with words
vietnamese: Khéo ăn nói thật / Dẻo mồm dẻo miệng ghê
example:
A: I convinced him to give us a discount.
B: You have a way with words.
A: I just cried.
B: Manipulative.
A: Whatever works.
B: True.
japanese: 口（くち）がうまいね。／言葉（ことば）巧（たく）みだ。
spanish: Tienes labia / Qué bien hablas
chinese: 你真会说话 (Nǐ zhēn huì shuōhuà) / 你口才真好 (Nǐ kǒucái zhēn hǎo)

japanese: 自然（しぜん）とできるようになった。／体（からだ）が覚（おぼ）えた。
similar: I didn't have to try hard.
I have a knack for it.
spanish: Me salió natural / Me salió solo
vietnamese: Tự nhiên tôi biết thôi / Cái này tôi thạo từ trong máu rồi
primary: It just came naturally to me
example:
A: How did you learn piano?
B: It just came naturally to me.
A: Mozart reborn?
B: Maybe.
A: Play something.
B: Chopsticks.
chinese: 我自然而然就会了 (Wǒ zìrán'érrán jiù huì le)

vietnamese: Áng chừng xem / Cho con số đại khái đi
japanese: ざっくりでいいから。／大体（だいたい）で。
example:
A: How much will the renovation cost?
B: I haven't gotten the final quote yet.
A: Can you just ballpark it for me?
B: I’d say somewhere between 10k and 15k.
A: Thanks, that rough estimate helps a lot.
B: Just keep in mind, the price might change.
similar: 5. Just a stab in the dark
spanish: Dámelo a ojo / Haz un cálculo aproximado
chinese: 估算一下 (Gūsuàn yīxià) / 给个大概 (Gěi gè dàgài)

japanese: 自分（じぶん）に厳（きび）しいね。／ストイックだね。
vietnamese: Cậu kỷ luật thật đấy / Sống có nề nếp ghê
spanish: Eres muy disciplinado / Tienes mucha fuerza de voluntad
example:
A: I wake up at 4 AM to run.
B: You're very disciplined.
A: Then I meditate.
B: I sleep till noon.
A: Try it.
B: No thanks.
chinese: 你很自律 (Nǐ hěn zìlǜ)

japanese: 鳥肌（とりはだ）が立（た）つ。／ゾクゾクする。
spanish: Me da escalofríos / Se me pone la piel de gallina
vietnamese: Nổi hết da gà / Rùng mình luôn
example:
A: Did you hear that song?
B: It gives me chills.
A: In a good way?
B: Yes, she's amazing.
A: Encore.
B: Please.
chinese: 让我起鸡皮疙瘩 (Ràng wǒ qǐ jīpígēda)

vietnamese: Tôi quen thói... / Nó thành thói quen rồi
japanese: 癖（くせ）になっちゃった。／習慣（しゅうかん）づいた。
primary: I got in the habit of
example:
A: You bite your nails.
B: I got in the habit of it during finals.
A: Stop it.
B: I'm trying.
A: Use the bitter polish.
B: It tastes bad.
spanish: Cogí la costumbre de / Me acostumbré a
chinese: 我养成了...的习惯 (Wǒ yǎngchéng le... de xíguàn)

example:
A: You ruined my shirt.
B: Let me make it up to you. Dinner?
A: Steak.
B: Done.
A: And dessert.
B: Fine.
primary: Let me make it up to you
spanish: Déjame compensarte / Deja que te lo compense
japanese: 償（つぐな）わせて。／借（か）りを返（かえ）させて。
vietnamese: Để tôi đền bù cho cậu / Để tôi chuộc lỗi nhé
chinese: 让我补偿你 (Ràng wǒ bǔcháng nǐ)

vietnamese: Người hơi oải / Thấy trong người không khỏe
japanese: 具合（ぐあい）が悪（わる）い。／調子（ちょうし）が出（で）ない。
example:
A: Want to go hiking?
B: I'm feeling under the weather.
A: Fever?
B: Just tired and achey.
A: Stay home.
B: Will do.
spanish: Me encuentro mal / Estoy pocho
chinese: 我身体不太舒服 (Wǒ shēntǐ bù tài shūfu) / 我有点不舒服 (Wǒ yǒudiǎn bù shūfu)

vietnamese: Nổi đóa lên / Rung bần bật
japanese: バズる。／キレる。
example:
A: Why is your phone buzzing?
B: My tweet blew up.
A: Famous?
B: Briefly.
A: Don't forget us little people.
B: Never.
spanish: Explotar / Ponerse hecho una furia
chinese: 发火 (Fāhuǒ) / 爆发 (Bàofā) (also: 爆红 bàohóng for fame)

example:
A: How's the view?
B: As good as it gets.
A: Paradise?
B: Pretty much.
A: Send photos.
B: Already did.
spanish: No hay nada mejor / Inmejorable
japanese: これ以上（いじょう）ない。／最高（さいこう）だ。
vietnamese: Hết nước chấm / Tuyệt vời ông mặt trời
chinese: 最好也就这样了 (Zuìhǎo yě jiù zhèyàng le) / 已经很完美了 (Yǐjīng hěn wánměi le)

japanese: きっぱりやめる。／スパッとやめる。
vietnamese: Cai nghiện cái rụp / Bỏ ngang xương
spanish: Dejarlo de golpe / Cortar por lo sano
example:
A: You smoke?
B: I quit cold turkey last week.
A: Hard?
B: Hell yes.
A: Stay strong.
B: I need gum.
chinese: 突然戒掉 (Tūrán jièdiào) / 一次性戒断 (Yīcìxìng jièduàn)

japanese: ガチだ。／本物（ほんもの）だ。
spanish: Legítimo / De verdad
vietnamese: Uy tín luôn / Hàng chuẩn đấy
example:
A: Is this bag real?
B: It's legit.
A: How do you know?
B: I know a guy.
A: Suspicious.
B: Trust me.
chinese: 正宗 (Zhèngzōng) / 靠谱 (Kàopǔ)

example:
A: Are you playing against Tom?
B: Yeah, he's no match for me.
A: Don't be cocky.
B: Just confident.
A: We'll see.
B: Watch and learn.
japanese: 相手（あいて）にならない。／敵（てき）じゃない。
similar: He's out of my league (in reverse).
vietnamese: Cậu ta tuổi gì sánh với tôi / Không có cửa đấu với tôi
spanish: No es rival para mí / No tiene nada que hacer contra mí
chinese: 他不是我的对手 (Tā bùshì wǒ de duìshǒu)

japanese: 慎重（しんちょう）に。／焦（あせ）らないで。
spanish: Con calma / Despacito
vietnamese: Từ từ thôi / Nhẹ tay thôi
example:
A: I'm trying to move this glass table alone.
B: Wait, let me help. Easy does it!
A: It's heavier than it looks, for sure.
B: Just steady now, don't hit the wall.
A: Almost there... just one more foot.
B: Perfect, let's set it down handle with care.
chinese: 慢慢来 (Mànman lái) / 别着急 (Bié zháojí)

japanese: りょうかい。／オッケー。
vietnamese: Oki con dê / Duyệt luôn
example:
A: Pick up milk.
B: Okie dokie.
A: And eggs.
B: Okie dokie artichokie.
A: Stop rhyming.
B: Nope.
spanish: Ok / Vale / Oki doki
chinese: 好哒 (Hǎo da) / 没问题 (Méi wèntí)

japanese: ビミョー。／イマイチ。
