meaning: (비교 대상이 안 될 정도로) 전혀 다른 것
chinese: 截然不同 (Jiérán bùtóng) / 根本不是一回事 (Gēnběn bùshì yī huí shì)
去过你自己的生活吧 (Qùguò nǐ zìjǐ de shēnghuó ba) / 找点正经事做 (Zhǎo diǎn zhèngjing shì zuò)
spanish: Búscate una vida / Haz algo de provecho
meaning: 인생 좀 살아라, 쓸데없는 짓 그만해라
japanese: 現実（げんじつ）を見（み）ろ／いい加減（かげん）にしろ
vietnamese: Kiếm việc gì có ích mà làm đi / Bớt lo chuyện bao đồng đi
example:
A: Are you seriously stalking his ex again?
B: Just checking his stories, relax.
A: You need to get a life, for real.
B: I have a life! This is research.
A: It's pathetic. Go touch some grass.
B: Fine, putting the phone down.

primary: Don't dwell on it / Move on / Don't linger on it
example:
A: I still can't believe I made that typo in the email. 
B: Don't dwell on it, everyone makes mistakes. 
A: But the client noticed it immediately. 
B: It’s water under the bridge now, just move on. 
A: I'll try to put it behind me by tomorrow. 
B: Good, don't linger on it and focus on the next task.
meaning: (안 좋은 일에) 미련 갖지 마 / 잊어버려
chinese: 别再想了 (Bié zài xiǎng le) / 往前看 (Wǎng qián kàn)
vietnamese: Đừng day dứt mãi chuyện đó / Cho qua đi
spanish: Pasa página / No le des más vueltas
japanese: くよくよしないで／切（き）り替（か）えて

spanish: La gente no cambia / Genio y figura hasta la sepultura
japanese: 人（ひと）は変（か）わらない
example:
A: He swore he'd stop flaking.
B: Girl, people never change.
A: But he seemed so sincere this time.
B: They always do. Don't fall for it.
A: Maybe I'm just naive.
B: A little. Old habits die hard.
vietnamese: Giang sơn dễ đổi bản tính khó dời / Người ta khó thay đổi lắm
similar: Once a cheater always a cheater
chinese: 江山易改本性难移 (Jiāngshān yì gǎi běnxìng nán yí) / 本性难移 (Běnxìng nán yí)

spanish: ¡Es mi canción! / Me encanta esto
example:
A: Do you want to go to a jazz club tonight? 
B: Oh, that's my jam! I love jazz. 
A: I found a great spot in Greenwich Village. 
B: Perfect, live music is right up my alley. 
A: I knew you'd be down for it. 
B: Definitely, I'm all about that kind of atmosphere.
japanese: 最高（さいこう）！／ドハマりしてる！
chinese: 那是我的最爱 (Nà shì wǒ de zuì'ài) / 我就喜欢这个 (Wǒ jiù xǐhuan zhège)
vietnamese: Bài tủ của tôi đấy / Gu của tôi
meaning: 완전 내 취향이야 / 내가 정말 좋아하는 거야

example:
A: My trip to the Maldives was absolutely breathtaking. 
B: I'm just living vicariously through your photos. 
A: You should really go there yourself next year. 
B: I wish, but for now, I'm just living through you. 
A: I'll send you more videos of the ocean. 
B: Please do, I love the second-hand excitement!
vietnamese: Sống hộ cuộc đời người khác / Tìm vui qua trải nghiệm người khác
chinese: 通过他人间接体验 (Tōngguò tārén jiànjiē tǐyàn) / 代入感 (Dàirùgǎn)
japanese: （人（ひと）の話（はなし）や体験（たいけん）で）代理（だいり）満足（まんぞく）する
similar: 2. Getting a thrill from your story
spanish: Vivir a través de ti / Vivirlo como si fuera yo
¿Cuánto es la dolorosa? / ¿De cuánto estamos hablando?
similar: 5. How much did it set me back?
japanese: （お会計（かいけい））いくら？
example:
A: That was an incredible dinner, I'm stuffed. 
B: Alright, let's get the check. What's the damage? 
A: It looks like it's about a hundred dollars. 
B: Not bad for a three-course meal. What do I owe ya? 
A: Just give me fifty and we're even. 
B: Sounds fair, let's settle the tab and head out.
vietnamese: Thiệt hại bao nhiêu? / Hết bao nhiêu tiền?
chinese: 多少钱? (Duōshǎo qián?) / 花了多少? (Huāle duōshǎo?)
meaning: (주로 계산할 때) 얼마 나왔어? / 비용이 얼마야?

spanish: Nací así / Es mi naturaleza
example:
A: How are you always so optimistic about everything? 
B: I don't know, I guess I was born this way. 
A: I wish I had your natural energy in the morning. 
B: It’s just in my DNA, I’ve always been like this. 
A: It must be nice to never feel grumpy. 
B: Well, it’s just who I am, I can't really change it.
japanese: 生（う）まれつきだよ／昔（むかし）からこうだよ
chinese: 我天生就这样 (Wǒ tiānshēng jiù zhèyàng) / 生来如此 (Shēnglái rúcǐ)
vietnamese: Sinh ra đã thế rồi / Bản tính trời sinh
meaning: 난 원래 이래 / 태어날 때부터 이랬어

spanish: Al final del día / En resumidas cuentas
chinese: 关键在于 (Guānjiàn zàiyú) / 归根结底 (Guīgēnjiédǐ)
vietnamese: Tóm lại là / Mấu chốt là
japanese: 結局（けっきょく）のところ／要（よう）するに
similar: 3. At the end of the day
5. The crux of the matter is
example:
A: We’ve discussed the budget and the timeline for hours. 
B: The bottom line is we need more funding. 
A: So, in a nutshell, we can't start tomorrow? 
B: Essentially, no, not without the extra cash. 
A: At the end of the day, it's all about the money. 
B: Exactly, that’s the crux of the matter.

chinese: 别惹是生非 (Bié rěshìshēngfēi) / 别捅马蜂窝 (Bié tǒng mǎfēngwō)
vietnamese: Đừng chọc ổ kiến lửa / Đừng vuốt râu hùm
japanese: 余計（よけい）なことはしないで／寝（ね）た子（こ）を起（お）こすな
meaning: 긁어 부스럼 만들지 마 (잠자고 있는 사자를 건드리지 마)
spanish: No busques problemas / No le busques tres pies al gato
example:
A: I’m going to ask the boss for another day off. 
B: He’s in a terrible mood, don't poke the bear. 
A: But I really need to finish my errands. 
B: Trust me, just let sleeping dogs lie today. 
A: You’re right, I shouldn't push my luck. 
B: Good call, don't stir the pot when he’s like this.

chinese: 走着瞧吧 (Zǒuzheqiáo ba) / 看看再说 (Kànkan zàishuō)
example:
A: Should we book the outdoor venue for the party? 
B: The weather looks iffy, so let's see how it goes. 
A: We can't wait too long or it'll be taken. 
B: Let's play it by ear for a few more days. 
A: Okay, let's see how the dust settles with the forecast. 
B: Exactly, we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
japanese: 様子（ようす）を見（み）よう／流（なが）れに任（まか）せよう
spanish: Vamos viendo / Ya veremos qué pasa
similar: 5. Cross that bridge when we come to it
vietnamese: Để xem tình hình thế nào đã / Cứ chờ xem sao

example:
A: Should we accept this partnership deal? 
B: Absolutely, it’s a no brainer. 
A: The terms are much better than the last one. 
B: Definitely, it’s an easy call for the board. 
A: I thought it would be a slam dunk too. 
B: It’s a total cinch, let's sign it now.
chinese: 显而易见的事 (Xiǎn'éryìjiàn de shì) / 想都不用想 (Xiǎng dōu bùyòng xiǎng)
japanese: 考（かんガ）えるまでもないこと／簡単（かんたん）なこと
meaning: 아주 쉬운 결정 / 생각할 필요도 없는 일
spanish: Está chupado / Es pan comido
vietnamese: Dễ như ăn cháo / Việc này khỏi phải nghĩ

example:
A: I don't think I've seen Mark all week. 
B: Come to think of it, he wasn't at the meeting either. 
A: Now that I think about it, he mentioned a vacation. 
B: On second thought, maybe he’s just working from home. 
A: Actually, I should check the shared calendar. 
B: Good idea, thinking back, he did seem busy lately.
chinese: 经你这么一说 (Jīng nǐ zhème yī shuō) / 想起来了 (Xiǎng qǐlái le)
similar: 1. Now that I think about it
5. When you put it that way
spanish: Ahora que lo pienso / Pensándolo bien
vietnamese: Nghĩ lại thì... / Nhắc mới nhớ...
japanese: そういえば／考（かんが）えてみれば

meaning: 나에 대해 좋게 말해줘 (나 좀 잘 봐달라고 해줘)
spanish: Habla bien de mí / Échame un cable con eso
japanese: 私（わたし）のことを良（よ）く言（い）っておいて／口添（くちゾ）えして
chinese: 替我美言几句 (Tì wǒ měiyán jǐ jù) / 帮我说好话 (Bāng wǒ shuō hǎohuà)
example:
A: I heard you’re close with the hiring manager. 
B: Yeah, we’ve worked together for years. 
A: Could you put in a good word for me? 
B: Of course, I'd be happy to vouch for you. 
A: Thanks, I really need someone to talk me up. 
B: Don't worry, I'll give you a boost during lunch.
primary: Put in a good word for me
vietnamese: Nói đỡ cho tôi vài câu nhé / Khen tôi vài câu với sếp nhé

spanish: Estoy reventado / Estoy hecho polvo
vietnamese: Đuối quá / Mệt rã rời
chinese: 我累垮了 (Wǒ lèi kuǎ le) / 精疲力竭 (Jīngpílìjié)
example:
A: Want to grab some drinks after the shift? 
B: Sorry, I'm wiped out today. 
A: It was a pretty hectic day, wasn't it? 
B: I'm spent, I just want to go home and sleep. 
A: I get it, I'm feeling pretty beat myself. 
B: My brain is fried; I’m totally running on empty.
japanese: ヘトヘトだよ／全（まった）く力（ちから）が残（のこ）ってない

spanish: Me pasó por encima / Me trató como un trapo
japanese: 彼女（かのじょ）にいいように扱（あつか）われた／踏（ふ）みつけにされた
chinese: 她对我呼来喝去 (Tā duì wǒ hū lái hè qù) / 她根本不把我放在眼里 (Tā gēnběn bù bǎ wǒ fàng zài yǎn lǐ)
example:
A: Why did you agree to do all her work again? 
B: I don't know, she just walked all over me. 
A: You need to stop letting people treat you like a doormat. 
B: I know, she really knows how to push me around. 
A: She definitely took advantage of your kindness. 
B: Next time, I won't let her run roughshod over me.
vietnamese: Cô ta đè đầu cưỡi cổ tôi / Cô ta coi tôi không ra gì
similar: 1. She treated me like a doormat

example:
A: I can't believe I missed that deadline. 
B: Don't be so hard on yourself, it was a tight schedule. 
A: I feel like I let the whole team down. 
B: Cut yourself some slack, you've been working so hard. 
A: Thanks, I’ll try not to beat myself up about it. 
B: Good, just give yourself a break for tonight.
meaning: 자신을 너무 달달 볶지 마 / 자책하지 마
primary: Don't be so hard on yourself
chinese: 别对自己太苛刻 (Bié duì zìjǐ tài kēkè) / 别太为难自己 (Bié tài wéinán zìjǐ)
vietnamese: Đừng tự dằn vặt bản thân / Đừng khắt khe với mình quá
spanish: No seas tan duro contigo mismo / Tente paciencia
japanese: 自分（じぶん）を責（せ）めないで

spanish: Se pone de malas si tiene hambre / El hambre lo pone insoportable
chinese: 他一饿就脾气暴躁 (Tā yī è jiù píqi bàozào) / 饿肚子让他心情不好 (È dùzi ràng tā xīnqíng bù hǎo)
example:
A: Why is James being so short with everyone? 
B: He's moody when he's starving, haven't you noticed? 
A: Oh, so he's just hangry? 
B: Exactly, he's always a grouch when he hasn't eaten. 
A: Let's get him some pizza before the meeting. 
B: Good idea, he's way too cranky on an empty stomach.
vietnamese: Đói là ổng quạu đấy / Hễ đói là khó ở
japanese: 彼（かれ）は空腹（くうふく）だと不機嫌（ふきげん）になる
similar: 4. He's cranky on an empty stomach
5. He needs a snack to be human

primary: it just popped up in my mind / It just occurred to me
spanish: Se me ocurrió de la nada / Me vino a la mente
example:
A: How did you solve that bug so quickly?
B: It just popped up in my mind while I was making tea.
A: That’s a total flash of inspiration.
B: It just occurred to me that the syntax was wrong.
A: Well, I'm glad it hit you when it did.
B: Me too, it saved us hours of work.
chinese: 我突然想到 (Wǒ tūrán xiǎngdào) / 脑子里灵光一现 (Nǎozi lǐ língguāng yī xiàn)
japanese: ふと思（おも）い浮（う）かんだ
vietnamese: Tự nhiên nảy ra trong đầu / Bất chợt nghĩ ra

japanese: 足（あし）が長（なが）いのは家系（かけい）だ／遺伝（いでん）だ
vietnamese: Nhà tôi có gen chân dài / Cả nhà chân dài
primary: Long legs run in the family
example:
A: Your brother is so tall!
B: Yeah, long legs run in the family.
A: My family is the exact opposite.
B: It's definitely in our genes; my dad is tall too.
A: Must be nice to reach the top shelf.
B: It’s a useful family trait, I guess.
spanish: Viene de familia / Lo llevamos en la sangre
chinese: 长腿是家传的 (Cháng tuǐ shì jiāchuán de) / 我们家都是大长腿 (Wǒmen jiā dōu shì dà cháng tuǐ)

spanish: De tal palo, tal astilla / Hijo de tigre, pintito
japanese: 蛙（かえる）の子（こ）は蛙（かえる）／似（に）たもの親子（おやこ）
vietnamese: Con nhà tông không giống lông cũng giống cánh / Cha nào con nấy
primary: Apple doesn't fall far from the tree
chinese: 有其父必有其子 (Yǒu qí fù bì yǒu qí zǐ) / 孩子随爹妈 (Háizi suí diēmā)
example:
A: Did you see his kid fixing that computer?
B: Apple doesn't fall far from the tree.
A: He's a genius, just like his father.
B: He’s definitely a chip off the old block.
A: It's amazing to see him follow in those footsteps.
B: Like father, like son, as they say.

chinese: 突然 (Tūrán) / 冷不丁地 (Lěngbùdīng de)
spanish: De la nada / Sin previo aviso
example:
A: Why are you calling me at 3 AM?
B: Sorry, this idea just came out of the blue.
A: It’s totally unexpected, even for you.
B: I know, it came out of left field and I had to share.
A: Can it wait until tomorrow morning?
B: Probably, but it was just such a sudden thought.
japanese: 出（だ）し抜（ぬ）けに／突然（とつぜん）
vietnamese: Bất thình lình / Tự nhiên đùng cái

chinese: 罕见 (Hǎnjiàn) / 千载难逢 (Qiānzǎinánféng)
vietnamese: Năm thuở mười thì / Hiếm khi lắm
japanese: ごく稀（まれ）に／滅多（めった）にない
spanish: Una vez a las quinientas / Muy de vez en cuando
example:
A: Do you ever go to that fancy French restaurant?
B: Only once in a blue moon.
A: Is it too expensive or just too far?
B: Both, so we very rarely go there.
A: We go about once a year for our anniversary.
B: Yeah, it’s a once in a lifetime kind of price.

chinese: 他居然还有脸来这儿 (Tā jūrán hái yǒu liǎn lái zhèr) / 胆子不小敢露面 (Dǎnzi bù xiǎo gǎn lòumiàn)
spanish: Qué descaro aparecer por aquí / Hay que tener cara
japanese: よくもぬけぬけと来（こ）られたものだ／厚顔無恥（こうがんむち）な
primary: He has an audacity to show up here
example:
A: I can't believe Mark is at the party after the fight.
B: He has an audacity to show up here.
A: He really has some nerve after what he said.
B: What a cheek, acting like nothing happened.
A: I’m surprised nobody has kicked him out yet.
B: He’s got some gall, that’s for sure.
vietnamese: Mặt dày thật, còn dám vác xác đến đây / Gan to nhỉ, dám mò tới đây
Tuy nhiên, vẫn phải cẩn thận / Nói thì nói thế, cơ mà nhớ cẩn thận
example:
A: The neighborhood is generally very safe.
B: That being said, be cautious at night.
A: Should I avoid certain streets?
B: Mind you, it’s better to stick to the main roads.
A: Thanks, I’ll be sure to watch out.
B: Still, it’s always good to be aware of your surroundings.
japanese: とは言（い）っても気（き）をつけて／そうは言（い）うけど
chinese: 虽说如此，还是要小心 (Suīshuō rúcǐ, háishì yào xiǎoxīn) / 话虽这么说但我还是得提醒你 (Huà suī zhème shuō dàn wǒ háishì děi tíxǐng nǐ)
spanish: Dicho esto, ten cuidado / Aun así, ándate con ojo

example:
A: Have you heard the urban legend about this building?
B: Yeah, it gives me a chill just thinking about it.
A: They say the elevators move on their own at night.
B: Stop, you’re giving me the creeps!
A: Sorry, I didn't mean to send shivers down your spine.
B: It’s just a really hair-raising story.
japanese: ゾッとする／寒気（さむけ）がする
vietnamese: Lạnh sống lưng / Nổi da gà
spanish: Me da escalofríos / Se me pone la piel de gallina
chinese: 瑟瑟发抖 (Sèsè fādǒu) / 让我感到一阵恶寒 (Ràng wǒ gǎndào yīzhèn èhán)
你这说法有点离谱 (Nǐ zhè shuōfǎ yǒudiǎn lípǔ) / 听起来不太靠谱 (Tīng qǐlái bù tài kàopǔ)
primary: You seemed a little far fetched
vietnamese: Nghe hơi ảo đấy / Có vẻ hơi phi lý
japanese: ちょっと信（しん）じ難（がた）い／話（はなし）が大（おお）きすぎる
example:
A: He claims he met a billionaire at the bus stop.
B: You seemed a little far fetched there.
A: Why? It could happen in New York.
B: It’s a bit of a stretch, don't you think?
A: I suppose it does sound a bit fishy.
B: Yeah, that story is definitely hard to swallow.
spanish: Me sonó a cuento chino / Suena un poco exagerado

vietnamese: Suýt nữa là xuống tiền rồi / Tí thì múc luôn
meaning: 당장이라도 그걸 살 것 같아 (지름신 강림 직전)
primary: I'm a second away from buying that
japanese: 喉（のど）から手（て）が出（で）るほど欲（ほ）しい／買（か）いそうだ
chinese: 我马上就要买了 (Wǒ mǎshàng jiù yào mǎi le) / 差一点就剁手了 (Chà yīdiǎnr jiù duòshǒu le)
example:
A: That new phone looks incredible on you.
B: I'm a second away from buying that.
A: It’s a lot of money, though.
B: I know, I’m on the verge of buying it right now.
A: Maybe you should sleep on it first.
B: I’m this close to getting it, the temptation is real.
spanish: Estoy a un paso de comprarlo / Casi, casi me lo llevo

vietnamese: Cậu coi tôi là cái gì? / Mày nghĩ tao là ai?
primary: What do you take me for?
japanese: 私（わたし）を誰（だれ）だと思（おも）ってるの？／馬鹿（ばか）にしないで
example:
A: Are you sure you can handle this deal alone? 
B: What do you take me for? I've done this before. 
A: I just don't want you to get tricked. 
B: I'm not that naive, I know exactly what I'm doing. 
A: Fair enough, I'll leave it to you. 
B: Thanks, just give me some credit for my experience.
similar: 1. Who do you think I am?
2. Do I look like a fool?
spanish: ¿Por quién me tomas? / ¿Me ves cara de tonto?
chinese: 你把我当成什么人了? (Nǐ bǎ wǒ dàngchéng shénme rén le?) / 瞧不起谁呢 (Qiáobùqǐ shéi ne)

vietnamese: Tôi lỡ trớn / Hưng phấn quá đà
spanish: Se me fue la mano / Me dejé llevar
example:
A: You bought ten pairs of shoes today? 
B: Yeah, I guess I got carried away at the mall. 
A: You definitely went overboard with the credit card. 
B: I just got too excited when I saw the 70% off sign. 
A: You should probably return at least half of them. 
B: I know, I was just caught up in the moment.
japanese: 調子（ちょうし）に乗（の）りすぎた／夢中（むちゅう）になりすぎた
chinese: 我做得过火了 (Wǒ zuò de guòhuǒ le) / 一时冲动 (Yīshí chōngdòng)
我不是个爱后悔的人 (Wǒ bùshì gè ài hòuhuǐ de rén) / 往事随风 (Wǎngshì suí fēng)
primary: I'm not big on holding things with regret.
similar: 1. I don't dwell on the past
example:
A: Do you ever wish you had taken that job in London? 
B: Not really, I'm not big on holding things with regret. 
A: It was such a great opportunity, though. 
B: Maybe, but I don't dwell on the past once I decide. 
A: I wish I could move on as quickly as you do. 
B: Life is too short to look back and wonder "what if."
vietnamese: Tôi không thích ôm hận đâu / Tôi không phải kiểu người hay hối tiếc
spanish: No soy de arrepentirme / Lo hecho, hecho está
japanese: 過去（かこ）を引（ひ）きずるタイプじゃない／後悔（こうかい）はしない主義（しゅぎ）だ

meaning: 한 귀로 듣고 한 귀로 흘리네
japanese: 馬（うま）の耳（みみ）に念仏（ねんぶつ）／右（みぎ）から左（ひだり）へ受（う）け流（なが）す
spanish: Le entra por uno y le sale por el otro / Como hablarle a la pared
primary: In one ear, out the other
chinese: 左耳进右耳出 (Zuǒ ěr jìn yòu ěr chū) / 当成耳边风 (Dàngchéng ěrbiānfēng)
vietnamese: Nước đổ đầu vịt / Vào tai này ra tai kia
example:
A: Did you tell him to bring the files for the meeting? 
B: I did, but with him, it's in one ear, out the other. 
A: He’s obviously not even listening when we talk. 
B: Exactly, it’s like I'm talking to a brick wall. 
A: We need to make sure he actually hears us this time. 
B: I'll send it in a text so it actually sinks in.

japanese: ここからいくら引（ひ）ける？／ベストプライスを出（だ）して
spanish: Dame tu mejor precio / ¿En cuánto me lo dejas?
chinese: 最低报价 (Zuìdī bàojià) / 最优价格 (Zuìyōu jiàgé)
vietnamese: Báo giá tốt nhất / Giá chốt hạ
example:
A: I need your absolute best quote.
B: This is as low as I can go.
A: Can't you knock off another hundred?
B: I'm already losing money on this.
A: Alright, send me the invoice.
B: Pleasure doing business with you.

spanish: No nací ayer / No soy tonto
chinese: 我又不是三岁小孩 (Wǒ yòu bùshì sān suì xiǎohái) / 我也走南闯北过 (Wǒ yě zǒunánchuǎngběi guò)
example:
A: Trust me, this investment is a guaranteed win. 
B: Look, I wasn't born yesterday, tell me the risks. 
A: I promise there's almost zero risk involved. 
B: You can't trick me with that "guaranteed" talk. 
A: I'm just trying to help you make some money. 
B: I've been around the block; I know nothing is free.
japanese: 世間（せけん）知（し）らずじゃない／子供（こども）じゃないんだから
meaning: 나도 알 건 다 알아 / 바보가 아니야
vietnamese: Tôi đâu có ngu / Tôi thừa biết tỏng rồi

spanish: Lo dejamos para otra / ¿Te parece si lo posponemos?
similar: 3. I'll have to pass for now
chinese: 改天吧 (Gǎitiān ba) / 下次再说 (Xiàcì zàishuō)
example:
A: Are we still on for the movies tonight? 
B: I’m swamped with work, let's take a raincheck. 
A: No problem, we can always go some other time. 
B: Thanks for understanding, can we reschedule for Friday? 
A: Friday works perfectly for me. 
B: Great, I'll have to pass for tonight and finish this.
japanese: また今度（こんど）にしよう。
vietnamese: Để dịp khác nhé / Hẹn lần sau nha

primary: There he goes again, acting up again
japanese: また始（はじ）まったよ。／彼（かれ）の癖（くせ）が出（De）た。
spanish: Ya está otra vez... / Ya empezó con lo mismo
example:
A: Why is Kevin yelling in the breakroom now? 
B: There he goes again, acting up again over nothing. 
A: It's the same old story every single Monday. 
B: He really loves making a scene in front of everyone. 
A: Someone needs to tell him to calm down. 
B: Here we go again, I'll go try to quiet him down.
chinese: 他又开始了 (Tā yòu kāishǐ le) / 没完没了了 (Méiwánméiliǎo le)
vietnamese: Lại thế nữa rồi, lại giở chứng / Lão lại lên cơn rồi

spanish: Es mi humilde opinión / Yo solo digo...
example:
A: I'm thinking of quitting my job and starting a blog. 
B: It's risky, but it could work. That's my two cents. 
A: I was hoping for a bit more encouragement. 
B: Just my thoughts, you should have a backup plan. 
A: I guess my take on it was a bit more optimistic. 
B: For what it's worth, I think you're talented enough.
japanese: あくまで私（わたし）の意見（いけん）だけどね。
vietnamese: Ý kiến của tôi là vậy / Tôi góp ý chút thế thôi
chinese: 这只是我的个人观点 (Zhè zhǐshì wǒ de gèrén guāndiǎn) / 拙见 (Zhuójiàn)

japanese: いいから聞（き）いて。／私（わたし）の言（い）うことをよく聞（き）け。
primary: Hear me out, read my lips
example:
A: I don't think we should invest in that startup. 
B: Hear me out, the market is shifting rapidly. 
A: But they haven't made a profit in three years. 
B: Read my lips: their new tech is a game changer. 
A: Okay, let me finish looking at their data first. 
B: Just pay attention to their patents, mark my words.
meaning: 내 말 끝까지 들어봐 / 똑똑히 들어
vietnamese: Nghe cho rõ đây / Nhìn mồm tôi nói này
spanish: Escúchame bien / Léeme los labios
chinese: 听我说完 (Tīng wǒ shuō wán) / 听清楚了 (Tīng qīngchu le)
始料未及 (Shǐliào wèi jí) / 真没想到 (Zhēn méi xiǎngdào)
primary: I didn't see this coming / I couldn't expect this
example:
A: The company is relocating to London next month.
B: Wow, I didn't see this coming. 
A: It was a sudden decision by the board.
B: I'm totally blindsided; I have a lease here.
A: Many of us are in the same boat.
B: This really came out of left field for everyone.
japanese: まさかこうなるとは思（おも）わなかった。／予想外（よそうがい）だ。
vietnamese: Không ngờ tới luôn / Ai mà lường trước được
spanish: No me lo vi venir / Me tomó por sorpresa

example:
A: You're going bungee jumping for your 60th birthday?
B: Why not? I'm still green at heart. 
A: I hope you've cleared this with your doctor.
B: Don't worry, I'm still young at heart. 
A: You definitely have more energy than I do.
B: Age is just a number if you keep a positive spirit.
chinese: 心态年轻 (Xīntài niánqīng) / 老当益壮 (Lǎodāngyìzhuàng)
japanese: 気持（きも）ちはまだ若（わか）いよ。
vietnamese: Tâm hồn vẫn còn phơi phới / Tâm hồn vẫn còn trẻ trung lắm
primary: I'm still green at heart / I'm young at heart
spanish: Soy joven de espíritu / El corazón no envejece

japanese: 嫌（いや）な予感（よかん）が拭（ぬぐ）えない。
vietnamese: Cứ có cảm giác sao sao ấy / Không dứt ra được cảm giác...
chinese: 这种感觉挥之不去 (Zhè zhǒng gǎnjué huīzhībùqù) / 总觉得哪里不对劲 (Zǒng juéde nǎlǐ bù duìjìn)
similar: 4. Can't put my finger on it
example:
A: Are you sure we turned off the stove before leaving?
B: I think so, but I can't shake the feeling that I forgot. 
A: We’re already ten miles away from home.
B: Something just feels off about today.
A: Let’s just turn back and check for peace of mind.
B: Thanks, that nagging thought would have ruined the trip.
spanish: No me quito la sensación de encima / Tengo un mal presentimiento

japanese: 空気（くうき）を読（よ）んで。
vietnamese: Nhìn tình hình chút đi / Tinh ý lên chút
chinese: 察言观色 (Cháyánguānsè) / 看看场合 (Kànkan chǎnghé)
example:
A: Why did you start talking about work during the party?
B: I just thought it was a good time to bring it up.
A: You really need to read the room. 
B: Was everyone really that uncomfortable?
A: Yes, you should check the vibe before talking shop.
B: My bad, I'll try to pick up on the mood next time.
meaning: 분위기 파악 좀 해 / 눈치 좀 챙겨
spanish: Lee el ambiente / Ubícate un poco

example:
A: Have you tried that new Italian place on 5th?
B: Yes, I can't recommend it enough. 
A: Is the pasta really as good as they say?
B: It's beyond impressive; you have to try the carbonara.
A: I'll make a reservation for this weekend then.
B: Trust me, it’s worth every penny.
chinese: 强烈推荐 (Qiángliè tuījiàn) / 怎么夸都不为过 (Zěnme kuā dōu bù wéigo)
vietnamese: Nên thử nha, cực phẩm đấy / Không chê vào đâu được (rất khuyến khích)
japanese: 本当（ほんとう）におすすめ！／絶対（ぜったい）いいよ！
spanish: Lo recomiendo muchísimo / Tienes que probarlo sí o sí

vietnamese: Hiểu ý / Bắt sóng được ngay
japanese: 気（き）づく／察（さっ）する
primary: Pick up on that / catch on to that / take a hint
chinese: 领会 (Lǐnghuì) / 识趣 (Shíqù)
example:
A: Did you notice Sarah was upset during lunch?
B: No, I didn't pick up on that at all. 
A: She barely said a word and left early.
B: I guess I failed to catch on to her mood. 
A: You really need to learn how to take a hint. 
B: I'll try to read between the lines better next time.
spanish: ¿Pillas la indirecta? / ¿Te das cuenta?
Utensilios de cocina / Cosas para la cocina
example:
A: I'm blowing my paycheck on kitchenware.
B: Le Creuset again?
A: I need that dutch oven!
B: You don't even cook.
A: It looks good on the stove though.
B: Expensive decoration, but okay.
vietnamese: Đồ bếp / Dụng cụ nấu ăn
japanese: 調理（ちょうり）器具（きぐ）／キッチン用品（ようひん）
chinese: 厨具 (Chújù) / 厨房用品 (Chúfáng yòngpǐn)

spanish: Cotorra / Habla hasta por los codos
chinese: 话痨 (Huàláo) / 碎嘴子 (Suìzuǐzi)
japanese: おしゃべりな人（ひと）
vietnamese: Đồ mỏ nhọn / Người nói nhiều
example:
A: How was your date with that guy from the app?
B: He was such a yapper, I couldn't get a word in. 
A: Oh no, was he at least interesting?
B: No, he was just a total chatterbox about his car.
A: That sounds like a long and exhausting night.
B: It was; he's a real motor mouth when he starts.

chinese: 大约 (Dàyuē) / 上下浮动 (Shàngxià fúdòng)
example:
A: How long will it take to finish this report?
B: About three hours, give or take. 
A: Can you get it done before the meeting at 4?
B: It’ll be done roughly by then, more or less.
A: Great, let me know if you need any help.
B: It’s approximately a ten-page document, so I'm good.
spanish: Más o menos / Aproximadamente
japanese: およそ／だいたい
vietnamese: Khoảng chừng / Tầm đó

chinese: 投缘 (Tóuyuán) / 一见如故 (Yījiànrúgù)
example:
A: How did the meeting with the new client go?
B: Great! We really hit it off during lunch. 
A: That’s a relief; I heard they were difficult.
B: Not at all, we clicked instantly over shared hobbies.
A: I'm glad to hear you're seeing eye to eye.
B: Yeah, we got along famously from the start.
japanese: 意気投合（いきとうごう）する／馬（うま）が合（あ）う
spanish: Hicimos buenas migas / Hubo química al instante
vietnamese: Hợp cạ / Tâm đầu ý hợp ngay

japanese: 彼（かれ）は優良（ゆうりょう）物件（ぶっけん）だ／逃（のが）したら損（そん）だ
chinese: 他真是个不可多得的好对象 (Tā zhēnshì gè bùkěduōdé de hǎo duìxiàng) / 一等一的好人选 (Yīděngyī de hǎo rénxuǎn)
vietnamese: Anh ấy là cực phẩm đấy / Mối ngon đấy
spanish: Es un partidazo / Es un tesoro
example:
A: I heard you're dating a doctor now.
B: Yeah, and he is a legit, catch.
A: Is he nice too, or just successful?
B: He's the total package, honestly.
A: I'm so happy you found a keeper.
B: Thanks, I feel like I struck gold.

spanish: Trágate el orgullo / Baja la cabeza esta vez
chinese: 放下身段 (Fàngxià shēnduàn) / 委曲求全 (Wěiqūqiúquán)
vietnamese: Dẹp cái tôi sang một bên / Hạ cái tôi xuống
japanese: プライドを捨（す）てる／折（お）れる
example:
A: I know you’re right, but I don't want to apologize.
B: Sometimes you just have to swallow your pride.
A: It’s just so hard to admit I was wrong.
B: Just suck it up and make things right.
A: Fine, I’ll put my ego aside this once.
B: Good call, it’ll save the friendship.

spanish: Volverse loco / Perder los papeles
chinese: 欣喜若狂 (Xīnxǐruòkuáng) / 发疯 (Fāfēng)
japanese: 気（き）が狂（くる）う／大興奮（だいこうふん）する
example:
A: The crowd is going to go bananas when the band starts.
B: I know, everyone has been waiting for hours.
A: I think I might flip out a little myself.
B: Don't go ballistic and lose your phone!
A: I’ll try to stay calm, but no promises.
B: Let's just go nuts and enjoy the show!
vietnamese: Phát rồ lên / Quẩy tới bến
Kín như bưng / Úp mở
similar: 1. Playing it close to the vest
japanese: 秘密（ひみつ）主義（しゅぎ）の／ハッキリ言（い）わない
example:
A: Did she say anything about the new project?
B: No, she was being very cagey about it.
A: Why is she so tight-lipped lately?
B: I think she's playing it close to the vest until it's official.
A: She’s always been a bit guarded with info.
B: Yeah, she can be really evasive when she wants to.
spanish: Reservado / No suelta prenda
chinese: 守口如瓶 (Shǒukǒurúpíng) / 故弄玄虚 (Gùnòngxuánxū)

vietnamese: Việc gì quan trọng làm trước / Đầu tiên là...
spanish: Lo primero es lo primero / Antes que nada
japanese: まず初（はじ）めに／最優先（さいゆうせん）事項（じこう）として
chinese: 先说正事儿 (Xiān shuō zhèngshìr) / 凡事有先后 (Fánshì yǒu xiānhòu)
example:
A: Should we book the flights or the hotel first?
B: First things first, let's check our budget.
A: That makes sense, priority one is the money.
B: To begin with, we need to know what we can afford.
A: I’ll pull up the spreadsheet now.
B: Great, let's start at the top and work down.

chinese: 至交 (Zhìjiāo) / 死党 (Sǐdǎng)
vietnamese: Bạn chí cốt / Cạ cứng
japanese: 親友（しんゆう）／マブダチ
example:
A: She's been my best friend since pre-K.
B: That's a long time to know someone.
A: We're practically sisters at this point.
B: Does she know all your secrets?
A: Every single one. She's my ride-or-die.
B: Those are the ones you keep.
spanish: Mejor amigo / Colega del alma
meaning: 가장 친한 친구, 평생 가는 절친

chinese: 运气不好的一天 (Yùnqì bù hǎo de yītiān) / 状态不佳 (Zhuàngtài bùjiā)
example:
A: You missed three shots in a row today.
B: Yeah, I'm just having an off day.
A: It’s okay, everyone has a rough one sometimes.
B: I feel totally out of sorts and off my game.
A: Don't let a small slump get you down.
B: Thanks, I'll try again tomorrow.
spanish: Tengo un mal día / Hoy no es mi día
meaning: 잘 안 풀리는 날 / 컨디션 난조인 날
vietnamese: Ngày xui xẻo / Nay không được độ
japanese: 調子（ちょうし）が出（で）ない日（ひ）／ツイてない日（ひ）
買（か）って後悔（こうかい）すること
example:
A: Do you like your new luxury watch?
B: Honestly, I’m having some buyer's remorse.
A: Why? It looks amazing on you.
B: I keep having second thoughts about the price.
A: The post-purchase blues are real, but it's a great piece.
B: I'm just wishing I could return it and save the money.
chinese: 剁手后的后悔 (Duòshǒu hòu de hòuhuǐ) / 买后悔了 (Mǎi hòuhuǐ le)
vietnamese: Hối hận sau khi mua / Mua xong tiếc tiền
spanish: Me arrepentí de comprarlo / Culpa del comprador

vietnamese: Tiện thể / Nhân tiện làm luôn
similar: 2. Kill two birds with one stone
chinese: 顺便 (Shùnbiàn) / 捎带手 (Shāodàishǒu)
example:
A: I'm going to the kitchen to get some water.
B: While at it, could you grab me a soda?
A: Sure thing, might as well kill two birds with one stone.
B: Thanks, I appreciate you helping me out.
A: No problem, I'll grab it along the way.
B: You're the best, thanks for being so helpful.
spanish: Ya que estás en eso / Aprovechando el viaje
japanese: ついでに

example:
A: I shouldn't eat this cake; I have a marathon to train for.
B: Oh, come on, live a little!
A: I’ve been so strict with my diet lately.
B: Just treat yourself once; you need to let loose.
A: Maybe you're right, I should loosen up.
B: Exactly, enjoy the ride and have a slice!
chinese: 享受生活 (Xiǎngshòu shēnghuó) / 活出点样来 (Huó chū diǎn yàng lái)
japanese: 楽（たの）しまなきゃ／息抜（いきぬ）きも必要（ひつよう）だよ
vietnamese: Hưởng thụ tý đi / Xõa đi
spanish: Disfruta un poco / Date un gusto
meaning: 인생을 좀 즐겨 / 좀 풀어져 봐

spanish: Y mira tú por dónde / Quién lo diría
japanese: 驚（おどろ）いたことに／案（あん）の定（じょう）
vietnamese: Và rồi, ngạc nhiên chưa / Thấy chưa, biết ngay mà
chinese: 结果你瞧 (Jiéguǒ nǐ qiáo) / 居然 (Jūrán)
example:
A: I thought I lost my keys for good. 
B: And then what happened?
A: Lo and behold, they were in my shoe!
B: Surprise, surprise. You always leave them in weird places.
A: Believe it or not, I checked that shoe twice already.
B: Well, at least you found them out of nowhere.

japanese: ぴったり／完璧（かんぺき）に
chinese: 恰到好处 (Qiàdàohǎochù) / 分毫不差 (Fēnháo bù chà)
example:
A: How was the custom suit I recommended?
B: It fits me to a t.
A: I knew that tailor would be spot on.
B: The measurements were just right, honestly.
A: He follows every instruction to the letter.
B: It's a perfect fit for my cousin's wedding.
vietnamese: Chuẩn không cần chỉnh / Giống y hệt
spanish: Al pie de la letra / Como anillo al dedo

example:
A: Is that your son? He's the spitting image of you.
B: Everyone says he's a dead ringer for my father.
A: No, he’s a carbon copy of your old high school photo.
B: I guess he really is a chip off the old block.
A: Even his smile is identical to yours.
B: It’s a bit scary how much he looks just like me.
vietnamese: Giống như đúc / Bản sao y hệt
spanish: Son dos gotas de agua / Es su vivo retrato
chinese: 简直一模一样 (Jiǎnzhí yīmúyīyàng) / 像从一个模子里刻出来的 (Xiàng cóng yīgè múzi lǐ kè chūlái de)
japanese: 瓜（うり）二（ふた）つ／そっくり

vietnamese: Bồn chồn / Đứng ngồi không yên
example:
A: You’ve been very quiet since we arrived at the gala.
B: I just feel a bit ill at ease in these formal settings.
A: You look a little on edge. Do you want to leave?
B: I feel totally out of place in this tuxedo.
A: Don't be so restless, you look great.
B: I’m just feeling uncomfortable with all these people watching.
japanese: 居心地（いごこち）が悪（わる）い／落（お）ち着（つ）かない
chinese: 局促不安 (Júcù bù'ān) / 心里不得劲 (Xīnlǐ bù déjìn)
spanish: Incómodo / Fuera de lugar

example:
A: Did you hear the news about Sarah’s promotion?
B: Yes, she’s absolutely over the moon about it.
A: She’s been walking on air all morning.
B: She’s totally stoked; she worked so hard for this.
A: I’m so thrilled for her, she deserves it.
B: She’s definitely on cloud nine right now.
vietnamese: Vui như tết / Sướng rơn
spanish: Más feliz que una lombriz / En el séptimo cielo
meaning: 너무나 행복한 / 붕 떠 있는 기분
chinese: 快活疯了 (Kuàihuó fēng le) / 乐不可支 (Lèbùkězhī)
japanese: 有頂天（うちょうてん）になる／最高（さいこう）に嬉（うれ）しい

chinese: 双标 (Shuāng biāo) / 严以待人宽以律己 (Yányǐ dàirén kuānyǐ lǜjǐ)
japanese: 不公平（ふこうへい）／ダブスタ（二重基準）
spanish: Doble moral / La ley del embudo
similar: 3. One rule for them, another for us
vietnamese: Tiêu chuẩn kép
example:
A: My boss gets mad when I'm late, but he’s never on time.
B: That’s a total double standard.
A: It’s pure hypocrisy if you ask me.
B: It’s always "one rule for them and another for us."
A: I’m tired of his inconsistent behavior.
B: It’s definitely an unfair bias in the office.

chinese: 贵得离谱 (Guì de lípǔ) / 要了老命了 (Yàole lǎomìng le)
vietnamese: Đắt cắt cổ / Giá trên trời
japanese: バカ高（たか）い／目玉（めだま）が飛（と）び出（で）るほど高（たか）い
primary: Cost an arm and a leg
spanish: Cuesta un ojo de la cara / Vale un riñón
example:
A: Did you see the price of the new iPhone?
B: Yeah, it’s going to cost an arm and a leg.
A: I don't want to break the bank just for a phone.
B: Honestly, it feels like daylight robbery these days.
A: Even for Apple, that price is a bit steep.
B: I’ll wait until it’s less pricey next year.

A: I heard about the car accident. Are you okay?
B: Luckily, I got out in one piece.
A: Thank goodness you're safe and sound.
B: The car is totaled, but I'm completely unharmed.
A: That’s the most important thing. Is the engine intact?
B: Not really, but I made it through without a scratch.
japanese: 無傷（むきず）で／五体（ごたい）満足（まんぞく）で
vietnamese: Lành lặn / Bình an vô sự

example:

B: Clearly, that's the best choice for the night.

example:

B: Trust me, he'll be well taken care of. 
A: I'll call them every day to check in. 
B: He's safe with them, just try to relax.

spanish: Dame un respiro / No seas tan pesado
chinese: 饶了我吧 (Ráo le wǒ ba) / 别逼得太紧 (Bié bī de tài jǐn)
japanese: 大目（おおめ）に見（み）てよ／勘弁（かんべん）してよ

example:

example:

B: Trust me, seeing is believing.

Explícamelo despacito / Desglósamelo
vietnamese: Giải thích từ từ thôi / Chỉ dẫn từng bước một đi

example:
A: I don't understand how this software works.

B: No problem, let's go over it again.

example:

B: Figures, he always loved the culture.

japanese: どうりで／やっぱりね

example:

A: Maybe I should talk to her more. 
B: As a matter of fact, you should!
chinese: 如果非要说的话 (Rúguǒ fēiyào shuō de huà) / 相反 (Xiāngfǎn)
更好的是 (Gèng hǎo de shì) / 索性 (Suǒxìng)
example:

B: Better yet, let's have a BBQ in the park!

B: Or, here's an idea: let's do both!

japanese: いっそのこと／もっといいのは

chinese: 气坏了我妹妹 (Qì huài le wǒ mèimei) / 让我妹妹很郁闷 (Ràng wǒ mèimei hěn yùmèn)

B: I think I will, I’m definitely not feeling 100%.

spanish: No soy yo mismo / Ando un poco perdido
chinese: 我不在状态 (Wǒ bù zài zhuàngtài) / 我有点反常 (Wǒ yǒudiǎn fǎncháng)

vietnamese: Lựa chọn tốt nhất cho cậu / Giải pháp an toàn nhất
spanish: Tu mejor opción / Lo más seguro es...
chinese: 你的最佳选择 (Nǐ de zuìjiā xuǎnzé) / 首选 (Shǒuxuǎn)
时间紧迫 (Shíjiān jǐnpò) / 没时间了 (Méi shíjiān le)
spanish: Ando corto de tiempo / El reloj corre
meaning: 시간이 좀 촉박해 / 시간이 다 되어 가
japanese: 時間（じかん）がない／急（いそ）いでいる
example:
A: Can we talk about the budget for five minutes? 
B: Make it quick, I'm a little pressed for time. 
A: I just need your signature on this form. 
B: The clock is ticking on my next meeting. 
A: I understand, it’ll only take a second. 
B: Good, because time is running out before I have to leave.
vietnamese: Tôi đang vội / Thời gian gấp lắm rồi
primary: I'm a little pressed for time / Clock is ticking / Time is running out

example:
A: How is the new management role going? 
B: To be honest, I think I can't hack it. 
A: Is it the workload or the people? 
B: Everything. I’m just totally out of my depth. 
A: Give it some time before you give up. 
B: I’m trying, but I’ve never felt this overwhelmed.
japanese: やってられない／手（て）に負（お）えない
vietnamese: Không chịu nổi nhiệt / Không làm nổi đâu
chinese: 驾驭不了 (Jiàyù bùliǎo) / 吃不消 (Chībuxiāo)
spanish: No me da el cuero / No puedo con esto

vietnamese: Nghe hơi sến sẩm tí / Nghe hơi cải lương nhưng mà...
chinese: 听起来可能有点俗气 (Tīng qǐlái kěnéng yǒudiǎn súqì) / 有点煽情 (Yǒudiǎn shānqíng)
japanese: 臭（くさ）いセリフだけど／陳腐（ちんぷ）に聞（き）こえるかもしれないけど
primary: It's going to sound corny / It might sound cheesy
example:
A: Why did you get me these flowers? 
B: It's going to sound corny, but I just wanted to see you smile. 
A: That’s actually really sweet. 
B: I know it might sound cheesy, but I mean it. 
A: Stop, you're making me blush. 
B: I guess I’m just feeling a bit sentimental today.
spanish: Va a sonar cursi / Sonará empalagoso

vietnamese: Cũng không tệ lắm đâu / Khá ra phết đấy
japanese: 悪（わる）くないね／なかなかいいじゃん
primary: It's not too shabby / Not that bad
example:
A: How do you like the new apartment? 
B: It's not too shabby for the price. 
A: The neighborhood seems not that bad either. 
B: It’s actually a pretty decent area. 
A: And the view from the balcony? 
B: It’s respectable, I can see the park from here.
spanish: No está nada mal / Se deja ver
chinese: 还凑合吧 (Hái còuhé ba) / 挺像那么回事的 (Tǐng xiàng nàme huí shì de)

spanish: Un spa para que me mimen / Un día de relax
vietnamese: Đi spa tút tát lại bản thân / Đi spa thư giãn tí
chinese: 去水疗放松一下 (Qù shuǐliáo fàngsōng yīxià) / 享受一下按摩 (Xiǎngshòu yīxià ànmó)
japanese: スパで癒（いや）されたい／自分（じぶん）へのご褒美（ほうび）
example:
A: You look like you need a vacation. 
B: I’m thinking of heading to a spa for some pampering. 
A: You definitely deserve a self-care day. 
B: I just want to spoil myself with a massage. 
A: Go for it, it’s much-needed relaxation time. 
B: I’m booking the luxury break right now.

chinese: 顺口就说出来了 (Shùnkǒu jiù shuō chūlái le) / 嘴快了 (Zuǐ kuài le)
vietnamese: Buột miệng nói ra thôi / Lỡ mồm thôi
example:
A: Why did you tell him about the surprise party? 
B: I’m so sorry, it just slipped out! 
A: We’ve been planning this for weeks! 
B: It was a total slip of the tongue, I swear. 
A: You always let things slip when you're excited. 
B: I know, I just spoke without thinking.
japanese: つい口（くち）が滑（すべ）った
spanish: Se me escapó / Se me salió sin querer

chinese: 苦乐参半 (Kǔlè cānbàn) / 悲喜交加 (Bēixǐ jiāojiā)
example:
A: How was your last day at the office? 
B: It was very bitter sweet, to be honest. 
A: I bet you'll miss the team. 
B: Yeah, I have mixed feelings about moving on. 
A: It’s a poignant moment in your career. 
B: It is, I’m happy for the new start but wistful about leaving.
vietnamese: Vừa vui vừa buồn / Cảm xúc lẫn lộn
spanish: Agridulce / Una de cal y una de arena
japanese: 嬉（うれ）しくもあり寂（さび）しくもある／複雑（ふくざつ）な気分（きぶん）

spanish: No te creas tanto / Bájale los humos
chinese: 别往自己脸上贴金 (Bié wǎng zìjǐ liǎn shàng tiējīn) / 别臭美了 (Bié chòuměi le)
primary: Don’t flatter yourself / Don’t give yourself too much credit
example:
A: I’m pretty sure she was staring at me all night. 
B: Don’t flatter yourself, she was looking at the exit. 
A: Hey, I’m a catch! 
B: Don’t give yourself too much credit, man. 
A: You're just jealous of my looks. 
B: Seriously, come back to earth and eat your pizza.
vietnamese: Bớt ảo tưởng sức mạnh đi / Đừng tự sướng nữa
japanese: 自意識（じいしき）過剰（かじょう）だよ／調子（ちょうし）に乗（の）るな

chinese: 别烦我了 (Bié fán wǒ le) / 别没完没了的 (Bié méiwánméiliǎo de)
vietnamese: Đừng có lải nhải nữa / Thôi đi, đừng làm phiền tôi nữa
spanish: Deja de dar la lata / No me agobies
japanese: しつこくしないで／うるさく言（い）わないで
example:
A: Did you finish the report? Did you? 
B: Stop pestering me, I told you I'll do it by 5. 
A: I just need to make sure it’s done. 
B: Enough with this, I’m working on it right now. 
A: I'm only asking because it's important. 
B: Stop nagging me and get off my back for a bit.
primary: Stop pestering me / Enough with this / Stop nagging me

example:
A: Is it okay if I eat the last slice of cake? 
B: Knock yourself out, I’m full anyway. 
A: Are you sure you don't want it later? 
B: Suit yourself, if you want it, take it. 
A: Thanks, I’ve been craving it all day. 
B: Be my guest, I’m done with sweets for now.
spanish: Házte el gusto / Tú mismo
japanese: 好（す）きにしなよ／ご自由（じゆう）に
vietnamese: Cứ tự nhiên đi / Tùy cậu thôi
chinese: 请便吧 (Qǐngbiàn ba) / 随你便 (Suí nǐ biàn)
跟你商量一下 (Gēn nǐ shāngliang yīxià) / 听听你的意见 (Tīngting nǐ de yìjiàn)
vietnamese: Hỏi ý kiến cậu chút / Bàn qua với cậu xíu
example:
A: I have a new idea for the marketing campaign. 
B: Great, run it by me during our meeting. 
A: I also wanted to touch base with you on the budget. 
B: Let's check in on that after lunch. 
A: I need to get your take on the final design too. 
B: Perfect, loop me in on the email chain.
spanish: Consultártelo / Comentártelo
primary: Run it by you / Touch base with you
japanese: 相談（そうだん）する／確認（かくにん）をとる

spanish: He estado liado / Ando a mil
example:
A: I haven't seen you at the gym lately.
B: Sorry, I've been busy with my personal stuff.
A: Is everything okay at home?
B: Yeah, just got a lot on my plate right now.
A: I get it, life can be a bit much sometimes.
B: Thanks for understanding, I'll be back soon.
japanese: 取（と）り込（こ）んでいる／忙（いそが）しい
similar: 1. With my personal stuff going on
2. I've got a lot on my plate
3. Life's been a bit much lately
vietnamese: Dạo này tôi bận quá / Mấy nay lu bu việc riêng
chinese: 我最近一直挺忙的 (Wǒ zuìjìn yīzhí tǐng máng de) / 俗事缠身 (Súshì chánshēn)

vietnamese: Không chết được đâu mà lo / Còn sống nhăn răng đây này
example:
A: I think I broke my toe hitting the desk!
B: Let me see. It’s just a tiny bruise.
A: It hurts so much, I might need a doctor.
B: You will live, stop overreacting.
A: You're so heartless sometimes.
B: Just put some ice on it and keep moving.
chinese: 你死不了 (Nǐ sǐ bùliǎo) / 这点小事难不倒你 (Zhè diǎn xiǎoshì nán bù dǎo nǐ)
japanese: 死（し）にゃしないよ／大丈夫（だいじょうぶ）だって
spanish: No te vas a morir / Sobrevivirás
Tomar bajo el ala / Apadrinar
chinese: 关照 (Guānzhào) / 提携 (Tíxié)
vietnamese: Dìu dắt / Bao bọc (như gà mẹ)
example:
A: I'm so nervous about starting this new job.
B: Don't worry, I've been here for five years.
A: Everything seems so complicated in New York.
B: I’ll take you under my wing and show you around.
A: That would be a huge help, thank you.
B: Just stay close and ask anything you need.
japanese: 面倒（めんどう）を見（み）る／世話（せわ）をする

spanish: Para nada / En balde
chinese: 白忙活了 (Bái mánghuo le) / 徒劳无功 (Túláowúgōng)
example:
A: Did you hear? The meeting was canceled.
B: What? I traveled 두 hours into the city!
A: I know, the boss had an emergency.
B: So all this preparation was for nothing?
A: Pretty much. We'll reschedule for next week.
B: I'm going to grab a drink; I'm exhausted.
vietnamese: Công cốc / Chẳng được tích sự gì
japanese: 無駄（むだ）だった／骨折（ほねお）り損（ぞん）

chinese: 欠你一个人情 (Qiàn nǐ yīgè rénqíng) / 下次补偿你 (Xiàcì bǔcháng nǐ)
japanese: 一（ひと）つ借（か）りね／恩（おん）に着（き）るよ
spanish: Te debo una / Estoy en deuda
example:
A: I covered your shift yesterday when you were sick.
B: Oh man, thank you so much for that.
A: No problem, I knew you weren't feeling well.
B: I really owe you one, seriously.
A: Just help me out next time I need a break.
B: You got it. I'll buy you coffee tomorrow.
vietnamese: Nợ cậu một ân huệ nhé / Mang ơn cậu lần này
similar: I’ll make it up to you

vietnamese: Có gian tình với nhau / Có gì đó mờ ám giữa hai đứa nó
example:
A: Did you see how Mark and Jen were talking?
B: Yeah, there's definitely something going on between them.
A: I thought they were just coworkers.
B: No way, the chemistry is too obvious.
A: I hope it doesn't make things awkward at work.
B: As long as they keep it professional, we're good.
spanish: Hay rollo entre ellos / Ahí hay tema
chinese: 关系不一般 (Guānxì bù yībān) / 眉来眼去 (Méiláiyǎnqù)
japanese: 何（なに）かあるね（恋愛（れんあい）関係（かんけい））／怪（あや）しいね

vietnamese: Làm rõ mọi chuyện / Giải tỏa hiểu lầm
japanese: わだかまりを解（と）く／誤解（ごかい）を晴（は）らす
spanish: Aclarar las cosas / Limpiar el ambiente
example:
A: We haven't spoken properly since the argument.
B: I know, it's been feeling really tense lately.
A: Can we sit down and clear the air?
B: I'd like that. I hate this awkwardness.
A: I'm sorry for what I said that night.
B: Thanks, I'm sorry for my reaction too.
chinese: 消除误会 (Xiāochú wùhuì) / 把话摊开说 (Bǎ huà tānkāi shuō)

japanese: 邪魔（じゃま）をする
example:
A: You should come with us to the Hamptons!
B: I don't want to get in the way of your family time.
A: Nonsense, the more the merrier!
B: Are you sure your parents won't mind?
A: They actually asked if you were coming.
B: Okay then, I'll start packing my bags.
spanish: Estorbar / Meterse en medio
chinese: 妨碍 (Fáng'ài) / 碍事 (Àishì)
vietnamese: Cản đường / Làm kỳ đà cản mũi

chinese: 神经紧绷 (Shénjīng jǐnbēng) / 处于崩溃边缘 (Chǔyú bēngkuì biānyuán)
vietnamese: Đang cau có / Đang căng như dây đàn
example:
A: Everyone in the office seems so on edge today.
B: It's because the quarterly deadline is tomorrow.
A: I noticed the boss is extra grumpy too.
B: Yeah, don't go in there unless it's an emergency.
A: I'll just stay at my desk and keep my head down.
B: Good idea, let's just survive until Friday.
japanese: ピリピリしている／気（き）が立（た）っている
spanish: Con los nervios de punta / Inquieto

japanese: マジかよ／なんてこった
vietnamese: Trời ạ! / Vãi thật!
chinese: 哎呀! (Āiyā!) / 天哪! (Tiān nǎ!)
spanish: ¡Tío! / ¡Macho!
example:
A: Man! The subway is delayed again!
B: This is the third time this week.
A: We're going to be so late for the show.
B: Maybe we should just grab an Uber.
A: Look at the traffic, it's not any faster.
B: You're right. NYC transit is a nightmare today.

A: It's finally warm enough to walk through Central Park.
B: I know, spring is definitely in the air.
A: Everyone looks so much happier today.
B: No more heavy coats and gray skies.
A: Let's grab some iced coffee and sit outside.
B: Perfect. I've been waiting months for this.
spanish: Ya huele a primavera / La primavera está en el aire
japanese: 春（はる）めいてきた／春（はる）の予感（よかん）
vietnamese: Xuân về rồi kìa / Thấy mùa xuân về rồi
chinese: 春意盎然 (Chūnyì àngrán) / 春天的气息 (Chūntiān de qìxī)

japanese: ヤバいね／信（しん）じられない
spanish: Es una locura / Qué fuerte
vietnamese: Điên rồ thật / Vô lý đùng đùng
example:
A: I heard the rent for that studio is $5,000.
B: That's crazy! It's barely 400 square feet.
A: Welcome to Manhattan, I guess.
B: I'd rather live in a tent in Jersey.
A: Well, somebody's clearly paying for it.
B: People have too much money to burn.
chinese: 太离谱了 (Tài lípǔ le) / 简直疯了 (Jiǎnzhí fēng le)

japanese: エイプリルフールだよ／嘘（うそ）だよ
spanish: ¡Inocente! / Día de los Inocentes
vietnamese: Cá tháng Tư / Bị lừa ngày cá tháng Tư rồi
chinese: 万愚节玩笑 (Wànyújié wánxiào) / 愚人节快乐 (Yúrénjié kuàilè)
example:
A: Did you see the news? Google is closing down!
B: Wait, what? Are you serious?
A: April Fools'! I totally got you.
B: Oh my god, my heart actually stopped.
A: You should have seen your face.
B: I'm never believing anything you say today.

A: I can't believe she talked to the manager behind my back.
B: That's so cold. I thought you guys were friends.
A: Me too, but I guess she wanted that promotion.
B: You need to be careful who you trust in this city.
A: Lesson learned. I'm keeping my distance now.
B: Good. Don't let her get to you.
vietnamese: Sau lưng người ta / Đâm lén sau lưng
chinese: 背地里 (Bèidìlǐ) / 背着人 (Bèizhe rén)
spanish: A mis espaldas / Por la espalda
japanese: 影（かげ）でこそこそと／隠（かく）れて

spanish: Salúdame a ~ / Dale recuerdos a ~
example:
A: I'm heading home to visit my family this weekend.
B: Oh, that's nice! Say hi to your mom for me.
A: Will do! She'll be happy to hear from you.
B: Tell her I'll visit her next time I'm in town.
A: She'd love that. Have a great weekend!
B: Thanks, you too. Travel safe.
japanese: 〜によろしく言（い）っておいて
vietnamese: Gửi lời chào... giúp tôi nhé / Nhắn... là tôi hỏi thăm nha
chinese: 代我向~问好 (Dài wǒ xiàng ~ wènhǎo) / 问个好 (Wèn gè hǎo)

example:
A: No offense, but that tie doesn't really match the suit.
B: Really? I thought the red looked good with gray.
A: It's a bit too bright for a formal interview.
B: I appreciate the honesty. I'll go change it.
A: Better safe than sorry when you're job hunting.
B: True. Thanks for looking out for me.
spanish: Sin ofender / No te lo tomes a mal
vietnamese: Không có ý xúc phạm đâu / Đừng giận nhé nhưng mà...
chinese: 别介意 (Bié jièyì) / 冒犯了 (Màofàn le)
meaning: 상처 주고 싶지 않지만 (기분 나쁘게 듣지 마)
japanese: 悪気（わるぎ）はないんだけど

chinese: 明白我的意思吗? (Míngbai wǒ de yìsi ma?) / 跟得上吗 (Gēn de shàng ma)
example:
A: First you go left, then two blocks down, then right.
B: Wait, slow down a bit.
A: It's just past the deli on the corner. Follow me?
B: Okay, I think I've got it now.
A: Good, because I'm not repeating it a third time.
B: Don't worry, I'll find my way.
spanish: ¿Me sigues? / ¿Me explico?
japanese: ついて来（き）てる？（話（はなし）についてきてる？）
vietnamese: Hiểu ý tôi chứ? / Theo kịp không?

example:
A: I'm so stressed about this presentation!
B: Dude, you need to chill out. You've prepared enough.
A: What if I forget everything on stage?
B: You won't. Just take a deep breath.
A: Maybe we should grab a beer after work.
B: Definitely. Let's just chill out at that new bar.
japanese: 落（お）ち着（つ）けよ／リラックスしろよ
chinese: 放松点儿 (Fàngsōng diǎnr) / 别紧张 (Bié jǐnzhāng)
vietnamese: Bình tĩnh đi / Xõa đi
spanish: Relájate / Bájale un cambio

chinese: 学费 (Xuéfèi) / 授课费 (Shòukèfèi)
vietnamese: Học phí / Tiền học
japanese: 学費（がくひ）／授業（じゅぎょう）料（りょう）
example:
A: NYU tuition is absolutely insane.
B: Tell me about it. It's robbery.
A: How do people afford this?
B: Loans until we die, basically.
A: Worth it for the degree though?
B: Ask me when I'm paying it off.
spanish: Matrícula / Lo que cuesta la carrera
Un día sí y uno no / Día por medio
example:
A: How often do you go to the gym?
B: I try to go every other day.
A: That's quite a lot! I can barely go once a week.
B: It's the only way I can stay sane in this city.
A: Maybe I should start a routine like that.
B: You're welcome to join me on Wednesdays.
chinese: 每隔一天 (Měi gé yītiān) / 隔三差五 (Gésānchàwǔ)
japanese: 一日（いちにち）おきに／二日（ふつか）に一回（いっかい）
vietnamese: Cách ngày / Hai ngày một lần

chinese: 打小报告 (Dǎ xiǎobàogào) / 告发 (Gàofā)
vietnamese: Mách lẻo / Đi méc
example:
A: Did you see him take that extra stapler home?
B: Yeah, but I'm not going to tell on him.
A: Why not? It's technically stealing.
B: It's just a stapler, don't be a snitch.
A: I guess you're right. Not my business.
B: Exactly. Let's just focus on our own work.
japanese: 言（い）いつける／チクる
spanish: Chivarse / Acusar

chinese: 做过头了 (Zuò guòtóu le) / 操劳过度 (Cāoláoguòdù)
japanese: やり過（す）ぎる／無理（むり）をする
example:
A: I stayed at the office until 2 AM last night.
B: Don't overdo it, man. You'll get sick.
A: I just really wanted to finish this project.
B: Health is more important than a deadline.
A: I'll take it easy tonight, I promise.
B: Good. Go home early and get some sleep.
vietnamese: Làm quá lên / Cố quá thành quá cố
spanish: Pasarse / Exagerar

chinese: 跟某人有矛盾 (Gēn mǒurén yǒu máodùn) / 有过节 (Yǒu guòjié)
japanese: 不満（ふまん）がある／文句（もんく）がある
example:
A: Why aren't you talking to Sarah?
B: I have a bit of a beef with her lately.
A: What happened? You guys were so close.
B: She took credit for my idea in the meeting.
A: Wow, I can see why you're upset.
B: Yeah, I'm not in a hurry to forgive her.
vietnamese: Có xích mích với / Hục hặc với
spanish: Tener bronca con / Tener problemas con

japanese: なんとかやっていく／食（く）いつなぐ
chinese: 勉强糊口 (Miǎnqiǎng húkǒu) / 过得去 (Guò de qù)
example:
A: How's the freelance life?
B: It's tough, just getting by.
A: NYC is expensive for that.
B: Yeah, barely making rent some months.
A: Hang in there, it'll pick up.
B: Hoping for a big gig soon.
spanish: Ir tirando / Sobrevivir
vietnamese: Sống qua ngày / Đủ ăn đủ mặc

chinese: 受感动 (Shòu gǎndòng) / 很有感触 (Hěn yǒu gǎnchù)
spanish: Conmovido / Emocionado
example:
A: Everyone in the office chipped in for your gift.
B: Oh my god, I'm so touched! Thank you.
A: We wanted to show how much we appreciate you.
B: This is the nicest thing anyone's ever done.
A: You deserve it for all your hard work.
B: I don't even know what to say. I'm speechless.
japanese: 感動（かんどう）した／ジーンときた
vietnamese: Cảm động / Rưng rưng

example:
A: This steak is so buttery.
B: Right? It just falls apart.
A: Best thing I've ate all year.
B: The texture is unreal.
A: Worth every penny.
B: Totally. I'm dreaming about this later.
vietnamese: Béo ngậy như bơ / Mịn mượt
chinese: 油脂感丰富的 (Yóuzhīgǎn fēngfù de) / 顺滑如丝 (Shùnhuárúsī)
japanese: 濃厚（のうこう）な／口（くち）どけの良（よ）い
spanish: Mantecoso / Suave como la mantequilla

example:
A: Did you enjoy the movie last night?
B: Honestly, I dozed off halfway through.
A: Was it that boring?
B: No, I was just exhausted from work.
A: I noticed you were quiet during the climax.
B: Yeah, I woke up when the credits started.
vietnamese: Thiu thiu ngủ / Ngủ gật
spanish: Quedarse frito / Dar una cabezada
chinese: 打盹儿 (Dǎdǔnr) / 睡着了 (Shuìzháo le)
japanese: うたた寝（ね）する／寝（ね）落（お）ちする
危（あぶ）なかった／間一髪（かんいっぱつ）
chinese: 侥幸脱险 (Jiǎoxìng tuōxiǎn) / 虚惊一场 (Xūjīng yīchǎng)
spanish: Por los pelos / Casi no la cuento
similar: By the skin of one's teeth
example:
A: That yellow cab almost clipped your bike!
B: Phew, that was a really close call.
A: You need to be more careful in Midtown traffic.
B: I didn't see him turning without a signal.
A: Glad you're okay. That could've been bad.
B: My heart is still racing. Let's pull over.
vietnamese: Suýt chết / Tí thì toi

spanish: En otro color / De otro tono
japanese: 色違（いろちが）い
vietnamese: Màu khác không? / Có màu nào khác không?
chinese: 换个颜色 (Huàngè yánsè) / 同款不同色 (Tóngkuǎn bùtóng sè)
example:
A: I love the fit of this shirt.
B: It looks great on you.
A: Do you think they have it in another color?
B: I saw some blue ones on the other rack.
A: Let's go check. I'm not a big fan of yellow.
B: Blue would definitely suit you better.

vietnamese: Hàng lởm (xe) / Đồ phế phẩm
japanese: 欠陥（けっかん）品（ひん）／ポンコツ
spanish: Cacharro / Salió malo
chinese: 废品 (Fèipǐn) / 蹩脚货 (Biéjiǎohuò)
example:
A: My car broke down for the third time this month.
B: Man, I think you bought a total lemon.
A: I shouldn't have trusted that shady dealer.
B: You should try to get your money back.
A: He's not answering any of my calls.
B: That's rough. You might need a lawyer.

A: I can't finish this pizza. It's too huge.
B: Should we ask for a to-go box?
A: Yeah, I'll have it for breakfast tomorrow.
B: Excuse me, can we get a container here?
A: New York portions are always so massive.
B: I'm not complaining! More for later.
spanish: Para llevar / Una cajita
japanese: 持（も）ち帰（かえ）り用（よう）の箱（はこ）
chinese: 打包盒 (Dǎbāohé) / 打包袋 (Dǎbāodài)
vietnamese: Hộp mang về / Hộp gói đồ ăn thừa

japanese: 許（ゆる）さない／受（う）け入（い）れない
chinese: 不买账 (Bù mǎizhàng) / 不接受这一套 (Bù jiēshòu zhè yī tào)
example:
A: He tried to blame the delay on the intern.
B: And what did the boss say?
A: She was not having it. She knew it was his fault.
B: Good for her. He always avoids responsibility.
A: Yeah, she made him stay late to fix it.
B: About time someone stood up to him.
vietnamese: Không đời nào chịu đâu / Đừng hòng
spanish: No tragar con eso / No estar por la labor
Cortar el rollo / Interrumpir
chinese: 打断别人讲话 (Dǎduàn biérén jiǎnghuà) / 拦腰斩断 (Lányāozhǎnduàn)
japanese: 遮（さえぎ）る／割（わ）り込（こ）む
vietnamese: Chen ngang / Cắt mặt
example:
A: I was trying to explain my side of the story.
B: But he just kept cutting me off!
A: That's so rude. Why wouldn't he listen?
B: He thinks he's always the smartest in the room.
A: You should tell him it's disrespectful.
B: I tried, but he cut me off again!

japanese: 世（よ）の中（なか）そんなもんだ／仕方（しかた）ない
chinese: 事实就是这样 (Shìshí jiùshì zhèyàng) / 无可奈何 (Wúkěnàihé)
vietnamese: Cuộc đời là thế mà / Chịu thôi biết sao được
example:
A: I can't believe they increased the subway fare again.
B: That's just the way it is in this city.
A: Everything is getting so expensive lately.
B: You just have to adapt or move out.
A: I guess there's no point in complaining.
B: Exactly. Let's just focus on making more money.
spanish: Es lo que hay / Así son las cosas
primary: That's just the way it is

vietnamese: Nói toẹt ra đi / Nhả chữ ra nhanh lên
spanish: ¡Suéltalo ya! / Desembucha
example:
A: Um, I have something I need to tell you...
B: Just spit it out already! You're making me nervous.
A: I accidentally shrank your favorite sweater.
B: Oh... is that all? I thought it was something bad.
A: Are you mad? I feel terrible about it.
B: It's fine, it was getting old anyway.
chinese: 直说吧 (Zhíshuō ba) / 别吞吞吐吐的 (Bié tūntūntǔtǔ de)
japanese: はっきり言（い）えよ／白状（はくじょう）しろよ

chinese: 去世并火化了 (Qùshì bìng huǒhuà le) / 走了，已经火葬了 (Zǒu le, yǐjīng huǒzàng le)
example:
A: I didn't know your grandpa passed away.
B: Yeah, last month. He was cremated.
A: That must have been hard.
B: It was, but the service was nice.
A: Where are his ashes?
B: We scattered them at his favorite beach.
japanese: 亡（な）くなって火葬（かそう）された
vietnamese: Đã mất và hỏa táng rồi / Qua đời và làm lễ hỏa táng rồi
spanish: Falleció y lo incineraron / Murió y fue cremado

vietnamese: Buồn man mác mùa thu / Tâm trạng tan chậm ngày thu
spanish: Depresión otoñal / Bajón de otoño
example:
A: You seem a bit down lately. Is everything okay?
B: I think I'm just getting the fall blues.
A: Is it the shorter days and the cold?
B: Yeah, I always get like this when summer ends.
A: Maybe we should go for a hike upstate.
B: That sounds nice. The colors might help.
japanese: 秋（あき）の憂鬱（ゆううつ）／センチメンタルになる
chinese: 秋愁 (Qiūchóu) / 悲秋 (Bēiqiū)
坐稳了 (Zuò wěn le) / 别乱动，等一下 (Bié luàndòng, děng yīxià)
vietnamese: Ngồi yên đấy / Chờ ở đó nhé
japanese: じっとしてて／動（うご）かないで
spanish: Espera un momento / No te muevas
example:
A: Is our table ready yet? We've been waiting 20 minutes.
B: Just sit tight, the previous party is just leaving.
A: We're really hungry, can we sit at the bar?
B: It'll just be another minute, I promise.
A: Okay, fine. But please hurry it up.
B: Thank you for your patience. I'll call you soon.

spanish: En paro / Buscando curro
chinese: 空窗期 (Kōngchuāngqī) / 在找工作呢 (Zài zhǎo gōngzuò ne)
example:
A: What have you been up to lately?
B: Actually, I'm between jobs at the moment.
A: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. What happened?
B: My company downsized, but I'm looking for something better.
A: Let me know if you need any referrals.
B: Thanks, I really appreciate the support.
vietnamese: Đang thất nghiệp / Đang tìm việc mới
japanese: 求職（きゅうしょく）中（ちゅう）／今（いま）は働（はたら）いてない

vietnamese: Xát muối vào tim / Cứ nhắc lại nỗi đau đó mãi
chinese: 揭伤疤 (Jiē shāngbā) / 往伤口上撒盐 (Wǎng shāngkǒu shàng sā yán)
spanish: Restregarlo / Echar sal en la herida
japanese: 嫌味（いやみ）を言（い）う／傷口（きずぐち）に塩（しお）を塗（ぬ）る
example:
A: I know I lost the game, you don't have to rub it in.
B: I'm not rubbing it in! I'm just celebrating my win.
A: You've mentioned the score five times already.
B: Okay, okay. I'll stop. You played well too.
A: Thanks. Let's just talk about something else.
B: Deal. Want to grab some burgers?

spanish: Soso / Desabrido
japanese: 味気（あじけ）ない／パンチがない
vietnamese: Nhạt nhẽo / Vị lờ lợ
example:
A: How is the soup? It looks a bit thin.
B: To be honest, it's a little bland.
A: Maybe it needs more salt or some pepper?
B: Yeah, it definitely needs some seasoning.
A: I'll go grab the spice rack for you.
B: Thanks. This place is usually better than this.
chinese: 淡而无味 (Dàn ér wúwèi) / 没滋味 (Méi zīwèi)
全信了 (Quán xìn le) / 照单全收 (Zhàodānquánshōu)
vietnamese: Tin sái cổ / Nuốt trọn từng lời
example:
A: I told them I was a famous actor in Korea.
B: And did they actually believe you?
A: They totally ate it up! They even asked for an autograph.
B: No way! You're such a good liar.
A: I guess I have a convincing face.
B: I'm definitely not letting you borrow money from me.
spanish: Tragárselo todo / Creérselo todo
japanese: 鵜呑（うの）みにする／真（ま）に受（う）ける

example:
A: I can't believe Christmas is almost here.
B: I know, it's just around the corner!
A: Have you finished all your shopping yet?
B: Not even close. I'm so behind this year.
A: We should go to the mall this weekend.
B: Sounds like a plan. I need a lot of gifts.
vietnamese: Ngay kia kìa / Sắp đến rồi
spanish: A la vuelta de la esquina / Ya mismo llega
japanese: すぐそこまで来（き）ている／もうすぐだ
chinese: 近在咫尺 (Jìn zài zhǐchǐ) / 就在眼前 (Jiù zài yǎnqián)

example:
A: Are you excited about the move to Brooklyn?
B: Somewhat. I'm also a bit worried about the commute.
A: It's not that bad if you're near the L train.
B: I hope so. The apartment is much nicer though.
A: That's a huge plus! You'll love the area.
B: Yeah, I'm looking forward to the new cafes.
chinese: 多少有一点 (Duōshǎo yǒu yīdiǎn) / 某种程度上 (Mǒu zhǒng chéngdù shàng)
japanese: ある程度（ていど）は／多少（たしょう）は
vietnamese: Cũng tàm tạm / Một chút nào đó
spanish: Algo / Más o menos

japanese: 当（あ）たり前（まえ）だと思（おも）う／感謝（かんしゃ）を忘（わす）れる
example:
A: My parents do so much for me, I feel bad.
B: It's easy to take them for granted sometimes.
A: I should really show them more appreciation.
B: Why don't you take them out for dinner?
A: That's a great idea. I'll call them tonight.
B: They'll really appreciate the gesture.
chinese: 习以为常 (Xíyǐwéicháng) / 觉得理所当然 (Juéde lǐsuǒdāngrán)
vietnamese: Coi là hiển nhiên / Không biết trân trọng
spanish: Dar por sentado / No valorar

example:
A: Is Mark still planning to move to California?
B: Yeah, he's leaving for good this time.
A: I thought he was just going for a few months.
B: No, he sold his car and everything.
A: Wow, we're really going to miss him.
B: I know. New York won't be the same without him.
japanese: 永久（えいきゅう）に／これを最後（さいご）に
chinese: 彻底 (Chèdǐ) / 永远地 (Yǒngyuǎn de)
vietnamese: Mãi mãi / Vĩnh viễn
spanish: Para siempre / Definitivamente
Tomárselo a pecho / Sentirse aludido
example:
A: He didn't invite me to his birthday party.
B: Don't take it personally, it was a small gathering.
A: But he invited everyone else in the office!
B: Maybe he just forgot? You should ask him.
A: No way, I'm too hurt to talk to him.
B: You're overthinking it. I'm sure it was an accident.
vietnamese: Tự ái / Để bụng
japanese: 自分（じぶん）への当（あ）てつけだと思（おも）う／真（ま）に受（う）けて傷（きず）つく
chinese: 别往心里去 (Bié wǎng xīnlǐ qù) / 别计较 (Bié jìjiào)

japanese: ギリギリまで先延（さきの）ばしにする
primary: Put it off until the last minute
vietnamese: Nước đến chân mới nhảy / Trì hoãn đến phút chót
spanish: Dejarlo para el último momento / Procrastinar hasta el final
chinese: 拖到最后一刻 (Tuō dào zuìhòu yīkè) / 临阵磨枪 (Línzhènmóqiāng)
example:
A: Have you started your tax return yet?
B: No, I always put it off until the last minute.
A: You know the deadline is in two days, right?
B: I work better under pressure, I guess.
A: You're going to be so stressed on Wednesday.
B: I'm already stressed, but I'll get it done.

spanish: Pegajoso / Empalagoso
chinese: 粘人 (Niánrén) / 腻歪 (Nìwai)
vietnamese: Đeo bám như đỉa / Nhõng nhẽo dính người
japanese: べったりする／依存（いぞん）する
example:
A: Why did you break up with your boyfriend?
B: He was getting way too clingy lately.
A: Like, he wanted to be with you 24/7?
B: Exactly. I couldn't even go to the gym alone.
A: That sounds exhausting. You need your space.
B: Yeah, I feel much lighter now that I'm single.

chinese: 毫不在乎 (Háobù zàihu) / 关我屁事 (Guān wǒ pì shì)
example:
A: Did you hear what they're saying about you?
B: Honestly, I couldn't care less.
A: But it's spreading all over the office.
B: People in this city always love to gossip.
A: Aren't you going to defend yourself?
B: No, I have better things to do with my time.
vietnamese: Chẳng thèm quan tâm / Sao cũng được
spanish: Me importa un pimiento / Me da igual
meaning: 하나도 신경 안 써 (완전 관심 없어)
japanese: 全（まった）く興味（きょうみ）がない／どうでもいい

spanish: No lo gafes / No llames al mal tiempo
chinese: 别乌鸦嘴 (Bié wūyāzuǐ) / 别说不吉利的 (Bié shuō bù jílì de)
vietnamese: Đừng quở quang / Nói gở mồm thế
meaning: 말 꺼내서 재수 없게 하지 마
japanese: 不吉（ふきつ）なこと言（い）わないで／フラグ立（た）てないで
example:
A: We're definitely going to win this.
B: Shh! Don't jinx it!
A: What? We're up by ten points.
B: Anything can happen in the last minute.
A: You're too superstitious.
B: Just knock on wood, please.

vietnamese: Bớt mồm đi / Thôi đừng lải nhải nữa
chinese: 别嘚嘚了 (Bié dēde le) / 闭嘴 (Bìzuǐ)
spanish: Cierra el pico / Deja de parlotear
example:
A: And then he said, and then I said...
B: Dude, stop yapping.
A: I'm just telling a story!
B: You've been talking for an hour.
A: Fine, I'll be quiet.
B: Thank you. My ears are bleeding.
japanese: 無駄口（むだぐち）を叩（たた）くな／黙（だま）って

vietnamese: Đạt đủ tiêu chuẩn / Chuẩn không cần chỉnh
japanese: 条件（じょうけん）を全（すべ）て満（み）たす／完璧（かんぺき）だ
chinese: 符合所有要求 (Fúhé suǒyǒu yāoqiú) / 面面俱到 (Miànmiàn jùdào)
example:
A: How's the new apartment?
B: It checks all the boxes.
A: Washer dryer in unit?
B: Yes, and a dishwasher.
A: That's a unicorn in NYC.
B: I know, I felt so lucky.
spanish: Cumple con todo / Lo tiene todo

chinese: 随手记下来 (Suíshǒu jì xiàlái) / 趁还没忘赶紧写下来 (Chèn hái méi wàng gǎnjǐn xiě xiàlái)
vietnamese: Ghi lại ngay kẻo quên / Note lại không lại quên béng mất
spanish: Apúntalo antes de que se me olvide / Anótalo ya
primary: put it down before I forget
japanese: 忘（わす）れないうちに書（か）き留（と）める
example:
A: That's a great idea for the app.
B: Make sure you put it down before you forget.
A: I'll remember it.
B: No you won't, write it in your notes.
A: Okay, grabbing my phone.
B: Good. Million dollar idea right there.

vietnamese: Tỉnh bơ / Dửng dưng
japanese: 無頓着（むとんちゃく）な／平然（へいぜん）とした
spanish: Despreocupado / Como si nada
chinese: 若无其事的 (Ruòwúqíshì de) / 淡定的 (Dàndìng de)
example:
A: How is he so nonchalant about the exam?
B: He probably studied for weeks.
A: He acts like he doesn't care.
B: It's just a front.
A: I'm over here hyperventilating.
B: Try to be more like him, chill.

A: This vacation has been unreal.
B: I don't want to go back to work.
A: Can we just live here?
B: If we win the lottery, maybe.
A: The sunset is perfect.
B: Truly unreal. Adjusting to reality will suck.
chinese: 太不真实了 (Tài bù zhēnshí le) / 简直像做梦一样 (Jiǎnzhí xiàng zuòmèng yīyàng)
japanese: 夢（ゆめ）みたいだった／最高（さいこう）だった
spanish: Ha sido increíble / Parece mentira
vietnamese: Quá ảo diệu / Đỉnh của chóp
Cày đêm / Thức trắng đêm
spanish: Pasar la noche en vela / Quemarse las pestañas
japanese: 徹夜（てつや）する
chinese: 熬通宵 (Áo tōngxiāo) / 开夜车 (Kāiyèchē)
example:
A: You look like a zombie.
B: Pulling all-nighters for finals.
A: You need sleep, bro.
B: No time, too much to study.
A: Coffee isn't a meal replacement.
B: It is this week.

chinese: 心动 (Xīndòng) / 小鹿乱撞 (Xiǎolù luànzhuàng)
japanese: ときめく／胸（むね）キュンする
example:
A: Did you see how he looked at you?
B: It totally made my heart flutter.
A: You guys are crushing hard.
B: I think I really like him.
A: Ask him out then!
B: Maybe next time, I'm too shy.
vietnamese: Làm tim rung rinh / Tim đập thình thịch
spanish: Hacer vibrar el corazón / Acelerar el pulso

example:
A: He liked my story, does he love me?
B: Girl, are you delusional?
A: It's a sign!
B: It's a double tap on a screen.
A: But he never likes stories.
B: Okay, maybe a tiny sign. But chill.
vietnamese: Tôi bị ảo tưởng à? / Tôi đang mơ ngủ à?
spanish: ¿Estoy alucinando? / ¿Me estoy montando una película?
chinese: 我有幻觉吗? (Wǒ yǒu huànjué ma?) / 我在想入非非吗? (Wǒ zài xiǎngrù fēifēi ma?)
japanese: 勘違（かんちが）いかな？／自意識（じいしき）過剰（かじょう）？

spanish: No puedo / Imposible
chinese: 我可办不到 (Wǒ kě bànbùdào) / 实在是不行 (Shízài shì bùxíng)
vietnamese: Sao mà làm được chứ / Không đời nào
japanese: 絶対（ぜったい）無理（むり）／ありえへん
example:
A: Want another shot of tequila?
B: I can't possibly, I have work at 8.
A: Just one more!
B: I'll die tomorrow. Seriously.
A: You're no fun.
B: I'm responsible. Someone has to be.

primary: Are you investing in shares market?
spanish: ¿Inviertes en bolsa? / ¿Juegas a la bolsa?
chinese: 你在炒股吗? (Nǐ zài chǎogǔ ma?) / 玩儿股票吗? (Wánr gǔpiào ma?)
japanese: 株（かぶ）やってる？
vietnamese: Cậu có chơi chứng khoán không? / Có đầu tư chứng khoán không?
example:
A: Are you investing in the market lately?
B: Just some index funds, nothing crazy.
A: Tech stocks are blowing up.
B: Too risky for me.
A: Scared money don't make money.
B: Lost money don't pay rent.

A: The stock market took a dive today.
B: Retail investors are panicking.
A: Volatility is through the roof.
B: Good time to buy the dip?
A: If you have cash to burn.
B: I'll sit this one out.
spanish: La bolsa / El mercado de valores
japanese: 株式（かぶしき）市場（しじょう）
chinese: 股市 (Gǔshì) / 证券市场 (Zhèngquàn shìchǎng)
vietnamese: Thị trường chứng khoán / Sàn giao dịch

similar: pull a rabbit out of a hat
japanese: やってのける／成功（せいこう）させる
chinese: 搞定 (Gǎodìng) / 拿下了 (Náxià le)
example:
A: The presentation is in an hour.
B: Can we pull it off?
A: We have to. No choice.
B: I'll finish the slides, you prep speech.
A: Teamwork makes the dream work.
B: Let's do this.
spanish: Lograrlo / Sacarlo adelante
vietnamese: Làm nên chuyện / Xử đẹp vụ này

japanese: マジで／ガチで（嘘（うそ）じゃない）
vietnamese: Nói thật luôn / Không điêu đâu
spanish: Te lo juro / Va en serio
example:
A: That burger was the best, no cap.
B: Better than Shake Shack?
A: Way better. On god.
B: That's a bold claim.
A: Go try it, you'll see.
B: Alright, I trust your taste.
chinese: 真不是盖的 (Zhēn bùshì gài de) / 真的，没骗你 (Zhēnde, méi piàn nǐ)

spanish: Encaja perfecto / Es justo lo que buscaba
japanese: ぴったりだ／おあつらえ向（む）きだ
example:
A: I need a cheap, reliable car.
B: This Honda fits the bill.
A: It's kinda ugly though.
B: You said cheap and reliable.
A: Fair point.
B: It runs forever, just buy it.
chinese: 挺合适的 (Tǐng héshì de) / 正中下怀 (Zhèngzhòng xiàhuái)
vietnamese: Đúng cái cần tìm / Chuẩn bài

chinese: 我上班呢 (Wǒ shàngbān ne) / 工作时间 (Gōngzuò shíjiān)
example:

B: Can't, I'm on the clock.

B: Doesn't matter, cameras everywhere.
A: Lame.
B: Being employed is nice though.

vietnamese: Đang trong giờ làm / Đang cày đây
japanese: 勤務（きんむ）中（ちゅう）だ／仕事（しごと）中（ちゅう）だ

example:

B: Yeah, major brain fog today.
A: Drink some water.
B: I need an IV drip, honestly.
meaning: 안개가 조금 꼈다 / (비유적으로) 머릿속이 좀 흐릿하다
spanish: Lo tengo borroso / No me acuerdo bien
chinese: 脑子有点糊涂 (Nǎozi yǒudiǎn hútu) / 模模糊糊的 (Mómóhūhū de)
japanese: 記憶（きおく）が曖昧（あいまい）だ／頭（あたま）がぼんやりする
vietnamese: Hơi lơ mơ / Đầu óc mù mịt quá

vietnamese: Tôi thính ngủ lắm / Ngủ không sâu giấc
example:

chinese: 觉轻 (Jiàoqīng) / 睡觉警醒 (Shuìjiào jǐngxǐng)
japanese: 眠（ねむ）りが浅（あさ）い／すぐ目（め）が覚（さ）める
人見知（ひとみし）りする方（ほう）だ／内気（うちき）だ
chinese: 我这人比较腼腆 (Wǒ zhè rén bǐjiào miǎntiǎn) / 我比较害羞 (Wǒ bǐjiào hàixiū)
vietnamese: Tôi hơi nhát / Hay ngại ngùng
example:
A: Why didn't you talk to him?
B: I tend to be shy around new people.
A: He was totally flirting with you.
B: I just froze up.
A: You need a wingman next time.
B: Please, help me out.
spanish: Soy algo tímido / Me da corte

example:
A: Let's take a selfie!
B: No, I'm camera shy.
A: Just one! You look great.
B: I always look awkward.
A: I'll use a filter.
B: Fine, but don't tag me yet.
meaning: 카메라 앞에서 부끄러워하는, 사진 찍히는 걸 어색해하는
spanish: No me gustan las fotos / Me da vergüenza la cámara
vietnamese: Ngại ống kính / Không ăn ảnh lắm
japanese: 写真（しゃしん）嫌（ぎら）い／カメラを避（さ）ける
chinese: 怕镜头 (Pà jìngtóu) / 不上相 (Bù shàngxiàng)
跟新的一样 (Gēn xīn de yīyàng) / 焕然一新 (Huànrán yīxīn)
vietnamese: Như mới luôn / Mới toanh
similar: It's back to its original state.
japanese: 新品（しんぴん）同様（どうよう）だ
spanish: Está como nuevo / Impecable
example:
A: Did you manage to fix that cracked screen?
B: Yeah, look at it. It's as good as new.
A: Wow, I can't even see where the crack was.
B: The repair shop did a solid job. Worth every penny.
A: No cap. It looks like you just bought it.
B: For real. I'm definitely going back there if I break it again.

chinese: 志趣相投 (Zhìqù xiāngtóu) / 有很多共同点 (Yǒu hěnduō gòngtóngdiǎn)
japanese: 彼（かれ）らは似（に）たもの同士（どうし）だ／共通点（きょうつうてん）が多（おお）い
similar: They have a lot of similarities.
They're two peas in a pod.
spanish: Tienen mucho en común / Son tal para cual
example:
A: Have you met my new coworkers? They're hilarious.
B: Yeah, I noticed they have a lot in common. Both are into retro games.
A: Facts. They've been talking about old-school Nintendo all day.
B: It's good they hit it off so quickly.
A: For sure. Makes the office vibe a lot more chill.
B: Definitely. Team chemistry is everything.
primary: They have a lot in common.
vietnamese: Hợp nhau phết / Có nhiều điểm chung lắm
Đúng là phong cách của cậu / Lại là cậu rồi
chinese: 这很像你的风格 (Zhè hěn xiàng nǐ de fēnggé) / 真是你的作风 (Zhēnshì nǐ de zuòfēng)
spanish: Típico de ti / No podías ser otro
example:
A: I decided to volunteer at the animal shelter again this weekend.
B: Haha, that's typical of you. Always trying to help out.
A: I just can't say no to those cute faces, you know?
B: I know. You've always had a big heart.
A: I guess it's just my thing.
B: It's a good thing. Don't ever change.
japanese: いかにも君（きみ）らしいね

chinese: 多多保重 (Duōduō bǎozhòng) / 照顾好你自己 (Zhàogù hǎo nǐ zìjǐ)
vietnamese: Bảo trọng nhé / Giữ gìn sức khỏe nhé
spanish: Cuídate mucho / No te descuides
example:
A: I'm heading out for my trip tomorrow morning.
B: Safe travels! Make sure you look after yourself while you're away.
A: I will. I've got my vitamins and my itinerary all set.
B: Good. Don't push yourself too hard, just enjoy the vibe.
A: Thanks, fam. I'll hit you up when I land.
B: Sounds good. Peace out!
japanese: 自分（じぶん）を大切（たいせつ）にしてね／元気（げんき）でね

chinese: 放手去做吧! (Fàngshǒu qù zuò ba!) / 去干吧! (Qù gàn ba!)
example:
A: I'm thinking about starting my own podcast, but I'm nervous.
B: Honestly? Go for it! You've got so many interesting stories to tell.
A: You really think people would listen?
B: 100%. Your perspective is unique, and that's what matters.
A: Alright, I'm gonna start recording this weekend.
B: That's what I'm talking about! I'll be your first listener.
spanish: ¡Dale! / ¡A por ello!
vietnamese: Chiến đi! / Thử đi sợ gì
japanese: やっちゃえよ！／行（い）け！

spanish: Menuda pieza / Es un personaje
chinese: 真是个奇葩 (Zhēnshì gè qípā) / 真是个极品 (Zhēnshì gè jípǐn)
japanese: 彼（かれ）は一筋縄（ひとすじなわ）ではいかない／厄介（やっかい）な人（ひと）だ
vietnamese: Ca này khó đỡ thật / Hắn ta đúng là quái chiêu
example:
A: Did you hear what the new manager did this morning?
B: Ugh, yeah. Honestly, he's a piece of work. Always micromanaging.
A: It's getting really old, really fast.
B: Facts. I don't know how much longer I can deal with it.
A: Hopefully he chills out once he's settled in.
B: I wouldn't hold my breath. He seems set in his ways.

spanish: No te fallaré / No te decepcionaré
japanese: 期待（きたい）は裏切（うらぎ）らないよ／任（まか）せて
vietnamese: Không làm cậu thất vọng đâu / Yên tâm đi
chinese: 我不会让你失望的 (Wǒ bùhuì ràng nǐ shīvàng de) / 包在我身上 (Bāo zài wǒ shēnshàng)
example:
A: I'm trusting you with this big account. Don't mess it up.
B: I won't let you down, I promise. I've been preparing for this.
A: I know you have. I believe in your skills.
B: Thanks for the opportunity. I'm gonna give it 110%.
A: That's the energy I like to see.
B: I'll have the first draft ready by tomorrow morning.

vietnamese: Gọi cho cậu nát máy luôn / Tìm cậu mãi đấy
chinese: 我一直都在联系你 (Wǒ yīzhí dōu zài liánxì nǐ) / 找你半天了 (Zhǎo nǐ bàntiān le)
japanese: ずっと連絡（れんらく）してたんだけど／探（さが）してたよ
spanish: Te he estado buscando / Llevo rato llamándote
similar: I've been trying to get ahold of you.
I've been blowing up your phone.
I've been trying to connect with you.
I was trying to find you.
example:
A: Yo, I missed like five calls from you! What's up?
B: Man, I've been trying to reach you all day! Where were you?
A: My phone was on silent during the movie. My bad.
B: All good. Anyway, we're all meeting at the pizza spot in ten.
A: Say less. I'm leaving right now.
B: Bet. See you there.
primary: I've been trying to reach you.

chinese: 真是走运 (Zhēnshì zǒuyùn) / 瞎猫碰上死耗子 (Xiāmāo pèngshàng sǐ hàozi)
example:
A: How did you find such a great apartment in this market?
B: Honestly, it was a stroke of luck. I saw the listing five minutes after it went up.
A: That's insane. Most spots are gone in an hour.
B: For real. I signed the lease before anyone else even toured it.
A: You definitely used up all your luck for the year on that one.
B: Facts. But honestly? Totally worth it.
spanish: Fue un golpe de suerte / Fue pura chiripa
primary: It was a stroke of luck.
vietnamese: Ăn may thôi / Chó ngáp phải ruồi
japanese: まぐれだ／運（うん）が良（よ）かっただけ
溜（た）め込（こ）まないで／吐（は）き出（だ）して
chinese: 别憋在心里 (Bié biē zài xīnlǐ) / 把话说出来 (Bǎ huà shuō chūlái)
spanish: No te guardes las cosas / Desahógate
example:
A: You've been really quiet lately. Is everything alright?
B: I'm just stressed, I guess. It's no big deal.
A: Look, don't bottle up your feelings. It's better to talk about it.
B: I just don't wanna bother anyone with my problems.
A: You're not bothering me. That's what friends are for.
B: Thanks. Maybe I do need to vent a little.
vietnamese: Đừng kìm nén nữa / Có gì cứ xả ra đi

chinese: 别不好意思 (Bié bù hǎoyìsi) / 别害羞 (Bié hàixiū)
japanese: 遠慮（えんりょ）しないで
example:
A: There's pizza left?
B: Yeah, don't be shy, take some.
A: I don't want to be greedy.
B: We're gonna throw it out otherwise.
A: In that case, give me two slices.
B: That's the spirit.
spanish: No tengas vergüenza / Sin miedo
vietnamese: Đừng ngại / Cứ tự nhiên

spanish: Te estoy cogiendo cariño / Me estás ganando
vietnamese: Tôi bắt đầu thích cậu rồi đấy / Cậu dần dần lấy lòng được tôi rồi
example:
A: I know I'm a bit much sometimes, but I mean well.
B: Don't worry about it. Honestly? You're growing on me.
A: Oh, really? I thought I was annoying you.
B: You were at first, but now I think you're just hilarious.
A: I'll take that as a win.
B: As you should. Let's get some coffee.
japanese: だんだん好（す）きになってきた／良（よ）さが分（わ）かってきた
chinese: 我越来越喜欢你了 (Wǒ yuèláiyuè xǐhuan nǐ le) / 你挺招人喜欢的 (Nǐ tǐng zhāorén xǐhuan de)
similar: I'm starting to appreciate you more.
I worked late into the night.
I put in extra hours at night.
example:
A: You look exhausted. Did you actually get any sleep?
B: Barely. I burnt the midnight oil to finish that presentation.
A: Was it worth it? Hope the client liked it.
B: They loved it. All that effort really paid off.
A: Good to hear, but you should definitely crash early tonight.
B: 100%. My brain is officially fried.
chinese: 挑灯夜战 (Tiǎodēngyèzhàn) / 熬夜苦干 (Áoyè kǔgàn)
spanish: Me quedé trabajando hasta tarde / Trasnoché trabajando
vietnamese: Chong đèn cày đêm / Thức khuya cày cuốc
japanese: 夜遅（よるおそ）くまで頑張（がんば）った

spanish: Menos de dos minutos / Casi dos minutos
vietnamese: Chưa đầy 2 phút / Suýt soát 2 phút
chinese: 差两分钟 (Chà liǎng fēnzhōng) / 不到两分钟 (Bù dào liǎng fēnzhōng)
example:
A: What was your lap time?
B: Shy of two minutes.
A: That's a new personal best!
B: I wanted to break 1:50 though.
A: You're getting faster.
B: Next time, I got this.
japanese: 2分（ふん）弱（じゃく）／2分（ふん）切（き）るくらい

example:
A: If we launch by Monday, we'll beat all our competitors.
B: That would be the best case scenario. Everyone's working overtime.
A: Do you think we can actually make it happen?
B: It's a tight squeeze, but it's definitely possible.
A: Fingers crossed. This would be huge for the team.
B: For real. Let's just keep this momentum going.
japanese: それが一番（いちばん）いい形（かたち）だね／理想（りそう）的（てき）だね
primary: That would be the best case scenario.
spanish: Sería lo ideal / En el mejor de los casos
vietnamese: Thế thì còn gì bằng / Được vậy thì tuyệt nhất rồi
similar: If everything goes right, that's what happens.
chinese: 那是理想情况 (Nà shì lǐxiǎng qíngkuàng) / 最完美的打算 (Zuì wánměi de dǎsuàn)

vietnamese: Đồng ý cả hai tay hai chân / Chuẩn cơm mẹ nấu
example:
A: New York is honestly the best city for inspiration.
B: I couldn't agree more. The energy here is just different.
A: For real. Everywhere you look, there's someone doing something creative.
B: It's like you can't help but feel motivated.
A: Facts. I never want to live anywhere else.
B: Same. This city is just in my blood.
spanish: Totalmente de acuerdo. / Ni que lo digas.
chinese: 我完全赞同 (Wǒ wánquán zàntóng) / 你说得太对了 (Nǐ shuō de tài duì le)
japanese: 全（まった）く同感（どうかん）です。／激（げき）しく同意（どうい）。

vietnamese: Não cá vàng quá / Nhớ trước quên sau
